title: Apple becomes first $3 trillion company
summary: Apple Inc. became the first company globally to reach a $3 trillion market capitalization on January 3, 2022, showcasing strong investor confidence. The tech giant's shares hit a record $182.88 during mid-day trading before slightly dropping. This milestone follows Apple reaching $2 trillion in August 2020, driven by sustained demand for its products and services like iPhones, MacBooks, Apple TV, and Apple Music.

title: Apple becomes first company to reach $3 trillion market value
summary: Apple Inc. has become the first publicly traded company to reach a $3 trillion market capitalization, with its shares rising 3 percent on Monday to $182.88. This milestone means Apple's value now exceeds the nominal GDPs of six of the world's top 10 economies, including the UK, India, and France. The company's growth has been rapid, jumping from $2 trillion to $3 trillion in just 17 months, driven by increased demand for tech given the pandemic.

title: Apple becomes first company to hit $3 trillion market value, then slips
summary: Apple Inc. briefly touched a $3 trillion stock market valuation on January 3, 2022, becoming the first company to reach this milestone, fueled by investor confidence in its product pipeline and expansion into new markets like automated cars and virtual reality. Although its market capitalization slipped just below $3 trillion by day's end, the achievement highlights the company's strong fundamentals and consumer loyalty for its iPhones, MacBooks, and services. The stock has seen significant growth since the first iPhone launch in 2007, and under CEO Tim Cook, Apple has successfully diversified its revenue streams beyond the iPhone.

title: Apple Just Became the World's First $3 Trillion Company Because of a 4-Word Quote From Steve Jobs 15 Years Ago
summary: Apple briefly became the world's first $3 trillion company, a significant milestone achieved just a year after crossing the $2 trillion mark. This success is attributed to Steve Jobs's 2007 introduction of the iPhone as a "breakthrough internet communications device," fundamentally changing how people interact with the world. The article emphasizes the transformative impact of the iPhone, distinguishing it from incremental innovations.

title: $20 Billion In 2022: Google Pays Apple For Not Launching Its Own Search Engine?
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple and Google, alleging an illegal agreement where Google pays Apple billions annually to remain the default search engine on Apple devices and prevents Apple from developing its own search engine. The lawsuit claims this arrangement violates US antitrust law, stifles competition, and inflates advertising rates. While both companies have been discreet about the exact figures, estimates suggest Google could pay Apple around $20 billion in 2022, accounting for a significant portion of Apple's annual earnings.

title: Lumentum hooks up with Hesai on auto lidar
summary: Lumentum has partnered with Hesai Technology to ramp up production of VCSEL-based lidar sensors for automotive applications, specifically the AT128 hybrid solid-state lidar units. These sensors, using Lumentum's 905 nm vertical emitter arrays, are designed for advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) and aim to significantly improve lidar cost competitiveness and scalability. Both companies anticipate volume shipments of these sensors to begin this year from Hesai's new manufacturing center, which has a planned capacity of over 1 million units.

title: Hyundai Motor beats Honda in US for the first time in its 35 years there
summary: Hyundai Motor Group, including Hyundai and Kia, surpassed Honda Motor Co. in US sales in 2021 for the first time in 35 years, becoming the fifth-largest player in the market. This achievement, alongside Toyota displacing GM as the top US automaker, highlights the impact of effective supply chain management during the chip shortage and strong demand for eco-friendly vehicles. US automakers like Ford and GM are responding by accelerating their EV strategies to reclaim market share.

title: Trump’s social media app to launch in February, App Store listing says
summary: Donald Trump's new social media app, Truth Social, is set to launch on February 21, 2022, according to an Apple App Store listing. The launch comes 13 months after Trump was banned from major social media platforms like Facebook and Twitter. The app will offer features similar to Twitter and is the first of three planned stages for the Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG), which also plans a subscription video service and a podcast network.

title: JVAL Stock Price | JPMorgan U.S. Value Factor ETF
summary: This article provides detailed information about the JPMorgan U.S. Value Factor ETF (JVAL), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NYSE), and comprehensive financial metrics such as dividend yield, expense ratio, and market capitalization. It also offers technical analysis insights and lists the ETF's top holdings, outlining its investment strategy as a value-focused equity fund targeting the U.S. market.

title: BlackBerry smartphones: what impact did they have on the workplace?
summary: BlackBerry discontinued services for its legacy devices, marking the end of an era for a device that significantly impacted the workplace. While BlackBerrys enabled remote work and efficiency, they also blurred work-life boundaries, a challenge still faced today. HR professionals reflect on the devices' dual impact, from increased productivity to the expectation of constant availability.

title: Apple Could Reinvent Money Management, Take on PayPal and Robinhood
summary: Apple has the potential to significantly disrupt the financial technology (fintech) sector by expanding its Apple Card offerings, introducing checking accounts, and developing new money management tools. This strategic move could position Apple as a direct competitor to established players like PayPal and Robinhood. The author suggests Apple is poised for a major impact in this space, with further developments expected in 2022.

title: T-Mobile begins blocking iPhone users from enabling iCloud Private Relay in the US [U]
summary: T-Mobile/Sprint in the US is reportedly blocking iCloud Private Relay for iPhone users on cellular data, following similar actions by some European carriers. While T-Mobile claims this only affects customers with content filtering, many users without such filters are experiencing the block. This move has raised concerns about carriers interfering with system-level privacy features and highlights the power they hold over internet activity.

title: T-Mobile Acquires Octopus Interactive And Says It’s Still Committed To Ad Tech
summary: T-Mobile Marketing Solutions has acquired Octopus Interactive, a rideshare-focused ad tech startup, signalling T-Mobile's continued commitment to the ad tech business. The move is part of T-Mobile's strategy to become a multibillion-dollar player in ad tech through measured, strategic acquisitions rather than large content plays. While financial terms were not disclosed, T-Mobile aims to leverage Octopus Interactive's video and digital-out-of-home capabilities, as well as its first-party data potential, especially with rideshare drivers who are T-Mobile wireless customers.

title: Stellar December gives TSMC record year
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (TSMC) reported record sales for the previous year, driven by strong demand for its advanced 5-nanometer process used in emerging sectors like 5G and high-performance computing. The company's consolidated sales reached NT$1.59 trillion ($57.47 billion USD), an 18.5 percent increase from 2020. TSMC is expected to announce a significant capital expenditure budget for the current year to further expand production capacity and maintain its technological advantage.

title: Two companies show where our UWB future is heading
summary: The article discusses the emerging applications of Ultra-Wideband (UWB) technology, highlighting its use for fine-ranged location and radar sensing. It features two companies, MKSemi and Novelda, as examples of how UWB is being adopted in smartphones, wearables, and other devices. The piece also touches on privacy implications and the future potential of UWB to create more personalized and ambient computing experiences.

title: Best Buy Co. Inc. stock falls Wednesday, underperforms market
summary: Best Buy Co. Inc. (BBY) stock declined by 1.35% on Wednesday, closing at $102.68, while the broader market indices like the S&P 500 and Dow Jones Industrial Average saw gains. This fall ended a two-day winning streak for Best Buy, leaving it significantly below its 52-week high. The stock also underperformed key competitors including Apple and Amazon.com, which rose or had smaller declines, respectively.

title: TSMC to Spend at Least $40 Billion to Address Chip Shortage
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) announced plans to spend between $40 billion and $44 billion in 2022 to expand and upgrade its capacity, addressing the persistent global chip shortage. The company also raised its annual sales growth projections to 15% to 20%, reflecting continued strong demand for semiconductors. This significant investment highlights the expectation that the high demand for chips will continue for several years.

title: Here’s Why the UPRO and TQQQ Stock Prices Fell 50.1% Premarket Despite Calm Markets
summary: The ProShares UltraPro S&P500 (UPRO) and ProShares UltraPro QQQ (TQQQ) ETFs both fell about 50% in premarket trading. This significant drop was due to previously announced forward stock splits by ProShares, intended to make the shares more affordable to a wider range of investors, not due to market instability. The splits ensure the total value of investors' holdings remains unchanged, similar to traditional stock splits seen with companies like Apple and Tesla.

title: A Tinder Revenge Story
summary: This article details the legal battle between Tinder co-founder Sean Rad and media mogul Barry Diller’s company, IAC, over the valuation of Tinder. Rad accused IAC of deliberately undervaluing the company, costing him a significant portion of his stock options. The contentious lawsuit, marked by personal animosity and accusations, ultimately concluded with a $441 million settlement for Rad and his co-plaintiffs, highlighting the emotional and financial stakes involved in the tech industry's ownership disputes.

title: Netflix raises monthly subscription prices in U.S., Canada
summary: Netflix has increased its monthly subscription prices in the U.S. and Canada by $1 to $2, depending on the plan, to fund new programming and maintain competitiveness in the streaming market. The standard U.S. plan now costs $15.49, up from $13.99, while the Canadian standard plan rose to C$16.49 from C$14.99. These increases, the first since October 2020 in these markets, reflect Netflix's pricing power as it faces growing competition from other major streaming services.

title: Apple Vision? Apple Reality? What Apple Could Name Its AR/VR Headset
summary: This article speculates on potential names for Apple's upcoming AR/VR headset, such as "Apple Vision" or "Apple Reality," as the product faces development delays. It also touches on other Apple-related news, including the expansion of CarKey, a key Apple chip engineer's move to Microsoft, and the App Store's developer earnings for 2021.

title: Apple teases Apple Pay coming soon to Argentina and Peru
summary: Apple has confirmed that its payment service, Apple Pay, will soon be available in Argentina and Peru, as indicated on its Latin America webpage. This expansion follows the service's recent launches in Costa Rica and Colombia in 2021, and similar teasers from banks in Peru and Chile suggest a broader rollout in South America. Brazil was the first country in the region to adopt Apple Pay several years ago.

title: Child Labor and the Human Rights Violations Embedded in Producing Technology
summary: This article examines the pervasive issue of child labor in the technology sector's supply chain, particularly concerning cobalt mining for batteries, and the broader human rights violations involved. It critiques the current international legal framework for corporate accountability, highlighting its inadequacies in prosecuting multinational corporations. The author advocates for new international forums and expanded definitions of corporate responsibility to hold both direct violators and corporate benefactors accountable for human rights abuses.

title: Apple Pay desembarca en la Argentina y revoluciona el negocio de billeteras digitales
summary: Apple Pay, el servicio de pago móvil de Apple, se lanzará próximamente en Argentina, sumándose a otros países de América Latina como Brasil, Costa Rica y Colombia donde ya opera. Este servicio utiliza tecnología NFC para pagos seguros en comercios y aplicaciones, funcionando de manera similar a otras billeteras digitales. La llegada de Apple Pay busca transformar el ecosistema de pagos móviles en el país.

title: Fubo Integrates FanView For Multiview Users on Apple TV
summary: FuboTV is integrating its FanView feature for Multiview users on Apple TV, allowing subscribers to access live stats and scores while watching multiple programming streams simultaneously. This enhancement aims to boost viewer engagement, especially among younger audiences interested in sports analysis and betting, by providing a more interactive and personalized viewing experience. The updated Multiview feature is being rolled out to all fuboTV subscribers.

title: Procter & Gamble’s sales jump as consumers brush off rising prices.
summary: Procter & Gamble reported a significant jump in its second-quarter earnings, with revenue increasing 6 percent to $21 billion and earnings up 9 percent to $4.2 billion, driven by higher prices on consumer products. The company plans further price increases on fabric and personal care items in February and April. This financial growth signals that consumers are largely absorbing increased costs despite rising inflation.

title: Rate Hikes Might Bring Quality ETFs Into Focus
summary: As interest rate hike jitters grip markets and previously high-flying growth stocks falter, quality-focused investments like the Invesco S&P 500 Quality ETF (SPHQ) are gaining attention. SPHQ, which holds profitable, cash-rich companies like Apple and Microsoft, is outperforming broader benchmarks. This trend suggests a market shift towards financially sound companies as the Federal Reserve prepares for a rate tightening cycle.

title: Cantaloupe Inc. celebrates 30 years of technology innovation in unattended retail
summary: Cantaloupe Inc., formerly USA Technologies, is celebrating 30 years of innovation in autonomous retail, having pioneered cashless vending and continuously evolving its technology. The company has seen significant growth in active points of sale and revenue, driven by enhancements to its Seed platform with AI, partnerships for cryptocurrency payments, and integrations for mobile wallets. Cantaloupe attributes its success to being at the intersection of autonomous technology, IoT transformation, and integrated digital payments, as well as market research showing the preference for digital payments and growth driven by younger consumers.

title: Netflix tanks again as investors question growth prospects
summary: Netflix shares continued to fall after the company announced a significant slowdown in subscriber growth, missing analyst forecasts and marking its slowest annual growth pace since 2016. Despite these challenges, some Wall Street analysts remain optimistic, viewing the subscriber shortfall as a forecasting issue rather than an existential threat, particularly given Netflix's strong international expansion prospects.

title: SRE: Benefits and Business Impact of the SRE Mindset
summary: This article discusses the benefits and business impact of adopting a Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) mindset. It explains how SRE, originating from Google, offers a concrete model to implement the DevOps philosophy by balancing feature development velocity with operational reliability risks. The SRE model aims to raise operational excellence, foster unified engineering vision, and redefine operations as a value creation center through concepts like SLIs, SLOs, and error budgets.

title: Zoom vs Facetime: Which is right for you?
summary: This article compares Zoom and Facetime, highlighting their differences in purpose, features, device compatibility, and pricing. It concludes that while Facetime is suitable for casual Apple users, Zoom offers more robust features and broader compatibility for professional and demanding users, even in its free version.

title: Trump 'TRUTH Social' developing content moderation practices to ensure 'family-friendly' community
summary: TRUTH Social, the social media platform by Trump Media & Technology Group, is developing stringent content moderation practices to ensure a "family-friendly" online community ahead of its launch. CEO Devin Nunes stated that the platform aims to be a safe place, utilizing AI technology from Hive to moderate content such as nudity, violence, hate speech, and spam. The platform intends to allow open political debate without censorship of differing opinions, contrasting with what they perceive as selective enforcement by other major tech companies.

title: Warren Buffett has nearly caught up with Cathie Wood after the tech-stock guru vastly outperformed him in 2020
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has nearly caught up to Cathie Wood's Ark Innovation ETF in terms of performance since the start of 2020, narrowing the gap from over 200 percentage points to just 5. While Wood's fund soared during the initial pandemic tech boom, it has since fallen due to investors pulling out of growth stocks in anticipation of interest-rate hikes. This shift highlights the differing investment strategies of Buffett's value-oriented approach and Wood's focus on disruptive growth companies.

title: How the ‘world's first’ AI-managed ETF stacks up, almost five years later
summary: This article reviews the performance and evolution of AIEQ, the "world's first" AI-managed ETF, nearly five years after its debut. Despite initially underperforming the S&P 500, AIEQ has grown in assets, team size, and model sophistication, though it has faced recent headwinds. The article details how the fund, run by EquBot, uses tens of thousands of proprietary AI models and IBM Watson to analyze structured and unstructured data, emphasizing its commitment to explainability and transparency.

title: Intel scores major win as court scraps $1.2 bln EU antitrust fine
summary: Intel has successfully overturned a 1.06-billion-euro ($1.2 billion) EU antitrust fine that was imposed 12 years ago for allegedly stifling rival Advanced Micro Devices. Europe's second-highest court annulled the fine, criticizing the European Commission's analysis as incomplete. This ruling is a significant setback for EU antitrust regulators and could impact similar cases involving other major tech companies.

title: Apple CEO Tim Cook: 'Everybody's seeing inflationary pressure'
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook stated that the company is experiencing inflationary pressures, a sentiment he believes is widespread. Despite this, inflation has not negatively impacted Apple's business, which saw rising gross margins in the December quarter, and prices in the U.S. have not been raised in response. Cook expressed hope that some of the increased costs, particularly in logistics, would be transitory and expects supply chain constraints to ease in the coming months.

title: Apple sales and profit top estimates as hit from chip shortages eases
summary: Apple Inc. reported record sales and profits for the holiday quarter, surpassing estimates despite global chip shortages. The company demonstrated better supply-chain management than competitors, leading to a 5% rise in shares after hours. While chip shortages still impacted older models, particularly iPads, Apple forecasted a narrowing shortfall in the current quarter and highlighted strong growth in services and Mac sales.

title: Apple’s $124 Billion Cult of Personality, PLUG-ing Back in & An EV Road Trip
summary: This article discusses Apple's impressive Q4 2021 earnings, highlighting its massive revenue and ability to navigate supply chain issues, despite the author's personal dislike for the company. It also addresses reader questions regarding Plug Power (PLUG) stock's recent performance and the considerations for long EV road trips, emphasizing the current challenges with charging infrastructure compared to gasoline vehicles.

title: Apple Posts Highest-Ever Quarterly Earnings in Sign It Tamed Supply Crisis
summary: Apple Inc. reported record quarterly sales of $123.9 billion, an 11% increase, surpassing Wall Street estimates despite ongoing supply-chain issues. The company's profit also beat projections, and it anticipates double-digit sales growth for the March quarter. This strong performance indicates Apple's success in navigating pandemic-fueled supply constraints and chip shortages.

title: Apple Probably Won’t Buy Peloton Despite Investor Push
summary: Despite investor pressure, Apple is unlikely to acquire Peloton, as suggested by sources close to the matter. The article also touches on Apple's intentions to compete with Square, record revenue, and a recent executive departure. Additionally, Apple is preparing for its first launch event of the year, expected to feature new iPhone SE and iPad Air models.

title: Chanel Pays $63M for Union Square Building in San Francisco
summary: Chanel has purchased a three-story, 18,000-square-foot building in San Francisco's Union Square for $63 million, signaling confidence in the city's struggling retail center. The luxury fashion brand acquired the property from Stockdale Capital Partners, with expectations that it will open a new store there despite recent retail exodus and looting. This move is seen as a sign of potential recovery for Union Square, as other luxury brands are also investing in the area.

title: Peloton announces new heart rate band, helps track noncompetitive Strive metric
summary: Peloton has launched its own heart rate monitor, the Peloton Heart Rate Band, priced at $90 USD, despite the availability of many third-party options. The author expresses surprise at this move given Peloton's recent challenges and suggests the company should focus on better integration with existing smartwatches like the Apple Watch. The new band, while tracking the "Strive Score," offers no unique features not already available with other compatible heart rate monitors.

title: Adobe XD Review
summary: Adobe XD is a powerful and intuitive design software for interactive interface design and prototyping, recognized as an Editors' Choice. It excels due to its seamless integration with other Creative Cloud apps and robust responsive design tools. While Sketch is Mac-only, Adobe XD supports both macOS and Windows, making it a versatile option for UI/UX designers, despite having fewer plug-ins than its competitor.

title: LGD and BOE Competing with SDC for Larger Share of iPhone Business
summary: LG Display (LGD) and BOE are increasing their competition with Samsung Display (SDC) for a larger share of Apple's iPhone OLED panel supply. While Samsung has historically dominated, Apple's strategy to minimize dependency has encouraged LGD and BOE to expand capacity and technology, leading to forecasted shifts in panel supplier percentages for future iPhone models. Chinese display manufacturers like BOE, Visionox, and CSOT are also significantly investing in flexible OLED screens, challenging South Korean dominance and aiming to capture a larger portion of the growing foldable smartphone market.

title: Meta Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2021 Results
summary: Meta Platforms, Inc. reported solid financial results for the fourth quarter and full year 2021, driven by continued growth in user engagement and business use of its services. The company highlighted significant investments in key growth areas such as Reels, commerce, and virtual reality, alongside a new financial reporting structure separating its Family of Apps (FoA) and Reality Labs (RL) segments. Meta also announced its upcoming ticker symbol change to 'META' and provided an outlook for Q1 2022 revenue, anticipating headwinds from increased competition, platform changes, and macroeconomic factors.

title: This Just In: HBO Documentary ICAHN: THE RESTLESS BILLIONAIRE Debuts February 15
summary: HBO documentary "ICAHN: THE RESTLESS BILLIONAIRE," directed by Bruce David Klein, will debut on February 15, exploring the life and career of financier Carl Icahn. The film chronicles his rise from humble beginnings, his significant financial deals and corporate takeovers, and his controversial legacy as both a corporate raider and shareholder activist. It features commentary from family members, journalists, and industry titans, detailing his impact on Wall Street and various companies.

title: Judge dismisses lawsuit over Apple iPhone water resistance claims
summary: A federal judge has dismissed a proposed class-action lawsuit against Apple Inc., which accused the company of making misleading claims about the water resistance of its iPhones. While the judge acknowledged the ads could be misleading, the plaintiffs failed to prove their iPhones were damaged by water in situations Apple claimed they could withstand, and there was no evidence of fraud. Apple had advertised that certain iPhone models could survive specific depths and durations underwater.

title: Apple Makes Progress in India as iPhone Sales Rise 34% to Record
summary: Apple Inc. achieved its strongest quarter for iPhone sales in India, with a 34% increase to 2.3 million units in the fourth quarter. This indicates that Apple is making significant progress in India, which is recognized as the world's fastest-growing smartphone market. Despite Apple's revenue success, China's Xiaomi Corp. and South Korea's Samsung Electronics Co. still led the market in terms of units sold during the same period.

title: Apple, Broadcom win new trial in $1.1 bln Caltech patent case
summary: A U.S. appeals court has invalidated a $1.1 billion jury verdict against Apple and Broadcom in a patent infringement case brought by the California Institute of Technology (Caltech). The court deemed the damages "legally unsupportable" and ordered a new trial on the amount of damages, while upholding the infringement findings for two of Caltech's patents. The case revolves around Caltech's Wi-Fi technology patents used in millions of Apple devices containing Broadcom chips.

title: Meta rivalry with Apple inflamed as Facebook parent company share price plummets
summary: Meta's stock price plummeted by 26%, wiping out approximately $220bn in market value, primarily due to "headwinds" caused by Apple's new iOS privacy changes. Mark Zuckerberg blamed these changes, which restrict advertisers' ability to target ads, estimating a $10bn impact on Meta's business. In contrast, other tech companies like Snap, Amazon, and Pinterest reported strong ad revenue or stock growth, indicating that Apple's privacy updates did not affect all companies equally.

title: Opinion: $1.4 trillion? Big Tech’s pandemic year produces mind-boggling financial results
summary: In 2021, despite supply-chain disruptions, labor shortages, and rising prices, major tech companies including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, and Microsoft achieved staggering financial results. They collectively saw over $320 billion in profit on $1.4 trillion in sales, with profits increasing more than 55% and sales growing 27% from an already record-setting base. This performance highlights Big Tech's resilience and expanding economic influence, making their combined sales comparable to a top-15 global economy.

title: Tim Cook Reaffirms Commitment to Being Carbon Neutral
summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook recently reaffirmed the company's commitment to becoming carbon neutral across its supply chain and products by 2030, a goal initially announced in 2020. This commitment was highlighted during the company's first-quarter earnings announcement, which reported record revenue. Investors interested in ESG-aligned exposure to Apple can consider the SPDR S&P 500 Fossil Fuel Reserves Free ETF (SPYX), which holds AAPL as its top position.

title: Hertz taps former Goldman exec as CFO-to-CEO path continues to widen
summary: Hertz has appointed Stephen M. Scherr, former CFO of Goldman Sachs, as its new CEO, effective February 28, with a compensation package including a $1.5 million base salary. This move highlights a growing trend of CFOs transitioning to CEO roles, which reached a 10-year high last year, as their expanded operational and strategic responsibilities make them attractive CEO candidates. Scherr's extensive banking background is expected to be beneficial for Hertz as it rebuilds after its 2020 bankruptcy and focuses on shared mobility, electrification, and digital customer experience.

title: SoftBank’s sale of Arm to Nvidia collapses, Arm to IPO
summary: The proposed sale of Arm Ltd by SoftBank Group Corp to U.S. chipmaker Nvidia Corp has collapsed due to regulatory hurdles and scrutiny from various international bodies. Instead of the sale, which was valued at up to $80 billion, Arm will now plan for an initial public offering (IPO). The deal faced opposition from U.S., British, EU, and Chinese regulators over concerns about competition, pricing, and innovation in vital chip markets.

title: Nvidia’s Arm deal: Largest-ever chip merger terminated over ‘significant regulatory challenges’
summary: Nvidia's $40 billion acquisition of Arm Limited, which would have been the largest chip merger in history, has been officially terminated due to significant regulatory challenges globally. SoftBank, Arm's parent company, will retain a $1.25 billion breakup fee from Nvidia and plans to take Arm public by March 2023. Analysts suggest the termination is a "plus" for Arm, allowing it to maintain impartiality as an IP purveyor, and while a "setback" for Nvidia, it won't derail its progress in AI.

title: Apple vs. the World
summary: This article highlights Apple's dominant market capitalization compared to the most valuable public companies on other continents as of February 2022. While North America is led by Apple, Asia's strongest contender, Saudi Aramco, reached nearly 70% of Apple's market cap. Other continents' top companies, such as LVMH in Europe and BHP in Oceania, had significantly smaller market capitalizations relative to Apple.

title: Meta Is Now Calling Out Google Over Apple's App Privacy Rules
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. is publicly criticizing Google, alleging it has an unfair advantage under Apple's new privacy policies. While Meta's apps must ask users for tracking consent, Google's search and browser functions do not, leading to a shift in advertiser budgets towards Google. This comes after Meta's revenue took a multibillion-dollar hit due to its inability to gather as much data on iPhone users.

title: Better Buy: Meta Platforms vs. PayPal Holdings
summary: This article compares Meta Platforms and PayPal Holdings after both companies experienced significant stock price drops due to disappointing earnings reports and future guidance. It outlines the reasons behind their respective crashes, including Apple's privacy changes and TikTok competition for Meta, and supply chain issues and increased competition for PayPal. The author ultimately argues that Meta is a more compelling investment due to its diversification, lower valuation, and wider moat, despite current risks for both stocks.

title: Is Qualcomm’s New ‘aptX Lossless’ Technology The Holy Grail of Bluetooth?
summary: Qualcomm has announced 'aptX Lossless,' a new capability within its aptX Adaptive codec, designed to deliver true CD-quality (16-bit, 44.1kHz) lossless audio over Bluetooth. This technology aims to overcome the traditional lossy compression of Bluetooth by optimizing wireless connectivity and audio technologies to scale up to approximately 1Mbps for lossless transfer. This development could be a significant step for audiophiles and addresses the growing consumer demand for higher quality streaming audio, allowing the clear transmission of lossless source content from services like Amazon HD.

title: Xiaomi sharpens focus on high-end smartphones to challenge Apple
summary: Xiaomi founder Lei Jun has announced the company's strategic shift to focus on the high-end smartphone market, aiming to challenge Apple and become China's top high-end brand within three years. This move comes as Xiaomi seeks to overcome intense competition and continue its growth, investing 100 billion yuan (US$15.71 billion) in R&D over the next five years. The company also plans to expand its retail presence in mainland China by rolling out 20,000 more stores over the next three years to achieve its goal of becoming the world's top smartphone vendor by 2024.

title: Apple Just Quietly Introduced a Feature to Your iPhone That Is Going to Upend This $1.7 Trillion Industry
summary: Apple has introduced a new feature called "Tap to Pay on iPhone," allowing merchants to accept contactless payments directly on their iPhones without extra hardware. This move is set to disrupt the $1.7 trillion contactless payment industry, which has been dominated by services requiring separate terminals like Square. Stripe is announced as the first payment platform to integrate this new capability, making iPhones even more powerful tools for small businesses.

title: Sonos IKEA Symfonisk Table Lamp Speaker with WiFi (Gen 2) review
summary: The Sonos IKEA Symfonisk Table Lamp Speaker with WiFi (Gen 2) is a stylish second-generation lamp speaker offering improved audio and a modular design with interchangeable shades. While it delivers solid trebles and mid-range sounds comparable to other Sonos devices, bass performance can suffer at high volumes. Despite being a little pricey and lacking built-in smart assistant functions, its elegant design and multi-room audio capabilities make it a practical choice for Apple users seeking a discrete speaker solution.

title: Color Star Technology Co., Ltd. Announces That Its Color World Metaverse Software Is Coming Soon to Asia
summary: Color Star Technology Co., Ltd. announced that a localized Asian version of its Color World metaverse software, currently only available in English and downloaded by over a million users, will be released this month. This new version will support simplified Chinese, traditional Chinese, Japanese, and Korean, and will feature Asian celebrities, music, and virtual events. The company is continuously updating the app with new features, including dedicated gaming and entertainment zones, and it will be available on both Apple and Android app stores in Asia.

title: Why Trump’s TRUTH Social Platform Will Struggle to Compete With Mainstream Social Media
summary: Experts predict that Donald Trump's TRUTH Social platform will struggle to compete with mainstream social media despite its appeal to conservative users. The platform's inherently ideological focus and lack of broad social features are expected to limit its growth potential, as previous right-wing alternatives have similarly failed to achieve large-scale success. While Trump's personal influence might initially draw users, the long-term viability is questioned due to the platform's niche focus and lack of technological innovation.

title: 3G Network Is Being Phased Out in Favor of 5G — Will Your Devices Be Affected?
summary: Cell service providers are phasing out 3G networks in favor of 5G, impacting older phones and other devices. AT&T, T-Mobile, Verizon, and Sprint have set dates for their 3G shutdowns, with AT&T completing its phase-out by February 2022. Users are encouraged to upgrade their devices to avoid service interruptions, with provisions made by providers like AT&T and Verizon listing compatible devices and those that will be affected.

title: ISS urges Apple shareholders to vote against CEO Cook's bonus
summary: Proxy advisory firm Institutional Shareholder Services (ISS) is urging Apple shareholders to vote against CEO Tim Cook's 2021 remuneration package. ISS cited concerns over the design and magnitude of his equity award, noting that half of it lacks performance criteria. Cook's total compensation for 2021 reached $98.7 million, significantly higher than the previous year, and was 1,447 times that of the average Apple employee.

title: Learning Spatiotemporal Occupancy Grid Maps for Lifelong Navigation in Dynamic Scenes
summary: This article introduces a novel method for generating and predicting Spatiotemporal Occupancy Grid Maps (SOGMs) to embed future dynamic scene information, crucial for lifelong navigation in robots. The process is entirely self-supervised, leveraging previous navigation data and lidar points to create groundtruth SOGMs. A 3D-2D feedforward architecture is designed to predict future SOGM time steps, enabling lifelong learning.

title: Apple defeats copyright lawsuit over racially diverse emoji
summary: A California federal judge dismissed a copyright lawsuit against Apple Inc., ruling they did not infringe on a company's racially diverse emoji designs. The judge stated that the "idea" of diverse emoji cannot be copyrighted, and the designs were not similar enough to constitute infringement. The plaintiff, Cub Club Investment LLC, had claimed Apple copied their "iDiversicons" app after a failed partnership discussion.

title: Inside Pat Gelsinger’s audacious $43.5B plan to revive Intel
summary: This article details Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger's plans to revitalize the company, which has recently faced significant challenges from competitors like Apple and AMD, and struggled with manufacturing delays. Gelsinger, who rejoined Intel after a period away, aims to tackle both technological and cultural issues to restore Intel's preeminence in the chip industry. His experience at Intel during its heyday positions him to address these intricate problems, including a new business model centered on investing $43.5 billion to regain its manufacturing edge.

title: Trump’s Truth Social Is Poised to Join a Crowded Field (Published 2022)
summary: Donald Trump's Truth Social, a right-wing alternative to Twitter, is set to launch next month, joining a crowded field of "alt-tech" social media platforms. Despite promising free speech without censorship, the business model for these platforms has often proven unstable, as many users do not desire an internet without any content moderation. Truth Social's rollout has faced delays and is subject to a regulatory investigation.

title: The Processor Chip Industry Is Another Ugly Capitalist Oligopoly
summary: This article criticizes the semiconductor industry, labeling it as an "ugly capitalist oligopoly" due to the extreme concentration of chip manufacturing among a few companies, primarily Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC). It highlights how this concentration, driven by high capital costs for fabrication plants and state subsidies, leads to supply chain vulnerabilities, inflation, and limited competition. The author argues that even efforts by governments like the US to subsidize domestic chip production won't fundamentally alter the industry's oligopolistic structure or shift its profit-driven nature.

title: Apple Readies New MacBooks and iMacs for Part Three of Overhaul
summary: Apple is preparing for the third phase of its Mac overhaul, with new MacBook Air, MacBook Pro, and iMac models expected in 2022. This article from Mark Gurman highlights Apple's upcoming product launch event, focusing on its Mac plans for the year. It also briefly mentions changes at Peloton and the development of iOS 16.

title: Trump's new social media application 'Truth Social' begins gradual rollout
summary: Donald Trump's "Truth Social" app began a gradual rollout on Sunday, with the aim of being fully operational in the US by the end of March. The platform is intended as an alternative to major social media sites that banned Trump after the January 6, 2021 Capitol assault. Former congressman Devin Nunes, CEO of Trump Media & Technology Group, stated that the app would be available on the Apple App Store, celebrating the return of free speech for those who felt their voices were canceled.

title: Truth Social backed by Trump Media and Technology group launches
summary: Former U.S. President Donald Trump's new social media app, Truth Social, officially launched on Monday, February 21, 2022. The app, backed by the Trump Media and Technology group, positions itself as a competitor to existing platforms like Facebook and Twitter. This launch comes over a year after Trump was suspended from major social media sites following the January 6 Capitol riot.

title: Kraft Heinz taps Google for multiyear transformation push
summary: Kraft Heinz announced a multiyear strategic partnership with Google to accelerate its digital transformation, leveraging Google Cloud's AI and ML to drive food innovations, build a customer data platform, and improve media efficiency. This initiative is part of Kraft Heinz's broader strategy to reinvest in its brands and achieve cost savings by 2024, enabling data-driven decisions as consumer behavior shifts online. The partnership aims to enhance personalization at scale, optimize media activations, and address changing market challenges amid the pandemic.

title: Apple Al Maryah Island opens Friday in the heart of Abu Dhabi
summary: Apple previewed its new retail destination, Apple Al Maryah Island, in Abu Dhabi, set to open on Friday, February 25, 2022. The store is elevated above cascading water, creating a direct connection to the water's edge, and offers unique design elements, including ramps made of Absolute Black granite and a golden carbon fiber roof. It will host special "Today at Apple" sessions inspired by Abu Dhabi's creative culture, provide business advice, and feature a multicultural team speaking over 30 languages.

title: Life360 shares plunge -30% as losses accelerate to fuel growth
summary: Life360 (ASX: 360) experienced a nearly 30% drop in its share price after reporting a larger-than-expected net loss of -US$33.6 million for 2021, despite revenue increasing by 40%. The company attributed accelerating losses to increased operating expenses in research, sales, and administration, aimed at fueling growth. Investors reacted negatively to the growing losses and concerns surfaced regarding Apple's AirTags privacy issues potentially impacting Life360's Tile acquisition.

title: Berkshire Hathaway posts higher profit, extends buybacks
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. reported a significant increase in fourth-quarter profit, driven by improved operating results and investment gains. The company's operating income surged by 45% to $7.29 billion, while net income rose 11% to $39.65 billion, greatly influenced by its holdings, particularly in Apple Inc. Berkshire Hathaway also continued its share buybacks, repurchasing an additional $6.9 billion in stock during the quarter.

title: Warren Buffett Takes ‘Mildly Attractive’ Path With $27 Billion Buybacks
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway repurchased a record $27.1 billion in stock in 2021, a strategy Buffett called "mildly attractive" given the lack of appealing acquisition opportunities due to high valuations. The buybacks helped reduce Berkshire's substantial cash pile. Buffett also highlighted Apple as a key holding and praised its CEO, Tim Cook, while Berkshire reported strong fourth-quarter earnings.

title: Apple Halts Product Sales in Russia After Ukraine Invasion
summary: Apple Inc. has ceased all product sales in Russia and removed RT News and Sputnik News apps from its App Stores outside of Russia, citing deep concern over the invasion of Ukraine. The company also disabled traffic and live-incident features in Ukraine for safety reasons. This decision aligns Apple with other global companies taking action against Russia.

title: Google, Meta face penalties in Russia as deadline passes to open local offices
summary: Google and Meta are among several internet companies facing potential penalties in Russia for failing to establish local offices and comply with a new communications law. Russia's state communications regulator, Roskomnadzor, had set a deadline of the end of February for these companies to open local offices or face restrictions. The move is part of Russia's broader effort to control internet content, with fines previously issued for failing to censor anti-government content, and recent curtailments of access to platforms like Facebook in retaliation for censoring state media.

title: Apple, Netflix, TikTok strike back against Russian state media content
summary: Several major tech and entertainment companies, including Apple, Netflix, and TikTok, are taking action against Russian state media and suspending services in Russia following the invasion of Ukraine. This includes Netflix refusing to carry Russian federal channels, Apple pausing product sales and limiting services, and social media platforms restricting access to and demoting content from outlets like RT and Sputnik due to concerns about propaganda and disinformation. The moves reflect a global condemnation of Russia's actions and a united front from U.S. businesses.

title: Apple to host spring event next week, low-cost 5G iPhone in focus
summary: Apple Inc. announced it will host its annual spring product launch event on March 8, where it is expected to unveil a low-cost 5G iPhone, a new iPad Air, and a high-end Mac Mini. The new iPhone SE model is rumored to feature an improved camera and faster processor, continuing Apple's focus on 5G capabilities. This event follows a period where Apple successfully navigated supply chain issues to achieve record sales.

title: Crypto exchanges won't bar Russians, raising fears of sanctions backdoor
summary: Major cryptocurrency exchanges are refusing to implement a blanket ban on Russian users, despite Western sanctions targeting Moscow and a plea from the Ukrainian government. They argue that a full ban goes against the libertarian ethos of cryptocurrency, though they will block accounts of individuals explicitly targeted by sanctions. This stance raises concerns among anti-money laundering specialists that crypto could serve as a loophole for Russians to circumvent financial isolation.

title: Amid Ukraine fallout, crisis-hardened chipmakers move to adapt
summary: Semiconductor manufacturers are adapting to potential disruptions from the Russia-Ukraine conflict by stockpiling materials, diversifying suppliers, and leveraging previous lessons from geopolitical events like Russia's annexation of Crimea. While Russia itself is a small market for chips, the war's broader impact on electronics consumption and supply chain stability for key materials like neon gas is a concern, though the industry has shown resilience. The conflict highlights the need for strategic reshoring and decoupling from hostile states to build greater industry robustness against future crises.

title: Ukraine war tests whether marketers can address crisis meaningfully
summary: The war in Ukraine is challenging marketers to address crises in a meaningful way, with consumers expecting concrete action from brands rather than just statements or paused marketing. A Gartner survey reveals that an overwhelming majority of consumers want brands to reconsider doing business in Russia and ensure the safety of employees, emphasizing a shift towards corporate governance and supply chain operations. While traditional communication tactics like public statements rank low in consumer preference, marketers must strategically plan for a time when consumer-facing messaging will be more receptive, keeping a close eye on the rapidly evolving situation.

title: Microsoft closes on $16 billion acquisition of Nuance
summary: Microsoft has completed its $16 billion acquisition of speech recognition company Nuance Communications. This deal, Microsoft's second-largest, aims to expand its presence in the healthcare industry through Nuance's medical dictation and transcription tools. The acquisition received clearance from British and European Union antitrust regulators after initial scrutiny.

title: EPAM Shares Continue to Fall on Ukraine Crisis & Russia Exit
summary: EPAM Systems' shares fell significantly due to the escalating Russia-Ukraine crisis and the company's decision to exit Russia. EPAM, with substantial exposure in Eastern Europe, announced it would discontinue services in Russia and offer humanitarian aid to Ukrainian employees, aligning with U.S. sanctions. The conflict is expected to impact the company's revenues and profitability due to its large workforce in the affected regions.

title: Mac Studio - Technical Specifications
summary: This document provides detailed technical specifications for the Apple Mac Studio, outlining two models with either the Apple M4 Max chip or the Apple M3 Ultra chip. It covers key features such as CPU and GPU cores, Neural Engine, memory and storage configurations, display support, connectivity ports, physical dimensions, and environmental considerations. The document also highlights the device's operating system (macOS), built-in accessibility features, and bundled applications.

title: Apple unveils M1 Ultra, the world’s most powerful chip for a personal computer
summary: Apple has announced M1 Ultra, its new chip for personal computers, which is available in the new Mac Studio. The M1 Ultra utilizes Apple's UltraFusion packaging architecture to combine two M1 Max chips, resulting in 114 billion transistors, a 20-core CPU, a 64-core GPU, and a 32-core Neural Engine. This powerful chip offers unprecedented performance and power efficiency for demanding tasks like 3D rendering and video editing, while consuming significantly less energy than high-end PC desktops.

title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split, $10 bln share buyback
summary: Amazon.com Inc. has announced a 20-for-1 stock split and authorized a $10 billion share buyback plan, causing its shares to rise by 7% in extended trading. This marks Amazon's first stock split since 1999, aimed at making shares more accessible to investors and increasing flexibility for employees managing equity. Trading with the new share price will commence on June 6.

title: Peloton now lets you use Apple Watch as a heart rate monitor, no GymKit required
summary: Peloton has introduced a new feature allowing Bike and Tread users to connect their Apple Watch for heart rate monitoring, eliminating the need for GymKit or separate heart rate monitors. This integration enables the use of the Apple Watch's heart rate data to calculate Peloton's Strive Score. Users need to ensure the Peloton app is on both their iPhone and Apple Watch and then follow a setup process involving the Health app to establish the connection.

title: Traction on Demand’s Greg Malpass on the Salesforce offer he couldn’t refuse
summary: Greg Malpass, founder and CEO of Traction on Demand, sold his company to Salesforce after 15 years, despite previously resisting acquisition offers. The deal, which has an undisclosed sale price but is expected to generate US$75 million in revenue for Salesforce in 2023, was an "incredible opportunity" that Malpass felt accountable to his employees to pursue. Traction on Demand, a Salesforce partner with 1,500 global employees, will become part of Salesforce Professional Services, with Malpass focused on a smooth integration before exploring his next steps within the larger company.

title: Amazon announces 20-for-1 stock split
summary: Amazon announced a 20-for-1 stock split, following similar moves by other major tech companies like Alphabet, Apple, and Tesla. The article explains that a stock split divides existing shares into multiple, smaller-priced shares without changing the overall value of an investor's holdings or the company's market capitalization. Stock splits are often done to increase a stock's liquidity and make it more accessible to a wider range of investors, though in an era of fractional shares, some of the practical impact is diminished.

title: Autodesk Inc. stock falls Friday, underperforms market
summary: Autodesk Inc. (ADSK) stock fell 3.74% on Friday, closing at $192.31, underperforming the broader market as the S&P 500 and Dow Jones also declined. This marked the second consecutive day of losses for the company, with its stock trading significantly below its 52-week high. Competitors like Apple, Adobe, and Synopsys also experienced declines, while Autodesk's trading volume exceeded its 50-day average.

title: Metaverse Technology Redefining Digital Experience
summary: The metaverse is emerging as a significant technological advancement, moving beyond gaming to redefine digital experiences across various industries. Companies like Facebook (Meta), Nike, and Microsoft are investing heavily, with projections indicating global metaverse revenue could approach $800 billion by 2024. While offering potential for retail, enterprise solutions, and new markets like virtual real estate and entertainment, the metaverse also faces challenges related to data security, legal frameworks, skilled resources, VR device limitations, and potential negative societal impacts.

title: Netflix stock has now lost all its gains from the pandemic
summary: Netflix's stock has lost all its gains from the pandemic, with shares falling to a 52-week low of around $332 and being more than 50% down from its mid-November high of $700.99. This decline is attributed to consumers returning to out-of-home entertainment, increased competition from other streaming platforms, and underwhelming subscriber growth forecasts, prompting Netflix to rethink its strategy and raise prices. Analyst Michael Pachter suggests that competition makes it harder to retain customers and increases content costs, while competitors are less likely to license content to Netflix now that they have their own platforms.

title: TSMC’s largest customer makes up 26% of revenue
summary: Sales from Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co's (TSMC) largest customer, believed to be Apple Inc., accounted for 26% of its total revenue last year, reaching NT$405.4 billion. Its second-largest customer, suspected to be Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD), contributed 10% of sales. The US market remained TSMC's biggest buyer, while sales to China declined due to US sanctions against Huawei Technologies Co.

title: Best AirPlay speakers in 2022: Apple, Sonos, Bose, and more
summary: This article reviews the best AirPlay-compatible speakers available in 2022, featuring top brands like Apple, Sonos, and Bose. It categorizes speakers by their ideal use cases, such as all-around performance, TV integration, portability, and premium audio, helping users choose the best option for their iPhone, iPad, or Apple TV. Each recommendation includes pricing information and highlights key features like AirPlay 2 support, smart assistant integration, and sound quality.

title: Apple’s Impact Accelerator unlocks new opportunities for US businesses to speed environmental progress
summary: Apple has announced that applications are open for the second class of its Impact Accelerator, a program launched as part of its Racial Equity and Justice Initiative. The accelerator supports Black-, Hispanic/Latinx-, and Indigenous-owned businesses in green technology and clean energy sectors. The first cohort included companies like Rickman Enterprise Group, Diversified Chemical Technologies, Inc., and Argent Associates, which are now exploring partnerships within Apple's supply chain to advance environmental and social justice.

title: Apple Pay has arrived in Argentina: how to pay with the iPhone and what are its advantages?
summary: Apple Pay is now available in Argentina, allowing iPhone and Apple Watch users to make contactless payments at merchants with NFC-enabled POS devices. The service works with Visa and Mastercard from several major banks like Patagonia, BBVA, Galicia, and ICBC, utilizing NFC technology for secure transactions. Apple emphasizes the security and privacy of Apple Pay, as it protects card numbers by assigning unique device account numbers and encrypting data, providing a safer alternative to physical cards and cash.

title: Apple Pay, disponible en Argentina: estos son los bancos y las tarjetas con los que funciona
summary: Apple Pay, the contactless payment system for iPhone and Apple Watch, has launched in Argentina and Peru. Several Argentine banks, including Galicia, BBVA, Brubank, ICBC, Macro, and Patagonia, along with Visa and Mastercard, support the service. Users can configure Apple Pay through the Wallet app and make secure, contactless payments using Face ID, Touch ID, or a passcode.

title: Why Is Berkshire Hathaway (BRK-A, BRK-B) Stock Up Today?
summary: Berkshire Hathaway's Class A stock hit an all-time high, closing above $500,000 for the first time, buoyed by strategic investments in oil companies like Occidental Petroleum and Chevron. The holding company, led by Warren Buffett, also reported strong fourth-quarter earnings with a 45% year-over-year jump in operating earnings. This performance, along with its substantial stake in Apple, has pushed Berkshire's market capitalization above $730 billion, surpassing Meta Platforms.

title: Berkshire Hathaway shares topped $500,000 each—here's why Warren Buffett says he'll never split the stock
summary: Class A shares of Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently closed above $500,000 each for the first time, making it the sixth most valuable company in the U.S. Despite calls for a stock split to lower share prices, Buffett has consistently refused, arguing that the high price attracts sophisticated, long-term investors. He believes a split would lead to a less aligned shareholder base and offers no intrinsic value if not tied to company growth.

title: Apple is rotten
summary: The author criticizes Apple for what he perceives as predatory trademark litigation against small businesses and even a public school district. He argues that despite Apple's immense wealth, the company bullies smaller entities out of using generic terms or similar-looking logos, often forcing them to give up due to prohibitive legal costs. The article highlights several examples of what the author deems Apple's litigious overreach.

title: LG's UltraFine 5K Display to return to take on Apple's Studio Display
summary: LG's 27-inch UltraFine 5K external display, which was presumed discontinued after Apple unveiled its Studio Display, will return to the market next month. The display was only out of stock due to component shortages and high demand, not discontinued. LG expects the UltraFine 5K to be available again on its website and other retailers, offering a more affordable alternative to Apple's Studio Display.

title: U.S. charges former Apple buyer with defrauding more than $10 million from company
summary: U.S. prosecutors have charged Dhirendra Prasad, a former Apple Inc. employee, with defrauding the company out of over $10 million. Prasad, who worked as a buyer in Apple's global service supply chain, allegedly took kickbacks, stole parts using false repair orders, and caused Apple to pay for items it never received. He faces multiple criminal counts, including wire and mail fraud, money laundering, and tax evasion, with his arraignment scheduled for March 24.

title: The BlackBerry veteran who took on Google and won
summary: Patrick Spence, CEO of Sonos, a veteran of BlackBerry (formerly Research In Motion), successfully led Sonos in a patent infringement lawsuit against Google. The U.S. International Trade Commission ruled in favor of Sonos, affirming that Google infringed on five of Sonos's patents related to smart speaker technology. This victory demonstrates that smaller companies can win intellectual property disputes against large tech giants, emboldening Sonos and potentially setting the stage for future actions against other major players like Amazon.

title: Apple, WiLAN settle patent disputes with license agreement
summary: Canadian patent-licensing firm WiLAN Inc. announced a new license agreement with Apple Inc. relating to wireless technology, settling their patent disputes across the U.S., Canada, and Germany. This agreement comes weeks after Apple successfully appealed an $85 million court ruling in WiLAN's favor. The terms of the settlement were not disclosed.

title: Vornado's Vision for the Future of New York's PENN District
summary: Vornado Realty Trust is spearheading the transformation of New York City's PENN District, building on the success of the Moynihan Train Hall with significant public-private investment. The project focuses on creating an amenity-rich, post-pandemic office culture, attracting major tenants like Facebook and Apple, and revitalizing the area with new construction and updated iconic structures. This long-term vision aims to create a vibrant, livable city center and a comprehensive ecosystem for businesses and residents alike.

title: How To Multiply The QQQ’s Dividend Yield By 15
summary: This article criticizes the low dividend yield of Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ) and suggests an alternative, Nuveen Nasdaq 100 Dynamic Overwrite Fund (QQQX), for significantly higher dividend income. The author explains how QQQX uses covered call options to generate a 7.3% dividend, contrasting it with QQQ's 0.5% yield and the less effective Global X Nasdaq 100 Covered Call ETF (QYLD). It highlights QQQX's strategy of cherry-picking profitable tech stocks and its advantage as a closed-end fund often trading at a discount.

title: Apple’s $4.7B in Green Bonds support innovative green technology
summary: Apple's $4.7 billion in Green Bonds have been instrumental in fostering advancements in low-carbon manufacturing and recycling technologies, including a breakthrough in aluminum production that creates oxygen instead of greenhouse gases. The company plans to use this innovative direct carbon-free aluminum from ELYSIS in its iPhone SE. These investments are part of Apple's broader commitment to becoming carbon neutral across its supply chain by 2030 and expanding its use of clean energy globally.

title: Household Appliances Global Market Report 2022
summary: The global household appliances market is projected to grow from $502.28 billion in 2021 to $557.70 billion in 2022, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 11.0%, driven by companies recovering from the COVID-19 impact. The market is expected to reach $818.87 billion by 2026. Key trends include the rising demand for outdoor cooking appliances and increased use of LED lighting in appliances for energy efficiency, while the growth of online on-demand laundry services may restrain the household washing machine market.

title: How big U.S. companies are creating a brand new abortion benefit
summary: Large U.S. companies are developing new benefits to cover travel costs for employees seeking abortions in other states, in response to stricter abortion laws being enacted across the country. Companies like Citigroup, Apple, Bumble, and Hewlett Packard Enterprise are offering such benefits, and some smaller companies like Alloy and Match Group are partnering with third parties to ensure access, privacy, and support for employees. This trend highlights the challenges employers face in navigating evolving legal landscapes, protecting employee privacy, and addressing legal actions, while also revealing disparities in benefit access for different worker groups.

title: Apple terminates Russian Mir card users from paying with Apple Pay
summary: Apple has suspended its Apple Pay service for Russia's Mir card payment system, effectively closing a loophole that allowed some Russians to continue using the service. This follows Apple's earlier restrictions on MasterCard and Visa cardholders and the cessation of all product sales in Russia. Concurrently, Apple is expanding its Apple Pay service into several South American nations, including Argentina and Peru, with Chile expected to follow.

title: Apple to cut iPhone, AirPods output, Nikkei reports
summary: Apple Inc. plans to reduce iPhone SE and AirPods production due to a slowdown in demand caused by the Ukraine crisis and rising inflation, according to a Nikkei report. The company intends to produce 20% fewer iPhone SEs next quarter and has cut 2022 orders for AirPods by over 10 million units. Analysts attribute the weaker demand to COVID-19 lockdowns in China, inflation, and the lack of significant design upgrades for the latest iPhone SE model.

title: Amazon Stock Rally Erases Losses for the Year
summary: Amazon.com Inc. shares rallied, becoming the first megacap tech stock to recover its losses for the year,

title: WA Police phone capabilities expanded to add support for Apple CarPlay
summary: WA Police's phone capabilities are expanding to include Apple CarPlay support for frontline officers. This initiative, part of a $34 million McGowan Government investment, allows officers to access police systems and real-time incident information directly from their car's display, enhancing safety and operational efficiency. WA Police is reportedly the first law enforcement agency globally to adopt Apple CarPlay for policing activities.

title: Intel CEO earned 1,711 times more than average company worker in 2021
summary: Intel CEO Pat Gelsinger earned $178.6 million in 2021, which is 1,711 times more than the average Intel employee's salary and 698% higher than his predecessor's pay. Most of Gelsinger's compensation was in stock awards, including one-time new-hire equity. This disclosure comes as Intel asks shareholders to approve executive compensation at their upcoming annual meeting, while executive pay across the U.S. continues to rise.

title: Warren Buffett's 3 Greatest Investments of All Time
summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, has a remarkable track record for identifying highly profitable investments. This article highlights his three greatest investments: Apple (AAPL) for its massive nominal dollar return, GEICO for its stellar return as an acquisition, and Moody's (MCO) for its impressive percentage gain from an existing investment. These choices underscore Buffett's strategic approach to value creation through both direct stock holdings and company acquisitions.

title: Apple reportedly seeks alternative flash memory chip suppliers
summary: Apple is reportedly looking into adding China's Yangtze Memory Technologies Corp. as a supplier for NAND flash memory chips. This move comes after a supply chain disruption at a facility co-owned by Kioxia Corp. and Western Digital Corp., which are key flash memory suppliers for Apple. The potential partnership with Yangtze Memory aims to diversify Apple's supply chain and reduce reliance on existing suppliers, despite geopolitical considerations and Yangtze Memory's older technology compared to competitors.

title: Why Big Companies Like Tesla and Amazon Are Splitting Stocks
summary: This article explains what a stock split is and why major companies like Tesla, Amazon, and Alphabet execute them. It delves into the financial and psychological reasons behind stock splits, such as making shares more accessible to a wider range of investors and signaling positive future performance, despite the total market capitalization remaining unchanged. The piece also discusses the impact of stock splits on retail investors and highlights the strong post-split performance of such stocks.

title: Putnam’s Collins Draws On Nature For ESG Investing Inspiration
summary: Katherine Collins, head of sustainable investing at Putnam Investments, integrates biomimicry and insights from nature, specifically honeybees, into her ESG investment strategies. After a career in traditional finance and a master's degree from Harvard Divinity School, Collins applies a unique lens to identify companies demonstrating leadership in sustainability. Her approach focuses on balancing risk and opportunity, evidenced in her co-management of Putnam Sustainable Leaders (PNOYX) and Putnam Sustainable Future (PMVYX) funds.

title: BlackBerry plans to settle lawsuit over BlackBerry 10, avoid U.S. trial
summary: BlackBerry Ltd plans to settle an eight-year-old class-action lawsuit alleging that it defrauded shareholders by misrepresenting the success of its BlackBerry 10 smartphones. A federal judge in Manhattan granted a request to adjourn the planned trial to allow for settlement negotiations. The lawsuit claims BlackBerry concealed the device's true sales prospects, which led to an inflated share price before the company ceased phone manufacturing in 2016 to pivot to cybersecurity.

title: Is QQQ A Good Buy For Dividend Portfolios? Why There Are Better Choices
summary: The Invesco QQQ Trust, Series 1 ETF (QQQ) has seen strong capital appreciation in recent years, largely due to factors like ultra-loose monetary policy and pandemic tailwinds. However, these factors are unlikely to persist, making QQQ less suitable for income-focused dividend portfolios. The author suggests investors might find better income opportunities by selecting individual stocks.

title: Warren Buffett made about $650 million on HP after stake was revealed
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway saw its stake in HP increase by about $650 million to $4.85 billion after the company revealed its ownership of 121 million shares. HP's stock surged 15% following the disclosure, marking its best day in over two years. This swift market reaction highlights the continued influence of Buffett's investment decisions on investor behavior.

title: Dreams of green data
summary: This article discusses the efforts by companies like Apple, Google, and Equinix, alongside Santa Clara officials, to make data centers more environmentally friendly. The primary focus is on reducing energy consumption and shifting towards carbon emission-free power sources for these energy-intensive technological backbones. The article highlights the increasing importance of cleaner power sources and less electricity use for data centers.

title: Survey finds 87% of US teenagers own an iPhone, Apple's AirPods remain immensely popular
summary: A Piper Sandler survey reveals that 87% of US teenagers own an iPhone and 72% own AirPods, demonstrating Apple's continued dominance in these product categories among this demographic. The Apple Watch also saw growth, now owned by 37% of teens, and Apple Pay is preferred for contactless payments. However, Apple TV+ lags behind Netflix and YouTube for daily media consumption.

title: Harsh truth: Trump’s social media app follows long line of failed ventures
summary: Donald Trump's social media platform, Truth Social, has experienced significant challenges since its launch, with low engagement, declining downloads, and executive resignations. Despite Trump's high expectations for the app as an alternative to "big tech," it has struggled to gain traction and user interest, with many users stuck on waitlists and Trump himself rarely using the platform. These issues, coupled with investigations into the SPAC that intends to take it public, raise questions about Truth Social's future, especially as Elon Musk's new involvement with Twitter hints at a potential reinstatement of Trump's account there.

title: DeFiChain Adds New dTokens Corresponding to Disney, Intel, MicroStrategy, and iShares MSCI China ETF
summary: DeFiChain has announced the addition of four new decentralized tokens, or dTokens, following a community vote. These new dTokens correspond to Walt Disney Co ($dDIS), iShares MSCI China ETF ($dMCHI), MicroStrategy Incorporated ($dMSTR), and Intel Corporation ($dINTC), allowing users to gain price exposure to these traditional assets without ownership. DeFiChain, a blockchain built on the Bitcoin network, aims to provide decentralized financial applications and services, expanding its dToken universe to offer an alternative to traditional financial brokers.

title: Apple starts manufacturing iPhone 13 in India
summary: Apple Inc has begun manufacturing its iPhone 13 model in India, aiming to reduce its reliance on Chinese supply chains. The production is taking place at Foxconn's plant in Sriperumbudur, Tamil Nadu, marking the fourth iPhone model to be produced locally in India since 2017. This move aligns with Apple's strategy to diversify its production to markets like India, Mexico, and Vietnam.

title: Snapchat Lawsuit Claims Executives Kept Investors In Dark About New Apple Privacy Restrictions
summary: A derivative lawsuit alleges that Snapchat executives, including CEO Evan Spiegel and CFO Derek Andersen, concealed from investors the negative impact Apple's new privacy features would have on Snap's advertising revenue. The lawsuit claims executives downplayed the effects, leading to a 26% drop in Snap's shares after a weaker-than-expected October 2021 earnings report. Plaintiff Vonmarie Thomas is seeking declaratory relief and restitution to Snap Inc. for alleged insider trading, unjust enrichment, and breach of fiduciary duties.

title: After Effects on Apple M1: giving motion designers the speed they need
summary: Adobe After Effects now offers native support for Apple M1, significantly boosting performance for motion designers, making it up to three times faster on M1 Ultra Macs. The latest release also introduces integrated review and approval tools with Frame.io for Creative Cloud and Scene Edit Detection, powered by Adobe Sensei, for more efficient workflow. These updates aim to enhance creative flexibility and speed up project turnaround times for users.

title: Yelp Joins Apple, Citigroup, Other Companies Covering Travel Costs for Abortions
summary: Yelp Inc. announced it would provide financial assistance to employees and their dependents for out-of-state travel costs related to abortion care, expanding their health insurance benefits. This move follows similar actions by companies like Apple and Citigroup as more states introduce restrictive abortion laws. The expansion aims to support employees seeking reproductive healthcare in states where access is more readily available.

title: Rare PFAS factory shutdown in Belgium costs 3M
summary: 3M is experiencing a significant disruption in sales due to the government-ordered temporary closure of its PFAS production plant in Zwijndrecht, Belgium. This closure, which has lasted five months, has particularly impacted the semiconductor, data center, and automotive industries, as the Belgian facility is a major producer of electronics coolants. The company is working to meet new, stricter PFAS emissions rules in Belgium and has pledged over $300 million for environmental remediation related to historical PFAS contamination in the region.

title: This Chip Maker Supplies Tesla and Apple. Its Stock Could Get a Boost as Shortages Continue.
summary: Dutch-Swiss chip maker STMicroelectronics has experienced a boom in business due to increasing global demand and persistent supply constraints. The company supplies chips to major tech firms like Tesla and Apple. Its stock is projected to benefit from the ongoing semiconductor shortages in the market.

title: Why did Buffett buy HP Inc (and should you invest too)?
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently acquired an 11.4% stake in HP Inc., making it Berkshire's second-largest tech position after Apple. This investment comes amidst concerns about inflation and the PC market, yet HP's strong fundamentals, consistent cash flow, strategic expansion into gaming and remote work solutions, and attractive valuation at a low P/E ratio and a 2.6% dividend yield, make it an appealing "boring but dependable cash machine" for value investors. The article questions whether individual investors should follow Buffett, advising personal research and considering pullbacks before investing.

title: Apple helps suppliers rapidly accelerate renewable energy use around the world
summary: Apple announced that its suppliers more than doubled their use of clean power over the last year, with over 10 gigawatts operational out of nearly 16 gigawatts committed. 213 major manufacturing partners have pledged to power all Apple production with renewable electricity across 25 countries, accelerating progress toward Apple’s 2030 carbon neutral goal. Apple is also investing directly in renewable energy projects globally and supporting communities through its Power for Impact program.

title: Apple, others face shipment delays as China COVID curbs squeeze suppliers
summary: Shipments of Apple products, Dell, and Lenovo laptops are facing potential delays due to China's ongoing COVID-19 lockdowns, which have forced suppliers like Pegatron and Quanta Computer to suspend operations. The impact's severity depends on the lockdown duration, with a longer lockdown potentially causing significant shortages for end-users, despite some companies' efforts to reroute production or use alternative plants.

title: TikTok owner ByteDance challenges Tencent, NetEase in China’s music-streaming market
summary: ByteDance, the owner of TikTok and Douyin, has launched a new music-streaming app in China called Qishui Yinyue ("soda music") to compete with market leaders Tencent Music Entertainment Group and NetEase. The app, which is currently in beta, offers over 10 million tracks and uses ByteDance's personalization algorithms to recommend music, similar to its overseas app Resso. This move signifies ByteDance's direct challenge to existing dominant players in the Chinese music-streaming market.

title: The Chips That Rebooted the Mac
summary: Apple successfully revitalized its Mac computer line by designing its own M1 chips, replacing Intel processors. This strategic move, led by Johny Srouji, improved Mac performance, energy efficiency, and profitability, and helped Apple navigate supply-chain disruptions. The in-house chip design capability also positions Apple for future products like cars and extended-reality headsets.

title: Netflix Scores as California Judge Says Broadcom’s Dynamic Resource Provisioning Patent Claim is Abstract under Alice
summary: A U.S. District Judge in California has invalidated a Broadcom patent claim against Netflix, ruling it was an abstract idea under the Alice/Mayo framework. The patent, related to dynamic resource provisioning for job placement, was deemed no different from common task allocation, dealing a setback to Broadcom's patent enforcement campaign against Netflix. This decision is part of ongoing litigation where Broadcom asserts Netflix's streaming services infringe on its server technology patents, affecting Broadcom's traditional set-top box business.

title: Why we may not get a Garmin Vivoactive 5: rumours, leaks, expectations
summary: This article explores the possibility that Garmin may not release a Vivoactive 5, suggesting the company might be phasing out the line in favor of its Venu series due to display similarities and Venu's improved battery life. It also discusses potential design and feature expectations for a hypothetical Vivoactive 5, including enhanced training metrics, new sensors like ECG and thermometers, and cellular connectivity.
