It highlights how consumer generative AI tools can pose dangers for sensitive corporate information and accuracy, leading many companies to restrict their use. The piece concludes by advocating for enterprise-specific generative AI solutions, like C3 Generative AI, that are designed for secure and reliable data handling to mitigate these risks.

title: Production of Apple iPhone 15 reportedly kicks off in India
summary: Apple has reportedly started the production of its upcoming iPhone 15 in India through its main manufacturer, Foxconn, at a facility in Tamil Nadu. This move is part of Apple's strategy to reduce its reliance on China for manufacturing and aims to shorten the lag time between Indian and Chinese assembled iPhones. The iPhone 15 is anticipated to be unveiled in September, with other manufacturers like Pegatron also expected to begin production in India soon.

title: Apple Card named Best Co-Branded Credit Card for Customer Satisfaction with No Annual Fee by J.D. Power
summary: Apple Card, issued by Goldman Sachs, has been recognized for the third consecutive year as the Best Co-Branded Credit Card for Customer Satisfaction with No Annual Fee by J.D. Power. This award highlights Apple Card's commitment to user financial health, including features like Daily Cash rewards and the recently launched Savings account, which has accumulated over $10 billion in deposits. Apple and Goldman Sachs continue to collaborate on expanding the value and benefits offered to Apple Card users.

title: WEBTOON is ready to take on Apple and Amazon (and win)
summary: WEBTOON's CEO, Kim Junkoo, is confident his company can compete with tech giants like Apple and Amazon in the digital comic space, citing their unique creator economy and extensive user data. Despite Amazon and Apple Japan launching their own vertical scroll comic formats, Junkoo believes these companies cannot replicate WEBTOON's developed ecosystem and massive user base of 85.6 million, largely under 30, and nearly a million creators. He suggests that if big tech were serious about the IP-creating business, they would have to acquire WEBTOON.

title: Nine million and counting: The controversial shopping app sweeping Australia
summary: Temu, a Chinese-owned online marketplace, has rapidly become Australia's fastest-growing shopping app, amassing over 9.2 million users since its April launch. Despite its popularity driven by low prices and strong advertising, Temu faces controversy regarding alleged forced labor, counterfeit goods, and a lack of financial transparency from its parent company, PDD Holdings. The article highlights Temu's significant market presence and the ethical questions surrounding its operations.

title: The best ETFs for retirement income
summary: This article explores the best ETFs for retirement income, focusing on conservative asset allocation ETFs and tactical overlays for retirees. It discusses how these ETFs can help reduce volatility, hedge inflation, and generate income, with insights from financial experts on their suitability for different risk tolerances and financial situations. The author also delves into defensive sector ETFs and low-volatility ETFs as additional strategies for Canadian retirees.

title: Arm, the Chip Designer, Files for an I.P.O. Expected to Be Among the Largest (Published 2023)
summary: Arm, the chip design company owned by SoftBank, has filed for an initial public offering (IPO) on the Nasdaq exchange, which is anticipated to be one of the largest in recent years. This move follows Nvidia's abandoned bid to acquire Arm for $40 billion 18 months prior. The IPO will provide SoftBank with additional capital for investments, and SoftBank will retain its position as Arm's controlling shareholder.

title: John Warnock, Inventor of the PDF, Dies at 82
summary: John Warnock, co-founder of Adobe Systems and inventor of the ubiquitous Portable Document Format (PDF), died on August 19 at the age of 82. His innovations in computer graphics, including PostScript and PDF, were instrumental in enabling modern personal computing and visually rich digital experiences. Warnock's persistent goal was to ensure consistent document rendering across different computers and printers.

title: Early Apple computer that helped launch $3T company sells at auction for $223K
summary: One of the first Apple-1 personal computers, signed by co-founder Steve Wozniak, recently sold at auction for over $223,000. This fully operational vintage computer was one of about 200 manufactured in Apple co-founder Steve Jobs' garage in the 1970s, contributing to the launch of the now multi-trillion dollar company. The auction also included the sale of an original handwritten advertisement by Jobs and an early Apple company check signed by both Jobs and Wozniak.

title: 'Not just money and math': Young people are willing to sacrifice returns for ESG
summary: New data reveals that young investors are increasingly willing to sacrifice investment returns to align their portfolios with environmental, social, and governance (ESG) causes they care about. This trend is particularly strong among Gen Z and millennials, with many prepared to underperform market averages for values-aligned investments. Behavioral scientists suggest this reflects a need for young people to signal their values and identity through their investment choices.

title: Corporate America’s Chief Critic, Carl Icahn, Gets His Comeuppance (Published 2023)
summary: Carl Icahn, a notorious activist investor, recently faced a challenge from short seller Nathan Anderson, who questioned the financial stability of Icahn Enterprises, Icahn's publicly traded company. This led to Icahn Enterprises cutting its dividend by half. The situation is seen as an embarrassing turn for Icahn, as he experienced tactics similar to those he has often used against other companies.

title: Hedge fund exposure to 7 biggest tech stocks at record high, Goldman Sachs says
summary: Hedge funds have reached record exposure to the seven largest tech stocks by market capitalization, according to Goldman Sachs data, driven by strong performance and the artificial intelligence theme. These seven stocks, including Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft, now constitute about 20% of the total net market value held by hedge funds tracked by Goldman Sachs. Despite the concentration risk, investment managers rationalize these allocations due to the significant returns generated by these companies this year.

title: Apellis to lay off 25% of staff, trim research in major restructuring
summary: Apellis Pharmaceuticals announced a major restructuring, including a 25% workforce reduction (about 225 employees) and trimmed research spending, to focus resources on its newly approved vision loss drug, Syfovre. This move is expected to save up to $300 million through 2024, helping to extend its operational runway. The restructuring follows a sudden stock price decline spurred by reports of rare but potentially blinding side effects associated with Syfovre, though the company suggests these issues might be unrelated to the drug itself.

title: Apple partner Globalstar taps former Qualcomm executive Paul Jacobs as CEO
summary: Globalstar, the satellite firm partnering with Apple for emergency communication services, has appointed former Qualcomm CEO Paul Jacobs as its new chief executive, replacing David Kagan. The announcement led to a nearly 20% surge in Globalstar's shares. Jacobs' appointment also involves a deal between Globalstar and XCOM Labs, a wireless tech startup he co-founded, for exclusive access to certain technologies and personnel.

title: Apple announces next Impact Accelerator class for businesses advancing environmental innovation
summary: Apple has announced its third Impact Accelerator class, a cohort of 12 Black-, Hispanic/Latinx-, and Indigenous-owned businesses focused on environmental solutions and green technology. This program is part of Apple's Racial Equity and Justice Initiative, aiming to help diverse businesses scale their impact and contribute to a greener, more equitable future. The participating companies offer solutions addressing climate change, resource management, and smarter chemistry, and will receive extensive support and mentorship from Apple.

title: The Fishbowl: Lydia Parker, Samsung Ads Europe
summary: This article features an interview with Lydia Parker, Head of International Agencies at Samsung Ads Europe, as part of The Media Leader's "Fishbowl" series. She discusses her career progression, professional experiences, leadership strategies in a hybrid work environment, changes in client conversations due to the booming CTV market, and her favorite ad.

title: Details on InvestigateTV+
summary: InvestigateTV+ launched its new weekday news magazine program on September 11, featuring groundbreaking investigations from Gray’s award-winning team, national reporters, and stories on consumer issues, health, and inspiration. Full episodes are available on their website and dedicated apps (Roku, Amazon Fire, AppleTV), alongside their weekend show focusing on longform investigative journalism. The article also lists recent topics covered by InvestigateTV+.

title: Globalstar taps SpaceX to launch Apple's Emergency SOS satellites
summary: Globalstar has partnered with SpaceX to launch satellites for Apple's Emergency SOS iPhone feature by 2025, with Apple funding 95% of the costs, including the $64 million launch fee. This agreement is notable given an ongoing regulatory dispute where Globalstar accuses SpaceX of attempting to secure radio spectrum vital for its own satellite services. The Emergency SOS feature, exclusively reliant on Globalstar's network for iPhone 14 and newer models, allows users to connect to satellites for emergency help without cellular or Wi-Fi.

title: An Apple-leased building in Sunnyvale sold for $41M
summary: A Miami real estate firm, BentallGreenOak, purchased an Apple-leased office-and-research building in Sunnyvale for $41 million. This transaction highlights ongoing commercial real estate activity in Silicon Valley. The article briefly mentions the high price paid for the property.

title: Claim Construction – We Agree . . . Or Do We? – One-E-Way, Inc. v. Apple Inc.
summary: This article discusses the Federal Circuit's decision in *One-E-Way v. Apple*, highlighting a dispute over a previously agreed-upon claim construction for "unique user code." The case illustrates that even agreed-upon claim constructions can lead to significant disagreements regarding their application to accused products, with Apple successfully arguing that the code must be associated with a user, not just a device. The ruling serves as a cautionary tale for litigants to ensure proposed claim constructions provide absolute clarity and are well-supported by intrinsic evidence.

title: Qualcomm CEO says AI may breathe new life into smartphones: 'It could create a new upgrade cycle'
summary: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon believes artificial intelligence could spark a new upgrade cycle for smartphones, revitalizing a market that saw a significant sales decline in 2022. Amon indicated that the company's upcoming Snapdragon Summit in October would showcase new AI-driven use cases from smartphone manufacturers. Unlike Nvidia's focus on data centers, Qualcomm aims to bring AI capabilities directly to devices, enhancing user experience and driving demand for new models.

title: India's policy contradictions are making it harder for investors to crack its market
summary: India's apparent policy contradictions, such as import restrictions on electronics despite efforts to attract foreign investment, are complicating the market for global investors. While Prime Minister Modi's "Make in India" initiative and production-linked incentives aim to boost domestic manufacturing and attract companies like Apple and Foxconn, protectionist measures and capital controls create uncertainty. These challenges exist even as India seeks to enhance its global reputation and capitalize on shifting geopolitical alliances and supply chain diversification away from China, potentially limiting its full economic growth and investment appeal.

title: Nvidia Says It Will Invest In Arm’s IPO After Failed Acquisition
summary: Nvidia has confirmed its intention to invest in Arm's initial public offering, despite its failed acquisition attempt of the company. The GPU giant was named as a potential "cornerstone investor" in Arm's updated IPO filing, alongside other tech companies like Intel, AMD, Apple, and Google. This investment highlights the continued importance of Arm's architecture to Nvidia's products and the broader tech ecosystem.

title: Everything Apple Plans to Show on Sept. 12: iPhone 15, Watches, AirPods
summary: Apple Inc. is set to unveil its latest products on September 12, including the iPhone 15 line, new smartwatches, and the latest AirPods. The event is expected to highlight the titanium iPhone 15 Pro, camera improvements, a switch to USB-C chargers, and faster smartwatches. This launch represents Apple's most significant new product unveiling of the year.

title: Self-care products set to grow big; no downtrading in healthcare, says Haleon CEO
summary: Haleon CEO Brian McNamara states that the self-care products market is poised for significant growth, driven by an increased focus on preventive healthcare post-pandemic and consumers' unwillingness to compromise on health. He notes that governments worldwide are also promoting self-care to alleviate pressure on healthcare systems. India is identified as a crucial growth market for Haleon, where the company sees little "downtrading" in healthcare products, including brands like Sensodyne.

title: History of Apple in Photos, From Steve Jobs Era to $3 Trillion
summary: This article chronicles the history of Apple, from its founding by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak to its recent achievement of a $3 trillion market cap under Tim Cook. It details key product launches like the Apple II, Macintosh, iPod, and iPhone, as well as leadership changes and challenges such as the recent DOJ lawsuit and declining iPhone sales in China. The article also touches upon Apple's expansion into services and new product categories like the Apple Watch and Vision Pro, alongside its pivot towards generative AI.

title: Qualcomm will make Apple’s iPhone modems until 2026, and its stock surges
summary: Qualcomm Inc. will continue to supply modem chips for Apple Inc.’s iPhones until at least 2026. This extended partnership suggests Apple's efforts to develop its own 5G modem chips are progressing slower than anticipated. Following the announcement, Qualcomm's stock (QCOM) experienced a significant surge of approximately 4%.

title: Qualcomm to supply Apple Inc with 5G chips until 2026 under new deal
summary: Qualcomm has signed a new deal with Apple to supply 5G chips for iPhones until at least 2026, extending their previous agreement which was set to expire this year. While the financial terms are similar to the prior deal, Qualcomm's financial projections assume that only 20% of iPhones will use its chips by 2026, as Apple continues to develop its own modem technology. A patent licensing deal between the companies, signed in 2019, remains in place and could be extended beyond its 2025 expiration.

title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms competitors despite losses on the day
summary: Shares of Apple Inc. (AAPL) fell by 1.71% to $176.30 on Tuesday, despite outperforming competitors on a day that saw general market losses with the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average also declining. This drop ended Apple's two-day winning streak.

title: Apple unveils its first carbon neutral products
summary: Apple has announced its first carbon neutral products, including models of the new Apple Watch lineup, as a significant step towards its Apple 2030 goal to make every product carbon neutral. This achievement results from innovations in design, substantial reductions in emissions, and advancements in clean energy use throughout its supply chain. The company has also eliminated leather from its product lines, introduced fiber-based packaging, and launched Grid Forecast in the Home app to help users optimize electricity consumption with cleaner energy sources.

title: Netgear's Nighthawk MK93S Mesh Router Arrives As iPhone 15 Readies A Big Wi-Fi 6E Push
summary: Netgear has launched its Nighthawk MK93S, a tri-band Wi-Fi 6E mesh router system, timed with the release of the iPhone 15 series. This system offers speeds up to 50% faster than its predecessor and covers homes up to 7,500 square feet. Netgear anticipates that the iPhone 15's adoption of Wi-Fi 6E will significantly boost the technology's market presence and consumer adoption.

title: Chipmaker Arm is valued at $55 billion after a blockbuster IPO that is this year's biggest by far
summary: Arm Holdings successfully priced its IPO at the top of its range, raising $4.87 billion and achieving a valuation of approximately $54.5 billion. This marks the largest listing of the year globally and is expected to invigorate the equity markets and a pipeline of other tech IPOs. SoftBank will retain about 90% ownership after the offering, which saw significant investment from major tech companies like Intel, Apple, and Nvidia.

title: Arm climbs 25% in Nasdaq debut after pricing IPO at $51 a share
summary: Arm Holdings saw its stock jump nearly 25% on its first day of trading on the Nasdaq after pricing its IPO at $51 per share, valuing the company at almost $60 billion. SoftBank, which took Arm private in 2016, retains about 90% of the company's outstanding shares. Despite its high valuation relative to its current growth, Arm's unique royalty-based business model, including revenue from decades-old designs, and its strategic importance in AI and chip design, are key factors in investor interest.

title: Apple: Streamlining Sustainability For Every Buyer's Perfect Product
summary: Apple is prioritizing sustainability, aiming for all products to be carbon-neutral by 2030, a goal already achieved by the Apple Watch. The company's strategy involves significant emission reductions, increased use of renewable energy, and incorporating recycled materials, ensuring sustainability without compromising product features for consumers. Apple seeks to lead the tech industry towards a more responsible and environmentally conscious future.

title: Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS) Stock Price, Holdings, Dividend Yield
summary: This article provides a detailed overview of the Invesco MSCI USA ETF (PBUS), including its current stock price, key statistics like PE Ratio and Dividend Yield, and its top holdings as of August 31, 2025. The ETF has a portfolio value of $8.44 billion with top holdings including NVIDIA, Microsoft, Apple, Amazon, and Meta Platforms. It also presents historical performance data and breakdowns of asset allocation and sector exposure.

title: Equal Weight ETF EQL Hits Key Buy Signal
summary: The article highlights the Alps Equal Sector Weight ETF (EQL) as an intriguing tool to mitigate concentration risk in the S&P 500, particularly due to the dominance of a few tech firms. EQL, an equal-weight ETF, assigns equal weight to each sector and then to stocks within those sectors, offering diversification and strong performance. The fund hit a key buy signal as its price rose above its 50-day simple moving average.

title: I Can't Stop Thinking About How Awkward Apple's 'Mother Nature' Ad Was, and I Finally Figured Out Why
summary: The author discusses Apple's "Mother Nature" ad featuring Octavia Spencer and Tim Cook, which aired during the iPhone keynote. While initially perceived as cringe-worthy and unfunny, the author concludes that the ad was effective in highlighting Apple's environmental efforts and contrasting them with competitors, even if it wasn't a comedic success. The ad addressed skepticism about Apple's carbon-neutral goals, particularly regarding the use of carbon credits, by having the "Mother Nature" character voice those criticisms.

title: SK Telecom to unveil call recording service for iPhones
summary: SK Telecom plans to introduce a call recording service for iPhone users through its AI app, A dot, coinciding with the iPhone 15 launch in Korea. This will address a long-standing disadvantage for iPhone users in Korea, as Samsung Galaxy phones already offer this feature natively. The company also intends to bring call summary functions to iOS users, which are currently available for Android.

title: Everything we know so far about Project Mugen
summary: Project Mugen, an urban open-world RPG developed by NetEase, has generated significant buzz since its trailer release on August 24th, 2023. The game features an anime-inspired art style, a futuristic setting where humans and the supernatural coexist, team-based combat with unique characters, and multiple traversal options like parkour and wall climbing. While pre-registrations are open, a definitive release date for PlayStation, PC, Android, and iOS platforms has not yet been announced.

title: Why U.S. Bancorp is investing in the static point of sale
summary: U.S. Bancorp's Elavon unit is investing in dedicated payment terminal technology through its talech Terminal system, despite the rise of softPOS solutions that turn smartphones into payment terminals. Elavon believes there's a significant demand for terminals that offer comprehensive business management tasks alongside payment acceptance, minimizing IT complexity and upfront costs for small businesses. While supporting softPOS, Elavon argues that dedicated devices provide greater control over software, offer more value-added services, and better cater to certain merchant needs, including longer battery life and a streamlined user interface.

title: iShares MSCI ACWI ETF
summary: The iShares MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI) offers investors global equity exposure to large and mid-capitalization developed and emerging markets. It tracks the MSCI All Country World Index, providing diversification across numerous countries and sectors efficiently. The ETF is marketed as a low-cost investment vehicle with potential for long-term growth, though it acknowledges inherent investment risks.

title: Expedia Group builds generative AI into multiple products in its “Fall Release”
summary: Expedia Group is unveiling its "Fall Release 2023," introducing numerous features and products for travelers and supplier partners, heavily integrating generative AI. Key updates include AI-powered conversational capabilities within its apps, dynamic travel guides, and "EG Labs" for consumer feedback on new tech. The release also brings influencer content, group trip planning tools, and AI-driven recommendations for hotel partners to enhance performance and guest insights.

title: Calix taps Jabil as part of $42B+ broadband manufacturing plan
summary: Jabil, a St. Petersburg-based tech company, is partnering with California-based Calix Inc. to manufacture parts for expanding broadband internet service in rural America. This collaboration is part of the Biden Administration's $42.45 billion Build America, Buy America (BABA) effort for the Broadband Equity, Access, and Deployment (BEAD) program. Jabil will produce Calix’s optical network terminals (ONTs) and optical line terminals (OLTs) in Michigan, creating over 100 new jobs.

title: T-Mobile CEO Sievert recalls the first iPhone launch on T-Mobile
summary: T-Mobile CEO Mike Sievert reflects on the company's transformation from a struggling "distant fourth" carrier that didn't offer iPhones to a leading provider. The turning point was the introduction of the iPhone 5 in 2013 and the simultaneous launch of its 4G LTE network, coupled with innovative contract-free payment plans. Sievert also credits a $3 billion breakup fee from a failed AT&T merger for helping to fund T-Mobile's growth into the "Un-carrier" it is today.

title: Uniqlo picks Tacoma Mall for its next Puget Sound-area store
summary: Uniqlo plans to open a new store in Tacoma Mall, its latest expansion in the Puget Sound area. The Japanese retailer will occupy the space previously held by White House Black Market, directly across from the Apple Store. This move follows other recent Uniqlo openings and plans in the region, including in downtown Seattle and Bellevue.

title: Buy the Dip in Apple, or Has the Trend Turned Rotten?
summary: This article discusses whether investors should "buy the dip" in Apple (AAPL) given its recent revenue decline for three consecutive quarters and concerns about its position in the AI race. It highlights Direxion's leveraged and inverse single-stock ETFs for AAPL (AAPD and AAPU) and broader technology ETFs (TECL and TECS) for traders with varying risk appetites and market outlooks. The article also provides important disclaimers regarding the risks associated with leveraged and inverse ETFs.

title: Should Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: This article evaluates the Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) as a potential investment, highlighting its focus on large-cap growth stocks, low expense ratio, and strong performance. It details VONG's sector exposure, top holdings, and risk profile, while also suggesting alternative ETFs for consideration.

title: Qualcomm defeats consumers' antitrust claims over chip supply contracts
summary: Qualcomm has successfully defeated a consumer antitrust lawsuit in California federal court, which alleged that the chipmaker's contracts with device manufacturers artificially inflated mobile phone costs. U.S. District Judge Jacqueline Scott Corley ruled in favor of Qualcomm, stating that allowing the plaintiffs to revive past evidence would lead to prolonged and expensive "do-over litigation." This ruling follows Qualcomm's 2020 victory against a similar lawsuit brought by the U.S. Federal Trade Commission.

title: Apple is ordered to face Apple Pay antitrust lawsuit
summary: Apple has been ordered to face a private antitrust lawsuit brought by payment card issuers. The lawsuit alleges that Apple exerts a 100% monopoly over the domestic market for tap-and-pay wallets on its devices, forcing banks to pay excessive fees and hindering competition and innovation. A U.S. District Judge dismissed a "tying" claim but ruled that plaintiffs could proceed with their claim that Apple violated federal antitrust law.

title: Nvidia's French offices raided in cloud-computing competition inquiry, Wall Street Journal reports
summary: French competition authorities raided Nvidia's offices on suspicion of anticompetitive practices related to cloud computing and graphics cards. The investigation, part of a broader inquiry into potential market dominance in cloud computing, follows Nvidia's significant growth partly due to rising demand for its AI chips. This action aligns with France's recent aggressive stance against large tech companies.

title: Dutch regulator rejects Apple’s objections to fines
summary: The Dutch competition watchdog ACM has rejected Apple's objections to 50 million euros ($53 million) in fines. The fines were imposed because Apple failed to fully comply with orders to allow alternative payment methods for dating apps in the Netherlands. Apple plans to appeal the decision to the Netherlands courts, maintaining that the original order degrades investment incentives and compromises user privacy and data security.

title: What the Cheesecake Factory Effect means for malls
summary: A new paper from Moody's Analytics identifies the "cheesecake factor," suggesting that the presence of a Cheesecake Factory restaurant indicates a mall's financial health. Approximately 93% of loans backed by malls with a Cheesecake Factory are current on payments, compared to 72% for malls without one. This phenomenon highlights how specific anchor stores can serve as unofficial gauges of a mall's economic prospects.

title: Scheels opens 250,000-sq.-ft. store in Phoenix
summary: Scheels has opened a new 250,000-sq.-ft. store in Macerich’s Chandler Fashion Center in Phoenix, Arizona, nearly doubling the tenant's previous footprint. This entertainment-based sporting goods store, which features attractions like a Ferris wheel, saltwater aquarium, and wildlife mountain, significantly boosted mall traffic by over 108% on its opening day. The new location is expected to generate four times the revenue of the former tenant, Nordstrom's, and builds on Scheels' strategy of creating destination retail experiences.

title: PayPal Makes Good on Its Apple Pay Card Vow
summary: PayPal has fulfilled its promise to integrate its PayPal and Venmo credit and debit cards with Apple Wallet, nearly a year after the initial vow. This integration allows users to add their cards to Apple Pay for in-app and Safari browser transactions. Former CEO Dan Schulman stated that this move aims to offer more choice to PayPal's merchants and increase branded transactions, citing a similar success with Google Pay in Germany.

title: Blackberry to Split in Two: Why We Like the Idea
summary: BlackBerry announced its intention to split into two distinct businesses: IoT and cybersecurity. Morningstar analysts view this separation positively, especially the IoT business which includes the QNX software for autonomous vehicles, as it is considered the most valuable part of the firm and an attractive investment. The cybersecurity segment, while struggling, is also expected to benefit from better execution as a separate entity and could be an attractive buyout candidate.

title: Tech spared as bond selloff buffets stocks
summary: Treasury yields surged to their highest levels in over a decade as traders anticipated prolonged high borrowing costs from the Federal Reserve, impacting most US stocks. However, the Nasdaq 100, driven by major technology firms like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, saw gains. This market movement occurred despite a last-minute deal to avert a government shutdown, highlighting the market's primary focus on interest rate policy.

title: Moat Investing Rises Above Magnificent Seven Dominance
summary: The article discusses how Morningstar's Moat Index outperformed the S&P 500 year-to-date despite being underweight the "Magnificent Seven" mega-cap stocks. It highlights the growing concentration risk posed by these seven technology giants and explains how equal-weighted Moat strategies offer a solution through diversification and attractive valuations. The article also details the September index reconstitutions for both the Moat and SMID Moat Indexes, noting shifts away from growth stocks and into more value-oriented sectors.

title: Google CEO Pichai wrote to founders Page and Brin to decry bad ‘optics’ of search engine deal with Apple when he was in charge of Chrome
summary: Sundar Pichai, years before becoming Google’s CEO, expressed concerns in 2007 emails to co-founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin regarding the "bad optics" of Google's exclusive search deal with Apple. These emails, used as evidence in a Justice Department antitrust case, revealed Pichai's discomfort with Google being the sole search provider in the Safari browser. The deals, where Google pays Apple and other smartphone makers billions, are central to the government's claim that Google maintains its search dominance through anti-competitive agreements.

title: Qualcomm layoffs in 2023: Why 1,258 jobs are being slashed
summary: Qualcomm is laying off 1,258 employees in California due to a slump in smartphone sales and a prior commitment to cost-cutting. The layoffs, primarily impacting engineering teams in San Diego and Santa Clara, represent 2.5% of its global workforce. The company is trying to diversify into new markets like IoT, VR, and automotive chips, but its revenue continues to be heavily reliant on the struggling smartphone market.

title: United Promotes Benefits of Bluetooth Enabled Planes
summary: United Airlines is highlighting the benefits of its new Bluetooth-enabled planes, emphasizing how these features, including new AirPods Pro capabilities, improve passenger and crew communication. The airline currently has Bluetooth connectivity on over 100 planes and plans to expand this to about 800 aircraft by 2032 as part of its United Next modernization program. This initiative also integrates other Apple products and aims for significant fuel efficiency improvements.

title: Google joins Adobe, Microsoft in promising to protect its AI users from copyright claims
summary: Google has announced it will legally protect users of its generative AI products against copyright infringement claims. This move follows similar commitments from other tech giants like Adobe and Microsoft, aiming to alleviate concerns about intellectual property violations in AI-generated content. The commitment means Google will shoulder legal responsibility for its users in such cases.

title: Amkor's Largest Global Semiconductor Factory Opens in Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park developed by Viglacera
summary: Amkor Technology Vietnam celebrated the grand opening of its largest global semiconductor factory in the Yen Phong 2C Industrial Park, developed by Viglacera Corporation. This advanced factory, with a $1.6 billion investment, will serve leading electronics companies and is expected to boost Vietnam's semiconductor industry. Viglacera, with over 20 years of experience in industrial real estate, continues to attract significant foreign direct investment, especially in high-tech sectors, and plans further expansion of its industrial park network.

title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team identified a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel. This fake app, distributed via a lookalike website, aimed to steal sensitive user data like contacts, call logs, and SMS messages, exploiting the critical need for rocket alert systems. Users who sideloaded the Android version from the malicious site are advised to delete the app and reinstall the legitimate version from the Play Store.

title: Malicious “RedAlert - Rocket Alerts” application targets Israeli phone calls, SMS, and user information
summary: Cloudflare's Cloudforce One Threat Operations Team discovered a malicious Android application impersonating the legitimate "RedAlert - Rocket Alerts" app, widely used in Israel for airstrike alerts. This malicious version, distributed via a deceptive website, collects sensitive user data including call logs, SMS, contacts, and account information, while mimicking the original app's functionality. Users who installed the Android version from `redalerts[.]me` are advised to delete it immediately and reinstall the authentic app from the Google Play Store.

title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is largely controlled by institutional shareholders who own 55% of the company
summary: Institutional shareholders control 55% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), giving them significant influence over the company's share price. The top 25 shareholders own 40% of the company, with The Vanguard Group, Inc. being the largest at 8.3%. Individual investors and public companies hold 39% and 5.9% respectively, while insiders own less than 1%.

title: Weibo trials displaying real names of influencers, starting with its CEO
summary: Weibo is trialling a new policy to display the real names and occupations of influencers, starting with its CEO, Wang Gaofei. The policy initially targets influencers with over 1 million followers and will expand to those with 500,000, affecting fields like politics, finance, and entertainment. This move follows the Cyberspace Administration of China's initiative to regulate "self-media" and has sparked debate regarding its impact on online discourse and user privacy.

title: Apple to spend $1 billion on AI in catch-up move: report
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly investing $1 billion to accelerate its efforts in generative artificial intelligence. This move comes after the company was reportedly "caught off guard" by the rapid rise of AI tools like ChatGPT last year. The investment aims to integrate generative AI technology across all Apple products, with the initiative being led by senior vice presidents John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi.

title: KT Partners with Thailand’s Jasmine Group for Global AI Collaboration
summary: KT announced a collaboration with Thailand’s Jasmine Group to develop a Thai Large Language Model (LLM) using KT's "Mi:dm" super-scale AI, marking the first overseas venture for a domestically produced super-scale AI. The partnership involves sharing technical expertise for LLM development, establishing a GPU farm, and exploring global LLM business models in Southeast Asia. Both companies aim to enhance Thailand's AI industry competitiveness and expand into other regional markets.

title: Nvidia to take on Intel, Qualcomm with Arm-based processors for PC; availability pegged for 2025
summary: Nvidia is developing Arm-based processors for personal computers, aiming to compete with Intel and Qualcomm, with availability expected as soon as 2025. This move intensifies competition in the PC chip market, especially as AMD is also reportedly working on Arm-based processors for PCs. The initiative follows Apple's successful transition to in-house Arm-based chips, boosting Mac sales, and poses a significant challenge to Intel's dominant position.

title: FDA Clears Omnipod 5 App for iPhones
summary: The FDA has granted 510(k) Clearance to the Omnipod 5 app for iPhone, making Insulet Corporation the first to offer a tubeless AID system with full control from compatible Android and iOS smartphones. This clearance follows the Omnipod 5 Automated Insulin Delivery system's initial FDA clearance for patients aged 6 years and older with type 1 diabetes in January 2022, and subsequent approval for children aged 2 years and older. The iOS app will offer the same functionality as the Android version, plus a new custom food feature, with a full market release expected in 2024.

title: ‘Fundamental rethink:’ Investors dump China shares despite new efforts to shore up the economy
summary: Global investors are offloading Chinese shares at an "unprecedented" rate due to concerns over China's economic slowdown, a lack of convincing policy responses, and an opaque business environment. Despite Beijing's efforts to stimulate growth through bond issuance and sovereign wealth fund purchases, these measures have failed to restore investor confidence. Experts suggest that a fundamental rethinking of China investment is underway, driven by a collapse of trust in the Chinese Communist Party and rising US-China tensions.

title: Top 10 Mobile Accessories Companies | Fuelling Market Growth
summary: The global mobile accessories market, valued at USD 295.5 billion in 2022, is projected to reach USD 598.1 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 7.5%. This growth is driven by the increasing adoption of smartphones, technological advancements in mobile devices, and the rising demand for complementary accessories like protective cases, headphones, and smartwatches. The article highlights key players such as Samsung, Apple, Google, Sandisk, Belkin, Harman, Aukey, Sony, Anker, and Bose, providing an overview of their history, products, geographical presence, and recent developments.

title: Apple Looks to Regain Chip Bragging Rights With Trio of M3 Processors
summary: Apple is set to unveil its new M3 chip line, along with updated MacBook Pros and an iMac, at a "Scary Fast"-themed event. This comes as the company also plans a total overhaul of AirPods, increases prices for Apple TV+, and revamps its TV app. The move aims to boost Mac sales and enhance Apple's performance chip offerings.

title: Apple unveils M3, M3 Pro, and M3 Max, the most advanced chips for a personal computer
summary: Apple announced its new M3, M3 Pro, and M3 Max chips, touting them as the most advanced chips for a personal computer. Built on 3-nanometer process technology, these chips introduce a next-generation GPU architecture with features like Dynamic Caching, hardware-accelerated ray tracing, and mesh shading, delivering significant performance improvements over the M1 family of chips. The M3 family also boasts faster CPU performance, an enhanced Neural Engine for AI/ML tasks, and an unrivaled unified memory architecture supporting up to 128GB, along with improved power efficiency for longer battery life and a reduced environmental impact.

title: 3 ETFs Setting the Gold Standard for Investor Returns
summary: This article highlights three top-performing ETFs for investors seeking wealth building in a bullish market: Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG), Invesco QQQ Trust Series 1 (QQQ), and VanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH). These ETFs offer diversification, high liquidity, and lower costs compared to actively managed funds. Each ETF focuses on specific growth sectors like tech and semiconductors, featuring holdings in major companies such as Apple, Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Taiwan Semiconductor.

title: MOAT: History May Not Repeat Itself (BATS:MOAT)
summary: The VanEck Morningstar Wide Moat ETF (MOAT) has a strong historical track record and reasonable fees but is significantly underweight in big tech. Given a bullish view on big tech, the author suggests MOAT may underperform the S&P 500 going forward. The article provides an overview of the ETF's strategy and the author's financial disclosures.

title: DarioHealth launches smart blood sugar meter for iPhone 15
summary: DarioHealth has launched a new smart blood sugar meter specifically designed for Apple iPhone 15 users. This new device leverages the iPhone 15's USB-C charging port for real-time data capture and instant connectivity, enhancing the personalized DarioHealth management experience. The company aims to innovate and continuously engage members in managing chronic conditions like diabetes.

title: Apple now has $162 billion in cash on hand, slightly less than last quarter
summary: Apple's cash reserves decreased slightly to $162.1 billion in its fiscal fourth quarter, down from $166.5 billion in the previous quarter. This comes as the company maintained one of the largest cash piles in the U.S. and posted revenue of $89.50 billion, exceeding Wall Street expectations despite analysts predicting a fourth consecutive quarter of revenue decline.

title: Globalstar's new CEO keeps things vague – for now
summary: Globalstar's new CEO, Paul Jacobs, addressed the company's future plans for the first time since taking the helm approximately two months ago. While optimistic about the combination of space and terrestrial assets, Jacobs provided few concrete details, stating he is still reviewing operations and initiatives, including previously announced private wireless network deals. He expressed hope for an agreement within a year for monetizing spectrum assets and highlighted Globalstar's role in direct-to-cell services and IoT, as the company reported increased revenue and raised full-year guidance.

title: Understanding the Role of Chatbots in Virtual Care Delivery
summary: Chatbots are increasingly vital in virtual healthcare delivery, offering applications from administrative tasks like scheduling appointments to clinical uses such as patient triage and mental health support. While they provide significant benefits in efficiency and patient satisfaction, concerns persist regarding their ability to handle complex diagnostic scenarios, data privacy, and HIPAA compliance, requiring ongoing efforts to build trust among both physicians and patients. The article details chatbot categories, their diverse use cases in virtual care, and weighs their benefits against current limitations.

title: 'We're Changing the Structure:' GM's Software Boss Steps Down Amid Major Overhaul
summary: General Motors is reorganizing its software and services engineering team, leading to the departure of former Apple executive Dave Richardson, who was VP of software and services engineering. Sterling Anderson, GM's chief product officer, will now oversee vehicle software engineering and Global Product. This change aims to accelerate the development and delivery of technology experiences as GM prepares to launch a new centralized computer system in 2028 and moves away from phone mirroring in future vehicles.

title: Cyberattack Hits Infosys' McCamish Systems, Disrupting Operations
summary: Infosys' step-down subsidiary, Infosys McCamish Systems, experienced a cyberattack that caused the non-availability of certain applications and systems. The company is collaborating with a cybersecurity products provider and has launched an independent investigation to determine the impact on systems and data. This incident adds to a growing concern about cybersecurity, following other significant breaches and warnings of state-sponsored attacks earlier this year.

title: On-device AI chips: New battleground for chipmakers
summary: Global chipmakers like Samsung Electronics, Qualcomm, Intel, and AMD are intensifying their focus on on-device AI chips for smartphones, laptops, and PCs, recognizing its potential for customized services, lower costs, and enhanced privacy compared to generative AI. This shift is expected to drive significant growth in AI-enabled consumer electronics and contribute to rising memory chip prices. Both on-device AI and generative AI are seen as complementary, with companies like Samsung planning to integrate both into future smart devices.

title: Starbucks raising wages at least 3% starting Jan. 1
summary: Starbucks announced it will raise wages for its U.S. retail hourly partners by at least 3% starting January 1, 2024, with more tenured employees receiving higher increases up to 5%. This move is part of an expanded benefits package and comes without anticipated menu price increases. The company stated these investments aim to improve the partner experience, reduce turnover, and enhance customer connection scores.

title: Why Warren Buffett has done more to educate investors than any other corporate executive
summary: Warren Buffett is recognized not only for his success as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway and his strategic investments in companies like Apple and Occidental Petroleum, but also for his exceptional candor with investors. The article highlights his significant role in educating investors about the intricacies of businesses and his willingness to discuss both successes and mistakes. This transparency sets him apart, making his biggest legacy potentially his contribution to investor education.

title: Why Warren Buffett has done more to educate investors than any other corporate executive
summary: Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway and a prominent figure on the MarketWatch 50 list, is recognized for his significant influence on investors. He is known for his candid communication, explaining complex business operations, and openly discussing his successes and failures, which has made him an exceptional educator in the investment world.

title: Lionsgate Increases Majority Stake In 3 Arts Entertainment
summary: Lionsgate Entertainment is finalizing a deal to acquire an additional, significant ownership stake in management and production company 3 Arts Entertainment. This move follows Lionsgate's initial majority stake acquisition in 2018 and comes after months of negotiations and a new valuation. The partnership has been fruitful, contributing five recent shows to Lionsgate’s TV series slate, including "Mythic Quest" and "The Serpent Queen."

title: Apple agrees to $25 million settlement with US over hiring of immigrants
summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to pay $25 million to settle claims by the U.S. Department of Justice that it illegally favored immigrant workers over U.S. citizens and green card holders for certain jobs. The Justice Department stated that Apple did not recruit U.S. citizens or permanent residents for jobs eligible for a federal program allowing employers to sponsor immigrant workers for green cards. This settlement marks the largest ever for the Justice Department concerning discrimination based on citizenship.

title: Booking.com settles Italian tax dispute with 94-million euro payment
summary: Travel website Booking.com has agreed to pay approximately 94 million euros ($100.25 million) to settle a tax dispute in Italy covering the period from 2013 to 2021. The settlement follows an investigation by Genoa prosecutors into the company's handling of VAT on properties rented through its platform. As part of the agreement, Booking.com also filed its VAT return in Italy for 2022 and committed to acting as a tax substitute for transactions with private individuals not registered for VAT.

title: Qualcomm and Iridium end their satellite chip partnership
summary: Mobile chip giant Qualcomm has terminated its satellite-to-phone partnership with Iridium, less than a year after its announcement. The collaboration, which aimed to provide satellite-to-phone services via Snapdragon Satellite and Iridium's satellites, struggled due to a lack of adoption by smartphone manufacturers who preferred standards-based solutions. Both companies plan to continue exploring standards-based satellite connectivity options, with Iridium now free to engage directly with OEMs and chipmakers.

title: FAANG Gives Way to the ‘Magnificent Seven’
summary: The "FAANG" stock group has evolved into the "Magnificent Seven," with Netflix replaced by Nvidia, and Microsoft and Tesla added to the mix of top U.S. market names. ETF issuers are beginning to launch products specifically targeting these seven stocks, while existing ETFs like the Vanguard Mega Cap Growth ETF (MGK) already hold significant concentrations of them. Investors have various options, from highly concentrated ETFs to diversified funds, to gain exposure to these influential companies.

title: Market Edges: The Recession (And Fourth Turning) Are Here
summary: Hedgeye argues that a recession is currently underway, contrary to investor hopes for a "Santa Claus rally" and standard seasonal trends. They emphasize a data-driven approach ("conditional factoring") over seasonal optimism, providing extensive resources and analysis of dire economic indicators like household debt, credit delinquencies, and retail sector weakness. The article also highlights insights from Demography analyst Neil Howe on "The Fourth Turning" and global recessionary signals from currency and commodity markets.

title: Spotlight on illegal hiring practices: Apple pays $25M for recruiting, job ad mistakes
summary: Apple Inc. has agreed to pay $25 million to settle allegations of illegal hiring practices related to its PERM program. A DOJ investigation found Apple failed to recruit U.S. citizens or green card holders, did not advertise these positions on its external website, and required paper applications for PERM jobs while allowing electronic applications for others. The settlement includes $18.25 million for discrimination victims and $6.75 million for civil penalties, with Apple also agreeing to revise its recruitment practices and provide employee training.

title: Tepper’s Appaloosa dumps Apple stake and loads up on Amazon’s stock
summary: David Tepper’s Appaloosa hedge fund significantly increased its stake in Amazon.com Inc. and completely divested its position in Apple Inc. during the last quarter. The fund also raised its holdings in Google parent Alphabet Inc. and Facebook parent Meta Platforms Inc., as detailed in a recent SEC filing. This move indicates a shift in Tepper's portfolio allocation towards these tech giants.

title: What's next for satellite communications on Android?
summary: Apple has extended its Emergency SOS via satellite service for iPhone 14 and 15 users to two years, while Qualcomm's Snapdragon Satellite initiative for Android has failed due to lack of OEM integration. This leaves Iridium, a key satellite provider, free to pursue new partnerships directly with smartphone makers. The future of satellite communication on Android is unclear, though Samsung is rumored to introduce a solution with the Galaxy S24, and current options include standalone devices like the Motorola Defy Sat Link and Garmin inReach Mini 2.

title: Warren Buffet's latest buys and sells
summary: This article details Berkshire Hathaway's latest stock buys and sells from its third-quarter 2023 13F filing, highlighting that the company was a net seller despite a tough market quarter, leading to a record cash stake. It identifies three undervalued Warren Buffett stocks within Berkshire Hathaway’s portfolio—Bank of America, American Express, and Kraft Heinz—providing Morningstar's analysis and key metrics for each. The piece also lists the stocks Berkshire Hathaway sold entirely, including Activision Blizzard, Celanese, General Motors, Johnson & Johnson, Mondelez International, Procter & Gamble, and United Parcel Service, while noting a significant reduction in its Chevron holdings.

title: Unity 6 debuts next year with AI-powered innovations and platform updates
summary: Unity announced its upcoming Unity 6 platform, set to launch in 2024, featuring significant AI innovations and platform updates. Key announcements from the Unite conference include the early access of Unity Muse for content creation, a preview of Unity 6, and the introduction of Unity Cloud. These updates aim to address developer feedback and enhance the game development process following recent controversies over pricing changes.

title: Warren Buffett Has Bought Shares of This Stock for 21 Consecutive Quarters -- and It's Not Apple
summary: Warren Buffett has consistently invested in Berkshire Hathaway's own stock for 21 consecutive quarters, authorizing over $72 billion in repurchases since mid-2018. This strategy, enabled by a change in buyback clauses, aims to reward long-term shareholders by increasing their ownership stakes and potentially boosting earnings per share. While Apple constitutes nearly half of Berkshire's invested assets, the frequent repurchase of Berkshire Hathaway shares itself highlights Buffett's conviction in his own company.

title: AT&T’s Black Friday Deals on the iPhone 15 Pro & More
summary: AT&T announced its Black Friday deals for the holiday season, offering significant savings on various Apple products including the iPhone 15 Pro, iPhone 15, iPhone 14, iPhone 14 Pro, iPhone SE 3rd Gen, iPad, and Apple Watch. Deals include trade-in options, no trade-in required offers, and low monthly payments, catering to both new and existing customers. Additionally, AT&T highlighted benefits like flexible shopping, unlimited plans with 5G access, AT&T Fiber deals, and special discounts for military, veterans, teachers, nurses, and physicians.

title: Network of Lies review: Brian Stelter on Fox News, Trump and Dominion
summary: Brian Stelter's book "Network of Lies" examines Fox News's role in spreading election conspiracies following the 2020 election, leading to significant legal and financial repercussions, such as the Dominion settlement. The review highlights Rupert Murdoch's recent step down, the Murdochs' balancing act of profitability and audience satisfaction, and the ongoing influence of Donald Trump on the network and Republican politics. Despite the controversies, the article suggests little change is expected from Fox News as it navigates complex defamation lawsuits and competitive political landscapes.

title: Apple’s In-House Technologies Team Still Has Plenty of Work to Do
summary: This article discusses Apple's intensive efforts to design and manufacture its own core hardware components, such as cameras, screens, modems, and batteries, for its products. It also touches on Apple's adoption of the RCS texting standard, the revelation of its revenue share from the Google search deal, and a key health executive's move to Oura. The piece highlights that Apple's in-house technologies team has extensive work ahead in its strategic initiative to control major parts of its devices.

title: Venmo, Cash App users sue Apple over peer-to-peer payment fees
summary: Venmo and Cash App customers have filed a proposed class-action lawsuit against Apple Inc. alleging the company abused its market power in mobile peer-to-peer payments, leading to "rapidly inflating prices." The lawsuit claims Apple's agreements with PayPal's Venmo and Block's Cash App violate U.S. antitrust law by limiting "feature competition," including prohibiting decentralized cryptocurrency technology. This case adds to Apple's ongoing antitrust legal challenges, seeking an injunction that could force Apple to divest or segregate its Apple Cash business.

title: Should You Buy the Invesco QQQ ETF With the Nasdaq At An All-Time High? History Offers a Clear Answer.
summary: This article discusses the Invesco QQQ ETF and whether it's a good investment despite the Nasdaq Composite being at an all-time high. It analyzes historical data of the Nasdaq's performance after reaching new highs, suggesting that further gains are common. The article concludes that dollar-cost averaging into QQQ is a sensible strategy regardless of market highs, as it tracks the Nasdaq 100 which tends to rise over time.

title: Gaming Industry Charts a Rebound in 2023
summary: The gaming industry is experiencing a significant rebound in 2023 driven by strong console sales, new game releases, and innovations in mobile and cloud gaming. This recovery, following a dip in 2022, is further supported by anticipation of increased M&A activity and strategic moves by major players like Microsoft, Apple, and Netflix, positioning the sector for continued growth into 2024. The article highlights strong U.S. sales, hardware as a primary catalyst, and key factors like M&A and technological advancements shaping the industry's future trajectory.

title: Sonos Readies $400-Plus Headphones to Rival Apple and Bose, TV Set-Top Box
summary: Sonos Inc. is planning a significant expansion into new product categories, including headphones priced over $400 and a TV set-top box. These new offerings are expected to compete with products from Apple, Bose, and Roku, marking a strategic move beyond its traditional smart speakers and sound bars. The headphones could launch as early as April, with the set-top box following by the end of 2024.

title: Department of Justice and Apple Reach $25 Million Landmark Agreement
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced a $25 million agreement with Apple Inc. to resolve allegations of discriminatory hiring practices related to the permanent labor certification (PERM) program. Apple's recruitment practices for PERM positions, which included not advertising on its public-facing career website and requiring mailed paper applications, deterred U.S. workers. The settlement includes $6.75 million in civil penalties and $18.25 million for a backpay fund for victims, and mandates Apple to align PERM recruitment with its standard practices.

title: OpenAI Says Sam Altman to Return as CEO in Dramatic Reversal
summary: Sam Altman is set to return as CEO of OpenAI after being ousted less than a week prior, following intense negotiations and pressure from investors like Microsoft and nearly all of the company's employees. The agreement includes a new initial board of directors led by Bret Taylor, with Larry Summers and Adam D’Angelo, and an internal investigation into the conduct that led to Altman's dismissal. This dramatic reversal comes after widespread calls for Altman's reinstatement and highlights the significant influence of investors and employees on the future of the AI startup.

title: Warren Buffett-Led Berkshire Hathaway Sells Its Entire Procter & Gamble Stake. Should You?
summary: Berkshire Hathaway fully exited its small position in Procter & Gamble, likely due to consolidating its portfolio and P&G's premium valuation compared to other opportunities like Apple. Despite Berkshire's sale, P&G is still considered a worthwhile investment for risk-averse individuals due to its strong brand development, pricing power, and commitment to shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks. The article suggests that while P&G's valuation is high, its other characteristics make it a solid choice for passive income and portfolio stability.

title: Sonos is reportedly working on a streaming video TV set top box, along with headphones
summary: Sonos is reportedly expanding its product line beyond speakers and soundbars. The company isSaid to be working on over-the-ear headphones for a March 2024 launch, possibly priced between $400 and $500, and high-end earbuds. Additionally, Sonos is reportedly developing an Android-based streaming video TV set-top box, codenamed "Pinewood," which could launch in late 2024 or early 2025.

title: Deere must face US farmers' 'right-to-repair' lawsuits, judge rules
summary: A U.S. judge has ruled that Deere & Co must face consolidated lawsuits from farmers accusing the agricultural machinery maker of unlawfully conspiring to restrict maintenance and repair services. The judge rejected Deere's arguments for dismissal, stating that the plaintiffs met the legal thresholds to pursue their antitrust claims. This case is part of a broader "right-to-repair" movement challenging restrictive repair practices across various industries.

title: Who says corporate bonds are dull? Apple’s bonds have seen equity-like returns in the past month
summary: Apple Inc. bonds have generated equity-like returns for investors in the past month, with prices climbing as Treasury yields have tumbled. This rally, fueled by hopes for Federal Reserve rate cuts in 2024, has led to significant gains in the broader fixed-income market, with the Bloomberg US Aggregate Bond Index seeing its biggest monthly gain since May 1985.

title: Apple to become largest customer of new $2B chip packaging facility in Arizona
summary: Apple announced it will be the primary customer for Amkor Technology Inc.'s new $2 billion chip packaging facility in Arizona, which will package chips produced by Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) at a nearby complex. This facility will play a crucial role in assembling and protecting the next generation of processors for various Apple products. The investment underscores Apple's commitment to American manufacturing and will create over 2,000 jobs.

title: One ETF that focuses on employee happiness is outperforming the market this year
summary: An ETF focused on companies with high employee happiness, the Harbor Human Capital Factor U.S. Large Cap ETF (HAPI), has outperformed the S&P 500 this year, climbing nearly 25% compared to the benchmark's less than 20% gain. The fund's strategy is based on Irrational Capital's extensive employee surveys and publicly available data, creating a "human capital score" that is considered a novel and uncorrelated investment factor. While the large-cap HAPI fund includes major tech stocks contributing to its performance, a similar small-cap ETF (HAPS) is also available, offering potential for future growth.

title: Walmart stops advertising on X, adding to growing list of companies
summary: Walmart has ceased advertising on X, joining several other major companies like Apple, Disney, and IBM, citing that it found other platforms to better reach its customers. This decision was part of a gradual reduction in advertising on X, not a sudden halt, according to a source familiar with the situation and an X executive. The social media platform has faced a significant exodus of advertisers due to concerns over antisemitic content, exacerbated by recent controversies involving Elon Musk's comments and his subsequent strong reaction to advertisers.

title: Amkor to invest US$2bn in US chip packaging
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. plans to invest US$2 billion to build an advanced semiconductor packaging facility in Peoria, Arizona. This new plant will create up to 2,000 jobs and is expected to start production within two to three years, primarily packaging chips made for Apple by TSMC in Phoenix. The investment aims to bolster the US’s chipmaking capabilities, reduce reliance on foreign packaging capacity, and has applied for Chips Act funding.

title: Sonos roadmap reveals multiple audio launches intended in 2024
summary: Sonos is planning an "aggressive" roadmap of new audio products and software updates for 2024, including new in-ceiling speakers, headphones, a second-generation Roam, a high-end amplifier, and a high-end soundbar. A set-top box and subwoofer are also planned for late 2024 or 2025 releases. The company aims to revive sales through these launches, despite facing skepticism in competitive markets.

title: 美股科技股“疯牛”行情未完待续? AI仍是引爆明年行情的关键
summary: Wedbush機構預計美股科技股在2024年將上漲至少20%，主要得益於人工智能（AI）和雲計算支出的大幅增長。分析師將AI比作互聯網的“1995時刻”，預計未來十年AI支出規模將達到1萬億美元，並點名英偉達和微軟將引領這一趨勢。儘管宏觀經濟不確定性存在，Wedbush仍堅定看好科技行業，並預計納斯達克100指數明年在高科技巨頭帶領下將大漲20%以上。

title: Amex CEO talks co-brand partnerships after Apple reports
summary: American Express CEO Steve Squeri discussed co-branded partnerships, emphasizing that Amex's "premium" card designation means it doesn't align with all potential partners, a point highlighted amidst reports of Amex potentially replacing Goldman Sachs as Apple's credit card partner. Squeri noted that Amex seeks partnerships that offer strong value propositions, target premium customers, and provide distribution while yielding "premium economics." Analysts have speculated on several potential suitors for Apple's card partnership, including Amex, JPMorgan Chase, Synchrony Financial, Capital One, and Bread.

title: Why Investors Should Keep An Eye on Small-Cap Stocks
summary: This article discusses how the increasing concentration of market-cap-weighted indexes, particularly driven by large-cap tech stocks, is creating historical levels of concentration. It explores the implications for the size premium, suggesting that higher market concentration leads to increased expected returns for small-cap stocks due to capital misallocation and reduced investor demand for smaller firms, making them an attractive area for investors.

title: Who’s ready for a Gamestop mutual fund? Not this broker
summary: Wedbush analysts are critical of GameStop's decision to delegate investment authority to CEO Ryan Cohen, allowing him to invest the company's capital in equities and other investments. Analyst Michael Pachter suggests that if GameStop believes in its own shares, it should use excess cash for buybacks instead. The move is compared to AMC's "embarrassingly stupid" investment in Hycroft Mining, which saw significant losses.

title: CastleArk Enters ETF Market With Large Growth ETF CARK
summary: CastleArk has launched its first exchange-traded fund, the CastleArk Large Growth ETF (CARK), an actively managed ETF focused on large-cap growth companies. The fund targets 25-30 leading growth companies, identifying competitive strength and seeking to optimize risk through quantitative inputs. CastleArk aims to provide an actively managed alternative to index-heavy portfolios, especially given the concentration of "Magnificent Seven" stocks in the large-cap growth sector.

title: Just 3 of the "Magnificent Seven" Have Outperformed the S&P 500 Since 2022. Here's the One I'd Buy for 2024 and 2025.
summary: This article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks against the S&P 500 since 2022. It identifies Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple as the only three that have outperformed the S&P 500. The author argues that Alphabet, despite underperforming recently, is the best buy for 2024 and 2025 due to its valuation, potential in digital advertising, and cloud segment growth.

title: Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:WISE)
summary: The Themes Generative Artificial Intelligence ETF (WISE) tracks an index of companies involved in AI-related industries, with a concentrated portfolio of 89 holdings. Launched on December 8, 2023, the ETF currently has $33.55 million in assets under management and a net expense ratio of 0.35%. Its top holdings include major tech companies like Advanced Micro Devices, Intel, Apple, and NVIDIA.

title: 'You have to be difficult': How Gap and J.Crew icon Mickey Drexler became the king of mall-brand retail
summary: This article details how Mickey Drexler, a prominent figure in retail, transformed Gap and revitalized J.Crew, known for his unconventional leadership. He also founded Old Navy and Madewell, served on Apple's board, and heavily influenced Alex Mill, a company started by his son. Drexler is characterized by his micromanagement style and significant impact on several successful brands.

title: The Nasdaq-100 Has Had a Monster 2023 So Far. Is It Too Late to Invest in It Before 2024 Arrives?
summary: The Nasdaq-100 has seen a significant rally of 45% in 2023 after a difficult 2022, outperforming other major U.S. indexes. Despite this strong performance, the article suggests that it's not too late to invest, emphasizing a long-term approach and dollar-cost averaging to mitigate short-term volatility. It highlights the Invesco QQQ ETF as a common way to invest in the Nasdaq-100, composed of innovative companies poised for continued growth in industries like AI, EVs, and biotech.

title: Apple supplier Corning to set up Rs 1,000 crore Gorilla Glass facility in Tamil Nadu
summary: Corning Inc., a key Apple supplier and Gorilla Glass manufacturer, plans to invest Rs 1,000 crore in a new manufacturing plant in Tamil Nadu, India. This facility, a collaboration with Indian partner Optiemus Infracom, will be located near other Apple suppliers and is expected to start operations in 2024, initially producing 30 million pieces of cover glass for smartphones. The move comes after Corning chose Tamil Nadu over Telangana, seeking central government incentives under the SPECS scheme.

title: How Google’s Epic Legal Defeat Threatens the App-Store Industry’s $200 Billion Business Model
summary: Google's recent legal defeat against Epic Games threatens the $200 billion app store industry, which includes Apple. A jury found Google's app store practices anticompetitive, which could lead to significant changes in commission rates and developer options. This verdict could also impact Apple's similar business model, despite its previous victory against Epic, as regulators and lawmakers globally scrutinize app store rules and consumer sentiment leans against the power of tech giants.

title: DGRW Has Hallmarks of a Great Dividend ETF
summary: The WisdomTree US Quality Dividend Growth Fund (DGRW) is highlighted as a strong dividend ETF due to its focus on companies' return on assets and return on equity, rather than just yield. This strategy has allowed DGRW to outperform the S&P 500 and other dividend indexes, especially in high-interest-rate environments. The ETF's approach aims for reduced volatility, potential capital appreciation, and diversification, including a significant allocation to tech stocks.

title: The Apple Inc (AAPL) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Apple Inc. (AAPL) to determine if the stock is undervalued. Based on the DCF analysis, Apple's stock appears overvalued with a DCF value of $118.68 per share compared to a current market price of $198.08, resulting in a negative margin of safety of -66.91%. The author emphasizes that this is a preliminary analysis and investors should conduct further research.

title: US Chamber, Comcast, tech group split over net neutrality
summary: The U.S. Chamber of Commerce and Comcast Corp are opposing the Federal Communications Commission's proposal to reinstate net neutrality rules, arguing it would harm investment and innovation. Conversely, major tech firms like Amazon, Apple, Alphabet, and Meta Platforms support the reinstatement to preserve open internet access. The FCC's move to regain regulatory oversight, following Democrats taking majority control, aims to ensure a "fast, open, and fair" internet, while some states also have existing net neutrality laws.

title: Focus: Exxon's low US tax payments ruffle Biden's climate agenda
summary: Exxon Mobil's income tax payments to the U.S. government have significantly decreased to 3% over the past five years, mainly due to deductions passed under former President Donald Trump, frustrating the Biden administration's climate agenda. This low tax rate, much lower than major companies in other sectors, highlights how current tax codes impede efforts to fund green energy initiatives. Experts suggest Exxon could maintain these low tax rates for several more years, even as the government seeks more revenue for climate action, partly because President Biden's new minimum corporate tax struggles with implementation and still accounts for the Trump-era accelerated depreciation deductions.

title: Call translation services new battleground in AI race
summary: The coming year will see a surge in real-time phone call translation services driven by AI, with Korean tech firms like SK Telecom and Samsung Electronics entering the fray. SK Telecom is launching its "A. call translator" service within its existing AI app, supporting four languages initially and expanding to 11. Samsung, conversely, plans to integrate its generative AI model "Samsung Gauss" directly into its new Galaxy S24 phones, offering on-device translation without external internet access, emphasizing privacy and reduced latency.

title: Apple Races to Tweak Software Ahead of Looming US Watch Ban
summary: Apple Inc. is rapidly working on software adjustments to its Apple Watch series to circumvent a looming U.S. ban. The changes primarily target the blood oxygen level measurement feature, which a company called Masimo Corp. claims infringes on its patents. These modifications include altering algorithms for oxygen saturation detection and data presentation to consumers.

title: Apple terminates credit-card and savings partnership with Goldman Sachs
summary: Apple Inc. has initiated the termination of its credit card and savings account partnership with Goldman Sachs Group Inc., proposing to end the collaboration within 12 to 15 months. This move follows Goldman Sachs' struggles with its consumer banking division, which has incurred losses despite the strong performance of Apple Card and Apple Savings. Apple is now seeking a new banking provider, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. and American Express Company emerging as top contenders.

title: Why Apple Had to Disable the Blood-Oxygen Sensor in Its Watches
summary: Apple has begun selling new versions of its latest Apple Watch models with the blood oxygen sensing feature disabled. This action stems from a patent dispute with Masimo Corp., a medical technology company, which successfully argued that Apple violated its patents. A US International Trade Commission ruling and subsequent appeals court decision led Apple to remove the feature rather than face a sales ban on its Watch Series 9 and Ultra 2 models.

title: Meta: Sell it? Buy it?
summary: Meta's stock price has experienced significant volatility in recent years, oscillating from a high of $384 in August 2021 to below $100 in October 2022, before rebounding to around $340. This article explores the company's journey from being considered a "sell" to a "buy," focusing on challenges such as Apple's privacy policy changes and competition from TikTok, and how Meta has responded by investing heavily in AI and re-evaluating its metaverse strategy. The future success of Meta is tied to its AI applications across its businesses, especially in advertising and new ventures.

title: U.S. Fitness Tracker Market Size, Growth and Trends [2030]
summary: The U.S. fitness tracker market was valued at USD 13.69 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach a significant size by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 18.9% during the forecast period. This growth is driven by the increasing prevalence of chronic diseases, high adoption of fitness monitoring devices, and technological advancements like integration with smartphones and advanced features. Despite potential inaccuracies, the market sees strong demand, particularly for smartwatches, with key players continuously innovating to meet consumer needs.

title: Intellexa and Cytrox: From fixer-upper to Intel Agency-grade spyware
summary: Cisco Talos has conducted an in-depth analysis of Intellexa, a spyware vendor previously known as Cytrox, detailing its operational paradigms, procedures, and the evolution of its Predator spyware. The analysis reveals that Intellexa's Predator spyware continues to evade detection, offers persistence even after device reboots, and demonstrates a sophisticated business model with high-priced solutions targeting state-sponsored agencies. Despite public disclosures and the US adding Intellexa to its Entity List, the company effectively continues its operations by adapting to patched exploits and maintaining plausible deniability through its sales and delivery methods.

title: PLDT warns public on AI-generated scams
summary: PLDT and Smart Communications are warning the public about the rise of AI-generated scams, which are becoming increasingly sophisticated and convincing. Criminals are leveraging AI to create polished messages and visuals that mimic official communications, making it harder for individuals to distinguish genuine messages from fraudulent ones. PLDT advises checking the source of emails and texts and adhering to the basic rule of not communicating with strangers to avoid falling victim to these scams.

title: Apple files appeal after Biden administration allows US ban on watch imports
summary: Apple has appealed a U.S. import ban on its watches, which stems from a patent dispute with medical monitoring company Masimo. The ban, allowed by the Biden administration, targets Apple Watches with blood-oxygen level features. Apple has also requested an emergency halt to the ban while awaiting a decision from U.S. Customs and Border Protection on redesigned versions of its watches.

title: T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF): A Promising Active Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF) has shown significant growth since its launch in June 2023, accumulating over $600 million in assets. The fund focuses on high-quality U.S. large-cap companies, employing a bottom-up stock selection approach with top holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. TCAF's investment philosophy emphasizes strong performance in areas like technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary.

title: How I used the Garmin epix Pro to train for Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend
summary: The author details their experience using the Garmin epix Pro to train for and complete Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend, which involves finishing three races (Sprint, Super, Beast) in one weekend. The article highlights how the smartwatch's features like training readiness, body battery, heart rate monitoring, and race prediction were crucial for their rigorous training regimen and successful completion of the demanding events. The author expresses that the Garmin epix Pro not only enhanced their athletic performance but also motivated them toward healthier habits and served as a worthy investment in their fitness journey.

title: Here Are All 49 Stocks Warren Buffett Holds for Berkshire Hathaway's $371 Billion Portfolio
