summary: This article details the 49 stocks held in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, valued at $371 billion. It highlights that over 85% of the assets are concentrated in eight major holdings like Apple and Bank of America, while also listing other billion-dollar investments and smaller holdings attributed to Buffett's lieutenants. The piece emphasizes Buffett's preference for financial and cyclical businesses, his valuation-driven approach, and recent investments in homebuilders.

title: Apple's stock underperformed top tech peers in 2023 due to longest revenue slide in 22 years
summary: Apple's stock underperformed its mega-cap tech peers in 2023, experiencing its longest revenue slide in 22 years with four consecutive quarters of declining sales. This decline was attributed to a combination of a weak economic environment for smartphones and PCs, and company-specific issues such as the lack of new iPad models and an Apple Watch sales ban. Despite a 49% stock rally for the year, Apple lagged behind competitors like Nvidia, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft.

title: Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:ZZZ)
summary: The Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF (ZZZ) is an exchange-traded fund launched on December 28, 2023, by Onefund, which invests in a 75/25 blend of US large-cap stocks and front-month CME Bitcoin futures. It does not directly invest in Bitcoin's spot price. The ETF has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy," assets under management of $6.02 million, and a net expense ratio of 1.01%.

title: Miami doesn’t need to be the Silicon Valley of the South
summary: Miami has been hyped as a "new Silicon Valley" or "Silicon Valley of the South," but this article argues it should be recognized for its unique strengths rather than compared to Silicon Valley. The author highlights Miami's advantages, including favorable tax laws, a supportive tech ecosystem, increasing VC attention, strong foreign investment, a focus on AI, and its role as a bridge to Latin America for nearshoring. Miami's distinct features allow it to stand as its own specialized tech hub.

title: Markets in Focus: Concentrating on Diversification
summary: The article discusses the increasing concentration in equity markets, exemplified by the "Magnificent Seven" stocks, and the diminished diversification benefits of bonds, especially since 2020. It suggests that investors reassess top-down allocations and broaden equity exposure across diverse regions, sectors, and styles to create more resilient portfolios. The MSCI Adaptive Multi-Factor Allocation Model indicates an overweight to value and low size as of December 31, 2023, with Europe and China trading at lower historical valuations.

title: Google and Samsung Set To Use Qualcomm's New VR Chip, Could Compete With Apple and Meta
summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) recently unveiled its new Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 2 Platform chip for virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR). Google (GOOGL) and Samsung are slated to integrate this chip into their upcoming VR/AR experiences, aiming to challenge market leaders like Apple (AAPL) and Meta Platforms (META). This development could intensify competition in the growing VR headset market, especially following Apple's Vision Pro and Meta's dominance in 2023.

title: Aeva to supply sensors to Daimler Truck in $1 billion deal
summary: Aeva Technologies has secured a $1 billion deal to supply lidar sensors to Daimler Truck for self-driving trucks, which are anticipated to be on U.S. roads by 2027. Aeva's sensors are designed to detect the speed of distant objects, aiding in collision avoidance. The agreement underscores continued interest in robotic trucking for long-haul routes despite challenges faced by self-driving passenger vehicle technology.

title: FTI Consulting adds trio to Strategic Communications team
summary: FTI Consulting has expanded its Strategic Communications practice in India by hiring Salonie Chawla as a Director, Snehil Singh as a Senior Consultant, and Shruti Vakhariya as a Consultant. These new additions bring diverse experience from government engagement, public policy, and financial communications to strengthen the team's capabilities in handling complex policy challenges and industry-side advisory for clients. The firm anticipates increased demand for such expertise given the disruptive business landscape expected in the coming 24 months.

title: Software Glitch Behind Recalled App Could Drastically Increase Insulin Delivery
summary: A software glitch in a recalled app has the potential to drastically increase insulin delivery, posing a significant health risk. This issue was highlighted on January 10, 2024, by Brian Bossetta.

title: Iridium Is Offering Its Own Smartphone-to-Satellite Service to Companies
summary: Iridium has launched its own smartphone-to-satellite service, "Project Stardust," after its partnership with Qualcomm ended. This new service will allow original equipment manufacturers and carriers to offer text messaging and emergency SOS capabilities through Iridium's global low Earth orbit satellite constellation, adhering to 3GPP 5G standards. Initially focusing on data messaging, Iridium plans to begin testing in 2025 with service expected to start in 2026.

title: Upcoming Events
summary: REX Shares, in collaboration with Tuttle Capital Management, has expanded its T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite with new offerings providing 200% daily targeted exposure to Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft. These ETFs (AAPX, GOOX, MSFX) are designed for seasoned traders to give leveraged exposure to major tech stocks. The T-REX suite, which debuted in October 2023, now covers diverse tech sectors and has already attracted over $100 million in assets.

title: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management Expand T-REX Single-Stock ETF Suite with Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft Offerings
summary: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management have expanded their T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite by introducing new offerings for Apple (CBOE: AAPX), Alphabet (CBOE: GOOX), and Microsoft (CBOE: MSFX). These ETFs provide seasoned traders with 200% daily targeted exposure to these major tech companies. The expansion aims to offer advanced tools for precise positioning in the tech giants, building on the success of earlier NVIDIA and Tesla single-stock ETFs which have attracted over $100 million in assets since their October 2023 launch.

title: Taiwan's strength in semiconductors could be its 'Achilles heel,' economist says
summary: An economist warns that Taiwan's heavy reliance on semiconductor exports, particularly to China, could become its "Achilles heel." Paul Cavey of East Asia Econ advises Taiwan to diversify its economy, as China's growing capacity for chip production could significantly impact Taiwan's economic future. This vulnerability is further exacerbated by the ongoing U.S.-China chip war and efforts by Western countries to restrict China's access to advanced chip technology.

title: Optimizing Communication and Data Sharing in Multi-Core SoC Designs
summary: This article discusses the evolution of interconnects in Systems-on-Chip (SoCs), from shared buses to Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures, highlighting their role in managing data flow and enhancing performance. It delves into the importance of cache coherence for data consistency in multi-core designs and the challenges engineers face in optimizing communication and data sharing as semiconductor technology advances. The article also mentions Arteris' Ncore Cache Coherent Interconnect as a solution.

title: IDC: Apple sold more smartphones than Samsung for the first time in 2023
summary: Apple Inc. surpassed Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. to become the world's largest smartphone manufacturer by unit shipments in 2023, according to a preliminary report by IDC. This marks the first time since 2010 that Samsung did not hold the top position. Despite an overall decline in global smartphone shipments for the year, Apple saw an increase in iPhone sales, particularly strong in the fourth quarter, whereas Samsung experienced a decline.

title: Supreme Court rejects Apple's appeal on App Store fees amid Epic Games legal battle
summary: The Supreme Court has rejected Apple's appeal regarding its App Store fees, allowing a lower court order to take effect. This decision could force Apple to permit app developers to offer alternative payment options, potentially reducing Apple's commission revenue. The ruling stems from an antitrust lawsuit filed by Epic Games, although Epic's broader antitrust claims against Apple were also rejected.

title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
summary: Apple Inc.'s stock experienced a 0.52% decline, closing at $182.68 on Wednesday, marking its second consecutive day of losses. This occurred during an overall negative trading session where the NASDAQ Composite fell by 0.59% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.25%. Despite the daily loss, the article title suggests Apple's stock performance still outpaced the broader market.

title: 5 key themes from the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show
summary: The 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) highlighted five key technological themes: AI as a pervasive enabler across all tech, advancements in smart home devices with unified communication standards like Matter, the evolution of immersive technology anticipating the Apple Vision Pro, the future of the automotive industry focusing on driver experience and electric vehicles, and the widening scope of health technology, including AI-powered mental wellness tools and wearables. These innovations provide a glimpse into the technological future.

title: Your S&P 500 Index Fund Has Higher Risk Than You May Think
summary: This article argues that the S&P 500 index fund, traditionally seen as diversified, now carries higher risk due to its concentration in a few technology stocks. It highlights concentration, valuation, and inflation risks, suggesting investors re-evaluate their S&P 500 holdings and consider more value-oriented, truly diversified investments.

title: More Computer Magazine Lore: A 1983 Nastygram from Wayne Green
summary: This article provides additional lore surrounding computer magazines, focusing on Wayne Green, a colorful and controversial figure in tech publishing. It details his eccentric career, his contentious relationship with figures like Steve Jobs and David Bunnell, and his impact on early computer magazines like Byte, PC World, and Macworld. The post highlights a "nastygram" Green sent in response to PC World's premiere issue, shedding light on the competitive landscape of the 1980s computer magazine industry.

title: Higher S&P 500 Earnings Could Benefit This Active ETF

His experience includes leading the development of HTML5-enabled web browsers at Apple and pivotal work on Chromecast, Home, and Nest products at Google.

title: Apple defeats lawsuit claiming it overpaid CEO Tim Cook, others
summary: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit against Apple alleging overpayment of CEO Tim Cook and other top executives through miscalculated performance-based stock awards. The judge ruled that Apple's compensation disclosures met securities law requirements and found no proof of improper actions by the board, also noting the plaintiffs did not allow sufficient time for the board to address their objections before filing suit. The lawsuit, brought by a Teamsters-affiliated pension fund, claimed Apple overvalued restricted stock units in 2021 and 2022.

title: Nvidia Overtakes Amazon in Market Value
summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon in market value, making it the fourth most valuable US-listed company earlier in the day. The chipmaker's rally, driven by exploding demand for AI chips, has seen its shares jump 46% this year. While Nvidia closed slightly below Amazon's valuation, the event marks a significant milestone fueled by the AI boom.

title: Meta settles lawsuit over haptic feedback tech in Quest headsets
summary: Immersion, a company specializing in haptic feedback technology, has settled a lawsuit with Meta over Meta's use of touch-feedback in its Quest VR headsets. The settlement involves Meta licensing Immersion's patents for its hardware, software, VR, and gaming products, though the terms were not disclosed. Immersion has a history of pursuing patent infringement lawsuits against major tech companies like Microsoft, Sony, Apple, and Valve.

title: Michael Burry Covers Bet Against Chips ETF, Buys Health and Tech Stocks

Burry, known from "The Big Short," had previously bet against the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indexes.

title: Stock market concentration is on the rise. Will this continue?

Investors should consider the implications of this concentration on future performance, especially in different economic scenarios like a soft landing.

While China has already surpassed Korea in LCDs, Korea is focusing on IT OLEDs and innovative TV features to maintain its competitive edge. Despite China's increasing market share in terms of volume, Korea still leads in sales value for premium displays.

These updates were showcased at AAOS in San Francisco, with further Mako expansions, including Mako Spine and Mako Shoulder, anticipated later in 2024.

title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $7,000 in the "Magnificent Seven" in 2017

This strategic move aligns with Walmart's plan to diversify its profit streams, with a greater focus on advertising and online marketplace businesses in the coming years.

The company's unique technology and supply chain have led to widespread adoption by major tech firms, with discussions emerging about potential shortages and the need for continued investment in chip manufacturing.

The ruling provides key takeaways for companies to guide their trade secret policies and practices to avoid litigation.

Symbiosis focuses on planting biodiverse native species using seeds from "mother trees" and employing technology to monitor carbon sequestration, while Forestal Apepu cultivates fast-growing eucalyptus for timber while also implementing social impact initiatives to support local communities. Both projects aim to restore the rainforest, enhance biodiversity, and provide sustainable livelihoods, demonstrating a "symbiotic" approach to environmental and community well-being.

It also notes common types of damage, the short time consumers wait for repairs, and factors influencing smartphone purchase decisions related to sustainability.

These actions are raising investor concerns about potential fines and the company's market dominance.

title: Under Pressure: Antitrust and Competition Authorities are Now Focusing on Apple
summary: Antitrust and competition authorities in both the EU and the US are increasingly scrutinizing Apple's market practices, a significant shift for a company that previously avoided intense regulatory attention. Recent actions include a substantial fine from the European Commission related to App Store policies and a lawsuit from the US Department of Justice alleging anticompetitive practices designed to maintain iPhone user lock-in. These developments suggest a global movement towards stricter regulation of big tech, challenging Apple's long-held justifications of privacy and security for its closed ecosystem.

title: Explainer: US lawsuit against Apple could make iPhone experience more consumer-friendly
summary: The U.S. Justice Department has filed a lawsuit against Apple, aiming to increase competition for the iPhone and benefit smaller companies developing apps for the device. The lawsuit claims Apple has harmed smaller rivals and inflated prices. If successful, it could lead to changes in the U.S. similar to those already implemented in Europe, such as universal charging cables, alternative app stores, and broader browser choices.

title: Wait, Did Europe Really Outperform the U.S. over the Last Three Years?
summary: This article challenges the common perception that European equities perpetually lag behind their U.S. counterparts. It highlights how a selection of 11 European "GRANOLAS" companies have delivered returns surprisingly similar to the U.S. Magnificent 7 over the past three years. Additionally, the WisdomTree Europe Hedged Equity Fund (HEDJ) has demonstrated significant outperformance against the S&P 500 and comparable European ETFs, attributed to its stock selection process, dividend yield weighting, and exporter tilt, all while trading at a valuation discount.

title: Apple’s stock has faltered, but a Steve Jobs moment in AI may be on the horizon
summary: Apple's stock performance has been underwhelming, leading to concerns about its AI capabilities. However, an analyst suggests that Apple CEO Tim Cook might be preparing a "Steve Jobs moment" by announcing an AI app store, similar to how Wall Street initially underestimated the iTunes Store. This could represent a significant play in the artificial intelligence sector, despite previous doubts fueled by reports of Apple discussing AI engines with other tech giants.

title: Baidu Shares Rise After Reports That Apple Will Use Its AI Services in China Products
summary: Baidu's shares increased significantly in Hong Kong following reports that Apple plans to utilize Baidu's AI services for its products in China. The collaboration, which has been the subject of preliminary talks, would make Baidu Apple's local generative AI model provider for the iPhone 16, Mac operating system, and iOS 18. This partnership is pending approval from Chinese regulators for generative AI models.

title: FTEC: Concentration Increases Volatility (NYSEARCA:FTEC)
summary: The Fidelity MSCI Information Technology Index ETF (FTEC) offers strong tech exposure, performing similarly to VGT. However, 45% of its assets are concentrated in Microsoft, Apple, and NVIDIA, which could lead to increased volatility despite a projected 7% upside by year-end 2024. The author, Ricardo Fernandez, highlights the importance of an investor's goals and definition of "tech" when choosing an ETF.

title: RERE: INITIATION: Leading Recycling Platform in China’s Burgeoning Circular Economy
summary: Zacks Small Cap Research initiated coverage on ATRenew Inc. (NYSE: RERE) with a 12-month price target of $4.00, citing its leadership in China's pre-owned consumer electronics market. The investment thesis highlights ATRenew's differentiated business model, multi-pronged growth strategy, accelerating financial performance, strong capital base, and attractive valuation. The report provides a deep dive into the company's operations, growth drivers, financial model, and investment risks.

title: One Fifth of Applied Materials Shareholders Vote to Address the Pay Gap
summary: Just over 20% of Applied Materials (AMAT) shareholders voted for a proposal requesting the company report on pay gaps across race and gender. This level of support, while lower than similar proposals at other tech giants, still sends a message to the board about the importance of the issue. The article also touches on other significant proxy season themes, including unionization, simple majority voting, climate change, corporate political influence, and artificial intelligence.

title: A new product allows 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven. Traders should be wary.
summary: A new exchange-traded product allows investors to make 5x leveraged bets on the Magnificent Seven group of stocks, raising concerns among analysts. While levered exchange-traded products are gaining popularity, traders are warned about the significant risks associated with such highly leveraged instruments. ETF analyst Todd Sohn from Strategas Securities notes that this product appears to be the most heavily levered available in any developed market.

title: SEC sides with ADF attorneys, denies Wells Fargo, Charles Schwab attempts to dodge shareholders
summary: The SEC has sided with Alliance Defending Freedom attorneys in two recent decisions, denying attempts by Wells Fargo and Charles Schwab to exclude shareholder proposals from their proxy ballots. This marks ADF's fifth such victory, compelling financial giants to address concerns regarding politicized de-banking and workforce discrimination rooted in DEI policies. The rulings emphasize shareholder accountability and transparency in corporate decision-making.

title: Executive Turntable: Sphere Gets Creative; PushaRod Joins Hallwood; Wasserman Orchestrates Division
summary: This article details recent executive appointments and promotions within the music industry. Key moves include Ned McNeilage becoming Sphere Entertainment's Chief Creative Officer, Roderick "PushaRod" Bullock joining Hallwood Media as VP of A&R/Management, DEAG founder Peter Schwenkow stepping down, and Wasserman Music expanding with promotions and a new orchestral division. Additionally, Splash appointed Tracy Chan as CEO, Universal Music Publishing Group promoted Adriana Ramos, and Primary Wave Music made several creative sync appointments.

title: F5 Networks (FFIV) Appoints Kunal Anand as Chief Technology Officer
summary: F5, Inc. announced the appointment of Kunal Anand as Executive Vice President and Chief Technology Officer (CTO), alongside Lyra Schramm as Executive Vice President and Chief People Officer (CPO). Anand will be responsible for F5's technology vision, leveraging his expertise in cybersecurity and data to accelerate the company's leadership in application and API security. Schramm will focus on driving people initiatives and fostering an innovative workplace culture.

title: FemTech and the Gender Health Gap
summary: A report by Citi Global Insights highlights the gender health gap, noting that women experience poorer health outcomes and negative healthcare experiences compared to men. FemTech, which uses digital technology to address women's health conditions, is presented as a crucial tool for closing this gap, despite being a smaller sector within healthcare innovation. The article also points out systemic issues like underfunded research and weak financial incentives that have historically disadvantaged women's health.

title: Leading chipmaker TSMC evacuates staff and pauses some machinery after Taiwan's largest earthquake in a quarter-century
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC), the world's largest advanced chipmaker, and its rival United Microelectronics Corp. (UMC) halted some machinery and evacuated staff after Taiwan experienced its strongest earthquake in 25 years. The 7.4 magnitude temblor raised concerns about the semiconductor supply chain's vulnerability, given Taiwan's critical role in producing high-end chips. TSMC shares slid about 1.5% and UMC less than 1% in early trading following the event.

title: Spotify to raise prices of plans in some markets, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Spotify is reportedly planning to increase its subscription prices by $1 to $2 per month in several markets, including the UK, Australia, and Pakistan, by the end of April. Prices in the United States are expected to rise later in the year, alongside the introduction of a new basic tier offering music and podcasts but no audiobooks. This move aims to boost profitability for the company, which forecasts strong subscriber growth and is diversifying its offerings beyond music.

title: Cashing in on the Bitcoin Bounce: 3 Stocks to Buy for Indirect Crypto Exposure
summary: The article identifies three stocks—MercadoLibre (MELI), Broadcom (AVGO), and SBI Holdings Inc (SBHGF)—that offer investors indirect exposure to the cryptocurrency market. These companies are involved in various blockchain-related activities, such as crypto trading, integration of blockchain technology, and investments in crypto startups, providing an alternative for those hesitant about direct crypto investments. The author suggests these stocks allow investors to benefit from Bitcoin's performance while diversifying their portfolios and maintaining traditional stock stability.

title: Garmin Lily 2 Review: A Smartwatch That Doesn't Scream 'Fitness Tracker'
summary: The Garmin Lily 2 is presented as a fashion-forward smartwatch for women, offering sleek design and robust health tracking features like body battery and stress scores. While praised for its aesthetics and comprehensive health data, the review notes shortcomings in display quality, activity tracking user experience, and the companion app's clunkiness, making it better suited for casual users rather than fitness enthusiasts or those seeking advanced smartwatch functionalities.

title: If I Could Only Buy and Hold 1 Vanguard ETF For the Next 5 Years, This Would Be My Top Pick
summary: The article recommends the Vanguard Large-Cap ETF (VV) as a top choice for a buy-and-hold strategy for the next five years due to its low expense ratio, focus on U.S. large-cap stocks like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, and its ability to provide broad diversification. It highlights the ETF's simplicity and stability, making it easier for investors to hold through market volatility compared to individual stocks or more specialized thematic ETFs. The author also notes the subtle differences and near-identical returns between VV and the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF, with a slight preference for VV.

title: EU regulators assess Apple's plan for complying with music streaming order
summary: EU antitrust regulators are evaluating Apple's proposal to comply with an order requiring it to allow music streaming services like Spotify to inform users of alternative payment options outside its App Store. Apple faces potential antitrust charges and additional fines if its proposed solution, which includes a 27% fee on digital purchases made via external links, does not satisfy the European Commission. The Commission is also investigating Apple's App Store rules under the new Digital Markets Act.

title: Apple Signs Deal For AI Training Data from Image Service Shutterstock
summary: Apple has signed a deal worth between $25 million and $50 million with Shutterstock to access its vast archive of images, video, and music for training AI systems. This move indicates Apple's strategic intent to develop its own AI models, rather than relying solely on existing solutions. The deal highlights Apple's characteristic approach to adhere to rules and protect user privacy, contrasting with some competitors' controversial methods of data acquisition.

title: Better Chip Stock: Marvell Technology vs. Qualcomm
summary: This article compares Marvell Technology (MRVL) and Qualcomm (QCOM) as semiconductor stock investments. Marvell, despite a near-term slowdown, is driven by AI-oriented chips and acquisitions, while Qualcomm is recovering from a smartphone market downturn with diversification into automotive and IoT, alongside new AI mobile chips. The author concludes that Qualcomm is the better buy due to its lower valuation, higher dividend yield, and anticipated market recovery.

title: Apple's New Jersey store workers file petition for unionization
summary: Workers at an Apple store in Short Hills, New Jersey, have filed for union representation with the Communications Workers of America. This move is part of a broader unionization push across various companies in corporate America, including Starbucks, Amazon.com, and Microsoft, where employees are seeking better working conditions. Apple stated that it offers top-tier pay and comprehensive benefits to its retail teams.

title: 14 improvements Royal Caribbean developed just for Icon of the Seas
summary: Royal Caribbean's Icon of the Seas was designed as a "white paper cruise ship," leading to numerous innovations and improvements over standard operating procedures. The article highlights 14 such advancements, focusing on enhancing guest experience, crew efficiency, and the overall ship environment, leveraging technology like AI, apps, and new physical designs. These changes range from digital guest services and photo viewing to destination elevators, a transformative Aquadome, and improved crew quarters.

title: Apple denies violating US court order in Epic Games lawsuit
summary: Apple is denying that it violated a court order related to its App Store and is urging a federal judge to reject Epic Games' request to hold it in contempt. Epic Games argues that Apple's 27% fee for alternative payment methods and its alleged ban on informing users about other payment options constitute "blatant violation" of the injunction. Other major tech companies like Meta, Microsoft, and Match Group have sided with Epic, claiming Apple is in "clear violation" of the court's order.

title: 1 Stock-Split ETF That Could Turn $500 Per Month Into $1 Million, With Help From Nvidia, Microsoft, and Apple
summary: The iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (IGM) recently undertook a 6-for-1 stock split, making its shares more accessible to a wider range of investors. This ETF, which has outperformed both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100, holds top tech stocks like Nvidia, Microsoft, Apple, Meta Platforms, and Alphabet, benefiting significantly from the burgeoning AI industry. The article suggests that a consistent monthly investment of $500 into IGM has the potential to grow substantially, potentially reaching $1 million or more over the long term, especially if the current high growth rates driven by AI continue.

title: IHG Hotels & Resorts Launches Apple AirPlay in North American Hotels
summary: IHG Hotels & Resorts has become the first hospitality company to launch Apple AirPlay functionality in its North American hotel rooms, allowing guests to securely stream content from their iPhone or iPad to in-room LG TVs. This new feature is currently available at over 60 locations and aims to enhance the guest experience by offering personalized entertainment and a "home-away-from-home" environment. Guests can connect privately and securely by scanning a unique QR code to the hotel's Wi-Fi network.

title: Why Kartoon Studios (TOON) Shares Are Falling
summary: Kartoon Studios (NASDAQ: TOON) shares are down 26% after the company announced the pricing of a registered direct offering of up to 7 million shares of its common stock and/or pre-funded warrants to institutional investors. The offering is expected to generate approximately $7 million in gross proceeds. The first tranche of 4 million shares is set to close around April 22, with an option for investors to purchase an additional 3 million shares within 180 days.

title: Apple Inc (AAPL) DCF Valuation: Is The Stock Undervalued?
summary: This article presents a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation for Apple Inc. (AAPL), concluding that the stock is overvalued. Based on the analysis, Apple's intrinsic value is estimated at $112.24 per share, significantly lower than its current market price of $167.20, resulting in a negative margin of safety. The DCF model uses specific inputs for discount rate, terminal growth rate, and WACC, along with forecasted free cash flows to arrive at the valuation.

title: Apple contributes to the environment by investing in water and clean energy
summary: Apple is a global leader in technology and a pioneer in ecology, committing to protecting the environment by investing in water and clean energy. The company aims to achieve climate neutrality across all business areas by 2030, actively optimizing its supply chain, investing in renewable energy, and engaging in water conservation efforts. These initiatives include developing solar and wind farms, restoring water supplies, and issuing green bonds to fund its environmental projects, influencing both consumers and business partners toward sustainable practices.

title: Luxury Soccer Tourism At D.C. Intercontinental Hotel
summary: The InterContinental Washington D.C. - The Wharf is targeting luxury soccer tourism with a new VIP package offered in partnership with D.C. United. This initiative capitalizes on the growing sports tourism sector and the increasing popularity of soccer in the U.S., particularly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The package includes premium accommodations, VIP match day experiences, and a meet-and-greet with the Player of the Match, aiming to attract affluent fans to the revitalized Buzzard Point neighborhood.

title: 3 reasons the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) is a great long-term investment
summary: The iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) is presented as an appealing long-term investment for Australians, despite the local ASX market. The article highlights three main reasons: its remarkably low management fees of 0.04%, its investment in 500 of the largest and most profitable US companies like Microsoft and Amazon, and its strong historical returns, averaging over 16% per annum over the last five and ten years.

title: Big Monthly Dividends From Big Tech: JEPQ Vs. JEPI
summary: The article compares two popular high-yield monthly dividend ETFs, JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI) and JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ). Both ETFs offer exposure to big tech companies and aim to provide income investors with attractive yields. The author intends to compare them to help investors decide which is better for a diversified income portfolio.

title: KKR joins global cos betting billions of dollars in fastest-growing India
summary: KKR & Co. is optimistic about investment opportunities in India's consumer sector and is expanding its private credit portfolio, planning to deploy $10 billion at a faster pace. The firm, along with others like Blackstone and Goldman Sachs, is attracted by India's rapid economic growth and government incentives for manufacturing. Despite past challenges, KKR remains committed to India, exploring new sectors like manufacturing and potentially real estate in the future.

title: Apple launches “Made for Business” in select stores around the world
summary: Apple is launching "Made for Business," a new series of free Today at Apple programming designed for small business owners and entrepreneurs. These sessions, led by other successful small business owners, will demonstrate how Apple products and services like Apple Business Connect, Apple Business Essentials, and Tap to Pay on iPhone can help drive business growth and success. The program will kick off in May during National Small Business Week in the U.S., with ongoing sessions in select stores globally.

title: Imax Earnings: Dune 2 Boosts North American Market Share In Q1
summary: Imax reported strong Q1 earnings, with revenue of $79 million and a significant earnings bump, largely due to the success of "Dune: Part 2" and other titles like "Godzilla x Kong: New Empire." The company achieved its highest-ever quarterly North American market share at 5.9% and saw its global box office reach its third-highest Q1 total of $261 million despite a soft release slate caused by Hollywood strikes. CEO Rich Gelfond highlighted the increasing use of Imax cameras for blockbuster content and a promising slate for future growth.

title: Microsoft and Apple are safer bets than the U.S. government? Bond investors seem to think so.
summary: Bond investors perceive Microsoft and Apple's bonds as safer than U.S. Treasury debt, with these corporate bonds trading at negative spread levels to Treasurys. This unusual market phenomenon is partly attributed to record annuity sales driven by investors planning for retirement. The article highlights that despite the general rule of being compensated for investment risk, these tech giants' bonds are an exception.

title: Why I Just Added This Ultra-High-Yield Dividend ETF to My Retirement Account
summary: The author recently added the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ) to their retirement account, aiming for consistent passive income and price appreciation with lower volatility. JEPQ offers a high dividend yield (9.2% over 30 days, 10.8% over 12 months) through a strategy of holding a high-quality equity portfolio and selling out-of-the-money call options on the Nasdaq-100 index. This approach is designed to generate monthly income and provide relatively steady returns, making it an attractive investment for retirement planning.

title: How to Buy Roku Stock Online in 2021
summary: This guide explains how to invest in Roku stock, detailing the company's background, growth potential, and current financial standing. It outlines a three-step process for buying Roku shares through online brokers like eToro, emphasizing reasons to consider the investment such as global expansion, increasing ad revenue, and a healthy balance sheet. The article also touches upon risks, market competition, and analyst outlooks for Roku.

title: How to Buy Alphabet (Google) Stock Online in 2025
summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide on how to buy Alphabet (Google) stock online in 2025, detailing steps, top brokers, and reasons to invest in the company. It covers Google's diverse services, its financial performance, business strategy, and historical stock information, emphasizing its growth and expansion into new technologies like AI. The article also addresses common FAQs regarding Alphabet stock.

title: Top 10 Smartwatch Companies | By Best Technology
summary: The article provides an overview of the smartwatch market, highlighting key drivers such as wearable technology adoption and technological advancements, and forecasting the market size to reach USD 138.7 Billion by 2033. It then details the top ten companies in the smartwatch market, including Apple, Samsung, and Google, offering company overviews, key management, financials, geographical presence, and recent developments for each. The report underscores the continuous innovation and strategic expansions by these major players in the growing wearable technology sector.

title: As You Sow Calls Out Big Tech for $5 Billion Loss in 401(k) Returns
summary: A report by the non-profit As You Sow and researchers at the University of Waterloo indicates that over two million employees of 12 tech companies missed out on $5.1 billion in retirement savings. This loss is attributed to the companies' 401(k) plans not adopting sustainable investing strategies, specifically by retaining investments in fossil fuels. The study found a significant difference in 10-year returns for portfolios without fossil fuels, leading As You Sow to accuse these companies of greenwashing and urge them to offer sustainable investment options.

title: Positive Tech Earnings Could Accelerate This Momentum ETF
summary: Positive tech earnings, especially from "Magnificent Seven" companies, are expected to fuel the Invesco S&P 500 Momentum ETF (SPMO) after a challenging April. The ETF focuses on large-cap growth names with a high momentum score, and strong tech performance could drive further gains for the S&P 500. Additionally, disinflation data and the Federal Reserve's monetary policy decisions will play a crucial role in sustaining market momentum.

title: The DOJ’s Apple antitrust suit doesn’t add up
summary: The U.S. Department of Justice has filed an antitrust suit against Apple, accusing it of anticompetitive conduct relating to its iOS operating system. The article argues that the DOJ's claims, which center on product tying and vertical restraints, fundamentally misunderstand Apple's long-standing "walled garden" approach, which many consumers prefer for its security and user experience. It also suggests that the alleged anticompetitive acts in areas like digital wallets or the App Store are not lucrative enough to justify the severe remedies sought by the DOJ, unlike the Microsoft case often referenced.

title: FTSE 100 back at record levels, Diageo gains on new CFO but IHG declines - Market Report
summary: The FTSE 100 approached a new intraday high, largely driven by gains from Diageo following the announcement of its new CFO. Despite strong revenue growth, Holiday Inn owner IHG saw its stock decline. Meanwhile, Trainline experienced significant growth in operating profit, and Apple's shares rose after exceeding earnings expectations.

title: Berkshire Hathaway operating earnings soar 39% as Buffett's cash hoard swells to record $188 billion
summary: Berkshire Hathaway reported a 39% surge in Q1 operating earnings, reaching $11.22 billion, driven by a 185% increase in insurance underwriting earnings. The company's cash hoard also hit a record high of $188.99 billion, reflecting a lack of major acquisition targets. These results were released just before Berkshire's highly anticipated annual shareholder meeting.

title: Apple’s 2024 Pride Collection shines light on LGBTQ+ communities
summary: Apple has introduced its 2024 Pride Collection, featuring a new Apple Watch Pride Edition Braided Solo Loop, a matching watch face, and dynamic iOS and iPadOS wallpapers. This initiative supports LGBTQ+ advocacy organizations and celebrates the diversity of LGBTQ+ communities. The collection emphasizes inclusivity with colors representing Black, Hispanic, Latin, transgender, and nonbinary individuals.

title: Samsung Display established a dedicated research team to develop OLED products for Apple
summary: Samsung Display has formed a specialized "A Team" at its Giheung Research Institute to focus on developing OLED products specifically for Apple. This strategic move comes as Apple expands its use of OLEDs beyond iPhones into IT products like tablets, where LG Display currently holds a leading position due to its experience with tandem OLED architecture. Samsung Display aims to strengthen its competitive edge against rivals like LG Display and Chinese AMOLED producers by dedicating resources to meet Apple's evolving display needs.

title: Healthy Returns: Eli Lilly, Novo Nordisk make progress toward boosting weight loss drug supply
summary: Eli Lilly and Novo Nordisk, dominant manufacturers of weight loss and diabetes drugs, are increasing their supply to meet high demand. Eli Lilly raised its full-year revenue outlook by $2 billion, confident in its increased production of Zepbound and Mounjaro, with several new manufacturing sites and a new delivery device for international markets. Novo Nordisk, despite increasing Wegovy supply, saw its shares fall after missing Wall Street's sales estimates for the drug, attributing it to declining prices due to increased volume and competition.

title: The rise of TSMC: How one chipmaker started making everyone's chips
summary: This article traces the journey of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) from its founding in 1987 by Morris Chang to becoming the world's leading chip manufacturer, producing 90% of cutting-edge logic chips. It highlights key milestones including Chang's early career at Texas Instruments, TSMC's unique business model focusing solely on manufacturing, its crucial partnership with Apple, and recent expansions with significant investments in the U.S. and Japan.

title: Can Crowding Scores Quantify US Stocks’ Fragility?
summary: The U.S. equity market has reached an unprecedented level of concentration, largely driven by mega-cap tech stocks, sparking concerns about market fragility and crowding risk. While crowded stocks globally have historically underperformed uncrowded ones, the U.S. market has recently bucked this trend, with crowded stocks outperforming since mid-2022. The MSCI Security Crowding Model offers insights into identifying areas of the market most susceptible to price correction if sentiment deteriorates.

title: Which Credit Bureau Stock Should You Buy for the Long-Term?
summary: This article analyzes three major credit bureau stocks: Experian (EXPGY), TransUnion (TRU), and Equifax (EFX), evaluating them based on analyst ratings, revenue, and free cash flow yield. It recommends Experian as a "Buy" due to its competitive free cash flow yield and market leadership, designates TransUnion as a "Hold" despite its recent revenue growth, and rates Equifax as a "Sell" primarily because of its lower free cash flow yield compared to its peers. The author also discusses the broader industry context, including the impact of "buy now, pay later" services on credit reporting.

title: Digital Map Market Size, Share with Focus on Emerging Technologies, Top Countries Data, Top Key Players Update, and Forecast 2029
summary: The digital map market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 11.1% from USD 28.3 billion in 2024 to USD 47.8 billion by 2029, driven by increased organizational need for efficiency and scalability. The routing and navigation data segment is expected to hold the largest market share, with the Asia Pacific region experiencing the highest growth rates due to technological advancements and smartphone adoption. Key players like Google, Apple, and Nearmap are adopting various growth strategies to expand their market presence.

title: Faulty insulin pump tech led to hundreds of injuries, prompting app recall
summary: A software glitch in an insulin pump app developed by Tandem Diabetes Care led to over 200 injuries due to unexpected pump shutdowns. The FDA has prompted the recall of more than 85,000 versions of the t:connect mobile app, which could drain the pump's battery and suspend insulin delivery, potentially causing serious health complications. Users are urged to update the app to version 2.7.1 or later immediately.

title: The Digital Markets Act Roundup: March 2024
summary: The Digital Markets Act (DMA) enforcement has continued actively, particularly with non-compliance investigations against major companies like Alphabet, Apple, and Meta. Key developments include discussions on competition law, AI integration in core platform services, and the role of national authorities in DMA enforcement. Additionally, Apple faced a significant fine for anticompetitive app store rules, small browsers have seen market share growth due to DMA-mandated choice screens, and Apple's iPadOS was designated as a gatekeeper under the DMA.

title: What Are Scope 3 Emissions And Why Should Trucking Companies Care?
summary: This article explains Scope 1, 2, and 3 greenhouse gas emissions, particularly focusing on why Scope 3 emissions are crucial for trucking companies. Trucking fleets' Scope 1 emissions contribute to their customers' Scope 3 emissions, making trucking companies vital partners in their clients' sustainability efforts. The article highlights how regulations and corporate sustainability goals are increasing the pressure on companies, including those in the trucking industry, to report and reduce these indirect emissions throughout their supply chains.

title: OUKITEL WP35がAliExpressで正式に登場：世界最薄の11000mAh 5Gタフネススマートフォンで高級なダイヤモンド柄のビジネススタイルを提供
summary: OUKITELは、世界最薄の11000mAhバッテリーを搭載した5Gタフネススマートフォン「WP35」をAliExpressで本日発売しました。厚さ14.9mmの本体にMediaTek Dimensityチップセットを搭載し、最大60日間の待機時間、拡張可能なRAMとストレージ、64MPのSony製メインカメラ、8MPのナイトビジョンカメラ、32MPの自撮りカメラ、そしてGorilla Glass5で保護された6.6インチ2.4Kディスプレイを備えています。IP68/IP69K/MIL-STD-810H認証も取得しており、ビジネス用途からアウトドア活動まで幅広く対応する堅牢性とデザイン性を両立させています。

title: PNQI: I'm Buying This Tech Stock ETF Instead Of QQQ (NASDAQ:PNQI)
summary: The Invesco NASDAQ Internet ETF (PNQI) is a tech-focused ETF with $751 million in assets and a diversified portfolio of about 80 positions. It has been in an uptrend since late last year and holds significant stakes in Alphabet, Amazon, and Apple. The author, a long-time stock market investor, is buying PNQI, expressing their own opinions and having a beneficial long position in its shares.

title: 3 Funds With Double-Digit Stakes in a Single Stock
summary: This article examines three equity funds––Fidelity Growth Company (FDGRX), American Century Ultra (TWCUX), and Akre Focus (AKREX)––that hold unusually high, double-digit stakes in single stocks, highlighting the risks associated with such concentrated portfolios. While some funds like Fidelity Growth Company have performed exceptionally due to these bold allocations to companies like Nvidia and Apple, others like Akre Focus demonstrate unique, sometimes unusual, concentrations in stocks like Mastercard and Constellation Software. The author notes that such high concentrations, once rare, are becoming more common, but pose meaningful risk for investors.

title: Apple’s Planning a $110B Stock Buyback. Here's Why. | Poole Thought Leadership
summary: Apple Inc. announced a significant $110 billion stock buyback plan, prompting Poole College finance professor Richard Warr to explain the rationale behind such corporate actions. Warr details why companies conduct buybacks, including investing extra cash, buying undervalued stock, restoring share value, and tweaking the debt-equity balance. He also clarifies that while stock prices often rise post-announcement, buybacks don't magically increase overall company value, but rather signal management confidence to investors.

title: Google Glass Making A Comeback? New Prototype AR Glasses Make A Cameo Appearance At I/O 2024
summary: Google revealed a prototype of new augmented reality (AR) glasses at its I/O 2024 conference, sparking speculation about a revival of the Google Glass project. The new prototype was shown in a demo video for Project Astra, worn by an employee, and suggests Google is actively pursuing its AR ambitions amidst a growing AR market. This comes after Apple's Vision Pro launch and the success of Meta's smart glasses.

title: Buffett’s Berkshire Reveals $6.7 Billion Stake in Insurer Chubb
summary: Warren Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway has revealed a $6.7 billion stake in insurer Chubb Ltd., a position it had secretly built since 2023. The disclosure ended months of speculation about Berkshire's mystery investment in a financial firm, causing Chubb's stock to jump. This investment highlights Berkshire's ongoing focus on the insurance industry, which Buffett considers the "core" of his conglomerate.

title: Secret’s out: Buffett’s Berkshire Hathaway a Chubb shareholder
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has revealed a significant US$6.7 billion investment in global insurer Chubb, making it Berkshire's ninth biggest holding by the end of Q1 2024. This stake was kept confidential for three quarters with regulatory approval. The disclosure comes as Berkshire Hathaway reported strong Q1 operating earnings and a record cash pile, while Chubb also announced impressive Q1 financial results.

title: Regulatory Risks, Investment Sentiment and the India/Bharat Conundrum
summary: The article discusses the economic and regulatory landscape in India following the anticipated re-election of the BJP government led by Prime Minister Narendra Modi. It highlights India's attractiveness to global investors due to policy continuity, economic growth, and a focus on infrastructure, tax reform, and digital regulations. The authors emphasize the "India-Bharat Conundrum," reflecting the diverse market opportunities between urban and semi-urban/rural areas, and underscore the need for investors to understand the competitive and dynamic market to succeed.

title: This Top Dow ETF Is Crushing the Nasdaq and the S&P 500 Over the Last Month. Here's What That Means for the Market Rally.
summary: The article discusses the recent outperformance of the Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF (DIA) compared to the Nasdaq and S&P 500 over the last month. It suggests this trend indicates investors are becoming more defensive and rebalancing portfolios towards value and income, and away from growth stocks. The author recommends the SPDR Dow Industrial Average ETF as a low-cost option for investors seeking to increase exposure to blue-chip stocks and diversify beyond tech-heavy portfolios.

title: Opinion: Apple has lost its shine. Here’s what can help it regain luster.
summary: Apple, historically a Wall Street and Silicon Valley favorite, is experiencing a downturn with its stock price trailing the broader market this year. The company faces concerns regarding its AI competitiveness and regulatory pressures, leading to questions about whether it has lost its way. An upcoming AI announcement at next month’s WWDC presents an opportunity for Apple to revitalize its narrative, though it also carries the risk of further highlighting product development concerns.

title: This Might Just Be YieldMax’s Craziest ETF Yet (104.56% Dividend Yield)
summary: YieldMax has introduced a new ETF, ULTY, which offers an impressive 104.56% dividend yield. The company is known for its synthetic covered call strategy on individual stocks, generating high double-digit to triple-digit dividend yields. This strategy has led to significant interest from income investors and continues to drive the launch of new products.

title: Jabil replaces CEO after investigation, pulls 2025 forecast
summary: Jabil announced that CEO Kenneth Wilson is immediately leaving the company following an investigation related to corporate policies, and finance chief Michael Dastoor will succeed him. The electronic components maker, which counts Apple among its customers, also withdrew its fiscal 2025 forecast due to the unexpected CEO transition. The company reiterated that the investigation does not relate to or impact its financial statements or reporting.

title: Sonos Picks a Good Time to Plug Into Headphones
summary: Sonos is entering the headphone market with its new Ace headphones, set to release in June. This move comes as the premium-headphone market is experiencing significant growth, while Sonos's traditional speaker sales are declining. The company faces stiff competition, particularly from Apple, in this new venture.

title: Sonos’ Lead Designers Get Nerdy About the New Ace ANC Headphones
summary: Sonos is launching its first active noise-cancellation (ANC) headphones, Ace, on June 5, priced at $449. The lead designers, Dana Krieger and David Keating, highlight the headphones' premium design, comfort-focused engineering, and a unique "TV Audio Swap" feature that allows instant audio transfer from Sonos soundbars. Despite entering a saturated market, Sonos aims to differentiate Ace with its specialized approach to wearable audio and ecosystem integration.

title: Cynthia Bowman Departs Bank Of America, Named Apple Inc’s New Head Of Diversity
summary: Cynthia Bowman, previously from Bank of America, has been appointed as Apple Inc.'s new head of diversity, becoming the fourth individual to hold this position. She will report to Apple’s chief people officer and support the company's diversity initiatives. Bowman's appointment comes as Apple continues its efforts to increase the representation of diverse groups within its workforce.

title: TSMC sees annual sales growth to reach 10% in semiconductor industry
summary: TSMC, a key supplier to Apple and Nvidia, predicts a 10% annual revenue growth for the global semiconductor industry, excluding memory chips, highlighting AI as a "new golden age of opportunity." This forecast is slightly lower than their previous prediction but aligns with their expectation of up to 30% sales growth in the second quarter due to strong demand for AI-related semiconductors. Nvidia's recent strong revenue forecast and stock split further underscore the booming AI sector.

title: Live Nation Entertainment Stock Tumbles as DOJ Announces Antitrust Suit
summary: The Department of Justice filed an antitrust lawsuit against Live Nation Entertainment, alleging monopolistic practices that harm fans, artists, and venues. The DOJ seeks to break up the company, causing Live Nation's stock to tumble 8% following the announcement. Live Nation denies the claims, arguing its profitability is low and its commission rates are competitive.

title: JAVS Courtroom Recording Software Backdoored - Deploys RustDoor Malware
summary: Malicious actors have backdoored the installer for Justice AV Solutions (JAVS) courtroom video recording software to distribute malware known as RustDoor, a supply chain attack tracked as CVE-2024-4978. The compromised installer, JAVS Viewer v8.3.7, was signed with an unexpected Authenticode signature, leading to the deployment of a malicious executable that contacts a command-and-control server and attempts to gather credentials. JAVS has acknowledged a "potential security issue," pulled the affected version, and advised users to check for indicators of compromise and re-image affected systems.

title: I just tried the Sonos Arc soundbar and it's now my new at-home audio system
summary: The author, initially not a fan of soundbars, declares the premium Sonos Arc their new favorite at-home audio system despite its $800+ price tag. They praise its Dolby Atmos support, superior audio quality with 5.0.2 channels, and Trueplay tuning capabilities, which adapt the sound to the room. However, drawbacks include the lack of conventional Bluetooth, Wi-Fi reconnection issues, and the high cost, though these are outweighed by its performance and expandability with other Sonos products.

title: The Justice Department is suing Ticketmaster and Live Nation. What does that mean for concertgoers?
summary: The Justice Department is suing Live Nation Entertainment, alleging it operates as a monopoly controlling much of the U.S. ticket-selling market, thereby driving up prices and stifling competition. While the lawsuit aims to increase competition and potentially lower ticket prices for consumers, experts suggest changes will not be immediate due to the lengthy legal process. The 2022 uproar over Taylor Swift's Eras Tour ticket sales issues highlighted the company's dominance and contributed to the current legal action.

title: As Apple's iPhone 16 Launch Nears, Report Says More Than Half Of World's Smartphones Will Be AI Capable By 2028: Here Are Top 5 Use Cases
summary: A new report by Canalys predicts that by 2028, over half of the world's smartphones will be AI-capable, driven by advancements in chipset technology and consumer demand. This surge is anticipated ahead of Apple's iPhone 16 launch, which is expected to feature significant AI integration. The article highlights five key AI use cases on phones, including generative AI, communication features, and productivity tools.

title: New AI-Powered Trip Planner from InterContinental Hotels Group to Offer Personalized Travel Recommendations
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG) is partnering with Google Cloud to launch an AI-powered travel planning feature within its IHG One Rewards mobile app. This new tool, powered by Google's Vertex AI and Gemini models, will offer personalized travel recommendations, help users build itineraries, and enable direct hotel bookings. The initiative aims to enhance the digital guest experience and customer loyalty, leveraging Google's AI capabilities and IHG's extensive hotel data.

title: TCW Transform 500 ETF Review and Aggregate Rating (VOTE)
summary: The TCW Transform 500 ETF (VOTE) has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy" and an aggregate price target of $80.22 based on analyst ratings for its underlying holdings. The ETF's portfolio includes major companies like NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon, with a significant portion of the portfolio composed of companies covered by 861 analyst ratings in the past year. These ratings and price targets are derived by MarketBeat from individual stock ratings within the ETF's holdings.

title: Apple Supplier STMicroelectronics to Build $5.4 Billion Chip Plant in Italy
summary: STMicroelectronics, a key supplier for Apple, Tesla, and Samsung, is investing approximately $5.42 billion to build a new chip-manufacturing plant in Catania, Sicily, with state support. This facility will produce silicon-carbide semiconductor products, essential for electric vehicles and data centers, as the company prepares for an anticipated recovery in the semiconductor market. The investment aims to expand production capacity for these energy-efficient chips.

title: Road Mapping Leads to Dead End, PTAB Litigation Blog | Insights
summary: The PTAB denied Masimo Corporation’s second petition for inter partes review against U.S. Patent No. 10,076,257. Applying the General Plastic factors, the PTAB found that factors 4 and 5 weighed in favor of denial, and factor 3 weighed strongly in favor of denial. This decision follows Masimo Corp. v. Apple Inc., IPR2024-00071.

title: These San Diego and California companies made the 2024 For 500 list
summary: California leads the nation with 57 companies on the 2024 Fortune 500 list, marking the first time in a decade it has surpassed Texas and New York. Three San Diego companies made the list: Qualcomm (No. 117), Sempra (No. 246), and LPL Financial Holdings (No. 392), despite Qualcomm's revenue decrease and layoffs. Nine new California-based companies joined the list, while others like Petco and Illumina are being watched for future inclusion.

title: AirPods Max vs. Sonos Ace: premium personal audio, compared
summary: This article compares Apple's AirPods Max with Sonos' new Ace headphones, analyzing their design, physical controls, audio processing, connectivity, battery life, and cases. While the AirPods Max are aging, the Sonos Ace offers a compelling alternative with superior comfort, sound quality, and USB-C connectivity, posing a challenge for Apple in the premium personal audio market. The author suggests Apple needs to integrate more features seen in its newer products to remain competitive.

title: Arm aims to win 50% of PC market in five years: Haas
summary: Arm Holdings PLC CEO Rene Haas announced the company's goal to capture over 50% of the Windows PC market within five years. This ambition is fueled by Microsoft's commitment to use Arm-based chips for its new line of AI-powered PCs and the development of robust software tools for Arm-based systems, aiming to challenge Intel's long-standing dominance in the PC industry. Apple's success with its Arm-based "M Series" processors in terms of battery life and performance is cited as a precedent.

title: Dexcom launches G7 with Apple Watch integration in the U.S.
summary: Dexcom has launched its G7 continuous glucose monitor (CGM) with direct Apple Watch integration in the U.S., making it the first and only CGM with this feature. This allows users to receive real-time glucose readings and alerts on their Apple Watch without needing their iPhone, offering greater freedom and convenience. The feature is also available in the UK and Ireland, with more markets expected soon.

title: Humane, the startup behind the AI Pin, in talks with HP, telecoms to sell
summary: Humane, the AI hardware startup founded by ex-Apple designers, is reportedly seeking a buyer following the lukewarm reception of its AI Pin. The company is in discussions with HP and several telecom companies and has enlisted investment bank Tidal Partners to advise on a potential deal. The AI Pin, intended as a smartphone replacement, faced criticism from reviewers for being untrustworthy and not very useful.

title: Nvidia overtakes Apple as No. 2 most valuable company
summary: Nvidia's valuation has surpassed $3 trillion, making it the world's second most valuable company, overtaking Apple. The AI chipmaker's stock surge is attributed to high demand for its processors among tech giants building AI capabilities. This shift highlights Nvidia's dominance in the current AI landscape, while Apple faces challenges with iPhone demand and AI integration.

title: What to Know Heading into FTSE Russell’s 36th Annual Russell Reconstitution
summary: FTSE Russell's 36th Annual Russell Reconstitution is a crucial process that rebalances its U.S. benchmark indices to reflect changes in the equity market. This multi-week event culminates in "Recon Day" where investment managers trade over $100 billion in equities, offering unique opportunities for volatility harvesting through options strategies. The article highlights the Russell 2000 Index, its unique characteristics, and how Cboe's RVX Index and options products can be utilized during this period.

title: Almost 300 Taiwanese students have come due to TSMC plants
summary: Nearly 300 Taiwanese students have enrolled in Phoenix schools, predominantly with the Deer Valley Unified School District, as their parents relocate to work for Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC). The students began arriving in waves in November 2022, with TSMC chartering planes to facilitate their transfer. TSMC's expansion in North Phoenix, including three planned semiconductor facilities, is expected to bring well-paying jobs and diversify the Valley's community.

title: Private Cloud Compute: A new frontier for AI privacy in the cloud
summary: Apple introduces Private Cloud Compute (PCC), a new cloud intelligence system designed for private AI processing with Apple Intelligence. PCC extends Apple's industry-leading device security and privacy to the cloud, ensuring personal user data remains inaccessible to anyone but the user, including Apple. The system is built with custom Apple silicon and a hardened operating system, featuring stateless computation, enforceable guarantees, no privileged access, non-targetability, and verifiable transparency for security researchers.

title: Who are the highest-paid S&P 500 CEOs of 2023?
summary: The median total compensation for S&P 500 CEOs increased by 12.6% in 2023, reaching $16.3 million, largely due to stock awards. Broadcom Inc CEO Hock Tan was the highest-paid chief executive with over $161 million, significantly more than other top earners like Fair Isaac Corporation CEO William Lansing and Apple CEO Tim Cook. This surge in CEO pay outpaced the 5.2% increase for the median S&P 500 employee, further widening the CEO-to-employee pay ratio.

title: New features come to Apple services this fall
summary: Apple is rolling out significant updates to its services this fall with iOS 18, iPadOS 18, macOS Sequoia, watchOS 11, visionOS 2, and tvOS 18. Key enhancements include new features in Apple Maps for exploring national parks and custom routes, greater flexibility in Apple Pay with rewards and installments, and a redesigned Apple Fitness+ experience. Apple Music also gains SharePlay improvements and accessibility features like Music Haptics, while Apple TV introduces InSight and smarter dialogue enhancement.

title: Affirm’s BNPL And Tap to Cash Are Coming to Apple Pay Later This Year
summary: Apple is expanding its financial services with new features for iOS18, including "Tap to Cash" for iPhone-to-iPhone transfers and broader Apple Pay availability for third-party browsers. Notably, Affirm's buy now, pay later service will integrate into Apple Pay later this year, allowing users to apply for loans directly at checkout. Apple is also enhancing reward redemption and installment loan offers within Apple Pay, building on its own Apple Pay Later service launched in 2023.

title: Ozempic Maker Novo Nordisk Closes In On Lego As Denmark's Most Valuable Brand: Report
summary: Pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk is rapidly closing in on Lego A/S for the title of Denmark's most valuable brand, driven by the immense global popularity of its weight-loss drugs, Ozempic and Wegovy. While Lego saw a modest 3% brand value increase to $8 billion, Novo Nordisk's brand value surged by 59% to $5.1 billion, significantly impacting Denmark's GDP and making it Europe's most valuable company. Despite this growth, both companies remain far behind global leader Apple Inc., valued at $517 billion.

title: Adobe Responds to AI Fears With Plans for Updated Legal Terms
summary: Adobe Systems Inc. faced backlash from customers over updated terms of use, prompting a reevaluation of how the company communicates legal issues, especially concerning generative AI. General counsel Dana Rao emphasized that legal terms must evolve with technology. This incident highlights heightened scrutiny on terms of service as technology advances.

title: Apple adds BNPL offerings from rivals
summary: Apple is expanding its buy now, pay later (BNPL) offerings on Apple Pay by integrating services from rivals like Affirm and flexible financing options from debit and credit cards issued by Citi, Fiserv, and Synchrony. This move represents a shift from Apple's traditional "walled garden" strategy, potentially influenced by recent antitrust pressures and fines regarding third-party payment restrictions. While Affirm does not expect a material impact on its fiscal year 2025 revenue or gross merchandise volume from this partnership, it provides users with more payment choices and integrates with Apple Pay's security features.

title: Apple reigns again as most valuable U.S. company for first time since January
summary: Apple Inc. has once again become the most valuable U.S. company, surpassing Microsoft Corp. for the first time since January. The company ended Thursday's trading session with a market capitalization of $3.285 trillion after its stock rose 0.6%. Apple shares have advanced 10.9% over the past three sessions, adding $324 billion to its market cap.

title: Semiconductors Hit Record Highs As Industry Reaches $6.3 Trillion: 5 Chip Stocks That Outpaced Nvidia This Week
summary: The semiconductor industry has reached a record $6.3 trillion valuation, driven by AI advancements, with the PHLX Semiconductor Sector Index showing significant gains. While Nvidia continues to dominate in market capitalization, several other chip stocks, including Broadcom and Arm Holdings, significantly outperformed Nvidia's weekly gains. This indicates a broader bullish trend in the semiconductor sector beyond just the largest players.

title: What is Gorilla Glass? Know the Differences Between Generations
summary: This article explains the evolution and differences between various generations of Corning's Gorilla Glass, a toughened alkali-aluminosilicate glass used in smartphones and other devices. It details how each version brought improvements in scratch resistance, drop protection, or thickness, from the original Gorilla Glass 1 on the first iPhone to the latest Gorilla Armor found in the Galaxy S24 Ultra and Gorilla Glass 7i for budget devices. The article also provides guidance on how users can determine if their phone uses Gorilla Glass.

title: Harmonix co-founder Alex Rigopulos and esteemed lawyer Priyanka Khimani join Songtradr board
summary: Songtradr has appointed Alex Rigopulos, co-founder of Harmonix, and Priyanka Khimani, a prominent entertainment lawyer, to its board of directors. Rigopulos brings deep understanding of music and technology, having been instrumental in games like Rock Band and Guitar Hero. Khimani, known for her work with high-profile clients and commitment to artists' rights, will add significant legal expertise. These appointments are expected to bolster Songtradr's strategic growth and innovation in the global music marketplace.

title: Trade the AI Trend With This Pair of Leveraged ETFs
summary: This article discusses how investors can capitalize on the booming artificial intelligence (AI) trend using leveraged ETFs. It highlights companies like Nvidia and Apple as key players in the AI space and introduces two Direxion leveraged ETFs, AIBU and AIBD, which offer 200% exposure to the Solactive US AI & Big Data Index for both upside and inverse plays. These ETFs provide traders with flexible options to express short-term convictions in the AI-big data sector.

title: Trade the AI Trend With This Pair of Leveraged ETFs
summary: This article discusses how investors can capitalize on the artificial intelligence (AI) trend using leveraged ETFs. It highlights companies like Nvidia and Apple as key players in the AI space and introduces two Direxion leveraged ETFs, AIBU and AIBD, which offer 200% exposure or inverse exposure to the Solactive US AI & Big Data Index, enabling traders to react to short-term market convictions.

title: Apple Original Films’ Formula 1® feature to be released on 25 June 2025 in partnership with Warner Bros. Pictures
summary: Apple Original Films' Formula 1 movie, starring Brad Pitt and directed by Joseph Kosinski, will be released internationally on June 25, 2025, and in North America on June 27, 2025. The film is a collaboration with Formula 1, filmed during actual Grand Prix events, and features Brad Pitt as a former driver alongside Damson Idris as his rookie teammate in a fictional team called APXGP. Production will continue at several races, including the British Grand Prix, and conclude in Abu Dhabi.

title: A major tech ETF is set to load up on Nvidia at the expense of Apple
summary: The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK), a major tech ETF, is poised to significantly increase its Nvidia holdings while reducing its Apple stake during its quarterly rebalance. This strategic shift comes as Nvidia has surpassed Apple to become the world's second-largest tech company. The rebalance will involve the purchase of $11 billion in Nvidia shares and the sale of $12 billion in Apple shares by State Street, the ETF's manager.

title: Nvidia becomes world’s most valuable company, dethroning Microsoft
summary: Nvidia has dethroned Microsoft to become the world's most valuable company, with its market capitalization reaching $3.335 trillion. The chipmaker's stock price surged by 3.5 percent to $135.58, driven by the strong demand for its GPUs essential for artificial intelligence development. This milestone follows Nvidia's consistent rise, fueled by its dominant market share in AI chips and strategic investments by CEO Jensen Huang.

title: Nvidia Beats Apple, Microsoft to Become Most Valuable Company On Earth
summary: Nvidia has surpassed Apple and Microsoft to become the world's most valuable company, with its market capitalization reaching $3.34 trillion, driven by explosive growth in the AI sector. The company's stock surge was fueled by an increased presence in the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund and an optimistic price target from Rosenblatt Securities, highlighting its dominant position in AI chip technology. Nvidia's value has soared nearly tenfold since early 2023, solidifying its lead among the 'Magnificent Seven' technology stocks.

title: 3 ETFs to Watch as Tech Looks Unlikely to Slow Down
summary: As the hype around artificial intelligence continues to drive investor interest, the momentum of tech stocks appears set to continue an upward trend. Direxion Investments offers three leveraged ETFs for traders looking to capitalize on this ongoing growth. These ETFs provide exposure to broad technology, AI and big data specifically, and the semiconductor industry, including key players like Nvidia.

title: Insulet fully launches Omnipod 5 with Dexcom G7 in the U.S. along with iPhone app
summary: Insulet has fully launched its Omnipod 5 automated insulin delivery system with Dexcom G7 continuous glucose monitor integration in the U.S., alongside a limited release of an iPhone app. The company also expanded Omnipod 5 availability in Europe, including France, the UK, and the Netherlands, offering compatibility with various CGM sensors like Dexcom G6 and Abbott FreeStyle Libre 2 Plus. These developments aim to improve access and flexibility for people with Type 1 diabetes globally.

title: Working with Elavon, Delta Adopts Tap to Pay on iPhone for U.S. Flights
summary: Delta Air Lines is implementing Tap to Pay on iPhone for its U.S. flights, in collaboration with processor Elavon Inc. This change allows flight attendants to use their existing iPhones for payment acceptance, eliminating the need for separate point-of-sale devices. Delta has been a long-time client of Elavon, a unit of U.S. Bank, which has over 1.3 million customers.

title: Apple Intelligence won't launch in EU in 2024 due to antitrust regulation, company says
summary: Apple announced that its Apple Intelligence AI product and three other new features will not be launched in the European Union in 2024. This delay is attributed to "regulatory uncertainties" stemming from the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), which Apple believes could compromise user privacy and data security due to interoperability requirements. Apple stated it would work with the EU to find a solution that allows the features to be rolled out without compromising safety.

title: IWF Is a Great Choice for Most, But I Like VUG ETF Better
summary: This article compares the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) and the Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG), both popular growth stock ETFs. While IWF is suitable for most, the author prefers VUG due to its lower expense ratio and historically better returns. The article details their holdings, costs, and performance differences, recommending VUG for investors seeking enhanced growth exposure.

title: Apple is first tech company accused of violating EU's new competition law
summary: The European Union has accused Apple of violating its new Digital Markets Act, making Apple the first tech company to face charges under the law. The investigation focuses on Apple's app store rules, which allegedly hinder developers from directing consumers to alternative purchasing methods. Apple has a chance to respond before a final decision in March 2025, potentially facing fines up to 20% of its global revenue for repeated infringements.

title: Collective Audience CEO, Peter Bordes, Appointed to Symitri’s All-Star Board of Digital Tech Executives and Investors
summary: Collective Audience, Inc. CEO Peter Bordes has been appointed to the board of directors of Symitri, a new company focused on integrated privacy firewalls and real-time data cleanrooms for the open web. Bordes brings over 30 years of experience in AdTech, media, AI, fintech, and technology, having founded and led numerous companies. Symitri aims to balance the economic viability of open-web advertising with consumer privacy, and Bordes joins other tech executives like Keith Bank and Jeff White to advance this mission.

title: What Exactly Does NVIDIA Do, and Why Should You Care?
summary: This article provides an introduction to NVIDIA, highlighting its transformation from a GPU manufacturer to a systems company offering hardware and an ecosystem for developers. It delves into the factors driving NVIDIA's success, its significant impact on the customer experience market through AI, and key partnerships with companies like Cisco, SAP, and ServiceNow. The piece emphasizes NVIDIA's role in advancing AI for personalized and intelligent customer interactions across various sectors.

title: A Shakeup for Technology ETFs
summary: The Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) recently underwent a significant rebalance, swapping a large position in Apple for Nvidia. This rebalance highlights the complexities of index rules, particularly the RIC diversification test, and its impact on ETF holdings. The article discusses how passive indexing can sometimes appear active due to various exceptions and weighting methodologies, influencing ETF performance and investor perceptions.

title: What’s the Secret Sauce in this High-Income Fund?
summary: The article explores the success of Fidelity Tactical High Income Sr F, managed by Adam Kramer, highlighting its consistent category-beating performance. Kramer attributes the fund's "secret sauce" to a flexible mandate across various income-oriented asset classes, the ability to preserve capital in down markets, and skillful matching of bonds and stocks. The fund's strategy involves actively adjusting asset class weightings and leveraging expertise from a specialized team to identify the best risk-reward opportunities across the capital structure.

title: How Global Regulators Are Catching Up With Temu
summary: Global regulators, lawmakers, and consumers are increasingly scrutinizing Temu's e-commerce platform due to concerns about its data collection practices.
