summary: Stephen Balaban, CEO of Lambda, discusses their partnership with SK Telecom (SKT) to expand into the AI infrastructure market, including the launch of a GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) in Seoul. Balaban highlights Korea's fast-paced AI innovation and emphasizes Lambda's strategy of building the best product for customers. He will also deliver a keynote speech at the "SK AI Summit 2024" discussing shifts in AI and the growing importance of open models and GPUs.

title: BlackRock launches ETF that expands beyond the ‘Magnificent Seven'
summary: BlackRock's iShares has launched the iShares Top 20 U.S. Stocks ETF (TOPT) to offer investors diversification beyond the "Magnificent Seven" tech giants. This new ETF comprises the 20 largest U.S. stocks by market capitalization, aiming to provide a broader and more diversified investment in leading U.S. companies. The move comes as some investors worry about the concentration of the Magnificent Seven in the S&P 500 and their high valuations.

title: Apple Earnings: Our Valuation Rises to Reflect Stronger Long-Term Growth, but Less of an AI Impact
summary: Morningstar has raised its fair value estimate for Apple to $200 from $185, driven by stronger longer-term growth expectations, particularly from iPhone unit sales and the services segment, along with expected margin expansion. However, the short-term forecast for iPhone revenue has been lowered due to a perceived lesser impact from AI on unit sales in the immediate future, leading to the conclusion that shares are currently overvalued. The article notes a positive inflection in iPhone revenue in the September quarter and continued strong growth in services.

title: Apple accused by NLRB of deterring employees from discussing pay equity
summary: The National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) has filed a complaint accusing Apple of preventing employees from discussing pay equity and forcing an engineer to quit after she circulated a wage survey. This marks the third such complaint in a month against Apple, which is also alleged to have barred a Slack channel for pay equity discussions and restricted employees' use of social media and workplace messaging. Apple denies the claims, stating that it strongly disagrees and will share the facts at the hearing.

title: CoreCard Corporation (NYSE:CCRD) Q3 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: CoreCard Corporation (NYSE:CCRD) reported better-than-expected Q3 2024 results, with total revenue up 17% year-over-year to $15.7 million, driven by higher license, professional services, and processing/maintenance revenues. The company provided 2025 guidance forecasting total revenue between $60 million and $64 million, and earnings per share between $0.88 and $0.94, with revenue growth excluding Goldman Sachs anticipated at 30% to 40%. CEO Leland Strange also discussed the secured Goldman Sachs agreement through 2026, the ongoing development of their CoreFi platform, and the company's share repurchase program, reiterating that the stock is undervalued.

title: Nvidia surpasses Apple to become world’s most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has overtaken Apple to become the world's most valuable company, with its market capitalization reaching $3.43 trillion. This shift highlights the increasing dominance of artificial intelligence in the technology sector, as Nvidia's shares have nearly tripled in 2024. The AI darling's stock closed at $139.91 on Tuesday.

title: Nvidia passes Apple as world's most valuable company
summary: Nvidia has once again surpassed Apple to become the world's most valuable publicly traded company, reaching a market cap of $3.43 trillion. This comes as Nvidia's stock has nearly tripled in 2024 due to high demand for its GPUs in the AI market, while Apple's shares are up about 17% this year. Nvidia is also set to join the Dow Industrial Average, replacing Intel.

title: SolarEdge Announces Changes to the Board of Directors
summary: SolarEdge Technologies, Inc. announced changes to its Board of Directors, electing Avery More as Chairman and appointing Guy Gecht as a new member. Gecht, bringing extensive experience from the technology and cybersecurity sectors, will replace Tal Payne, who stepped down. The company expressed confidence that these changes will strengthen the Board and guide future innovation and growth.

title: Analysts Say ‘We Would Be Aggressive Buyers on Any Pullbacks’ in Microsoft Stock. Should You Be Too?
summary: Despite a slight dip after its latest earnings report due to increased capital expenditures, Microsoft (MSFT) is viewed by analysts, including Morgan Stanley's Keith Weiss, as a strong buy on any pullbacks. The company exceeded Q1 earnings expectations across all segments, particularly in Intelligent Cloud (Azure), and analysts project continued strong growth for its stock. With 41 out of 48 analysts recommending a "Strong Buy" and an average price target implying a 27% upside, the sentiment suggests that Microsoft's AI advancements and robust financial performance make it an attractive investment despite market fluctuations.

title: After Earnings, Is Apple Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Morningstar raised its fair value estimate for Apple to $200 per share, reflecting better long-term growth expectations despite trimming short-term estimates due to lower anticipated AI impact on iPhone sales. Despite the upward revision, Morningstar considers Apple's stock overvalued, citing growth headwinds in the mature smartphone market and increased competition. The services business remains a key growth driver, and Apple maintains a wide economic moat due to its strong ecosystem, and financial strength, though it faces risks from consumer reliance and geopolitical supply chain issues.

title: Is Apple Inc. (AAPL) a Good Stock to Buy Now According to Redditors?
summary: This article examines Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) as a stock to buy according to Redditors, ranking it 5th on a list of top trending stocks. It discusses recent market trends, the impact of AI on mega-cap stocks, and strategies for hedging risks. The article highlights Apple's strong fundamentals, cash reserves, and growth potential, particularly with its new Apple Intelligence platform, citing positive analyst sentiments and a record-breaking September quarter.

title: World's Largest Technology Company by Revenue, world record set by Apple Inc.
summary: Apple Inc. has set a world record for being the World's Largest Technology Company by Revenue, with US$383.29 billion in revenues in 2023. The company, founded in 1976, is headquartered in Cupertino, California, and is known for its consumer electronics, software, and services. Apple has also been recognized as the world's most valuable brand and the largest company by market capitalization.

title: Intel fuels R&D Surge: Chip wars amid Asian AI expansion
summary: The R&D World Index saw a modest climb, driven by Intel's gains and strategic expansion in Asia, while Honda experienced setbacks. The article highlights significant R&D developments including CATL's advancements in solid-state batteries, Apple's new subsidiary in India, TSMC facing U.S. export restrictions on AI chips to China, and DuPont's growth fueled by AI-based semiconductor products. It also touches on Toyota's revised sales forecast and China's large debt rescue package.

title: Apple’s Next Device Is an AI Wall Tablet for Home Control, Siri and Video Calls
summary: Apple is preparing to launch a new wall-mounted AI tablet, code-named J490, designed to be a smart home command center. The device will control appliances, handle video calls, and leverage the new Apple Intelligence AI platform, positioning Apple to compete with rivals like Amazon and Google in the smart home market. A more advanced, robotic version with an AI personality is also in development, potentially priced up to $1,000.

title: Top 22 US Companies in 2026 (Updated List)
summary: This article lists and describes the top 22 US companies in 2026, highlighting their market capitalization as of February 2024 and the reasons for their success and popularity. It covers tech giants like Apple and Microsoft, e-commerce leader Amazon, financial services providers like Visa and Mastercard, and pharmaceutical and energy companies. The article aims to inform readers about the key players in the American business landscape and what makes them significant.

title: Warren Buffett reveals craving for Domino’s, while cutting back on Apple — on Berkshire’s investment portfolio
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway made new investments in Domino’s Pizza and Pool Corp. in the third quarter of 2024, while simultaneously reducing its stakes in Apple and Bank of America. The company significantly increased its cash holdings to $325.2 billion and sold $36.1 billion of stocks, signaling a cautious approach amidst potentially high valuations. Berkshire maintained its diversified portfolio, adding to Heico and exiting Floor & Decor and most of Ulta Beauty.

title: New Snapchat feature targets families with teens
summary: Snapchat is introducing new live location sharing and "Travel Notification" features within its Family Center, aimed at enhancing safety for parents and teens. Parents can request their teen's live location and receive alerts when teens arrive at or depart specific locations. These features, which emphasize user consent and privacy settings, are rolling out in the coming weeks and build upon Snapchat's existing Snap Map functionality.

title: Berkshire bites into Domino's Pizza, dips into Pool as stock retreat
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway announced new investments in Domino's Pizza and Pool Corp in the third quarter, while simultaneously reducing its holdings in stocks like Apple and Bank of America. The conglomerate also significantly increased its cash reserves to $325.2 billion and halted stock repurchases, suggesting a cautious outlook on current market valuations, while still preserving capacity for a major acquisition.

title: Berkshire bites into Domino's Pizza, dips into Pool amid stock retreat
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway disclosed new investments in Domino's Pizza and Pool Corp during the third quarter of 2024, despite significantly reducing its overall stock holdings, particularly in Apple and Bank of America. These purchases come as Berkshire nearly doubled its cash reserves to $325.2 billion and halted stock buybacks, suggesting a cautious stance amid high valuations. The new investments caused shares of Domino's and Pool to rise, reflecting investor confidence in Berkshire's selections.

title: Chilton Investment Co LLC Boosts Stake in Apple Inc
summary: Chilton Investment Co LLC, managed by Richard Chilton, has increased its stake in Apple Inc (AAPL) by 26,195 shares. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rates AAPL as "Outperform" due to its strong financial performance and robust revenue growth, despite high valuation and overbought technical indicators. Apple's strategic focus on services and overcoming supply chain issues are seen as positive long-term growth factors.

title: Biden locks in $6.6B for TSMC chip factories, ensuring Trump can’t rescind CHIPS Act deal
summary: The Biden administration has finalized a $6.6 billion award with Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) for its chip factories in Phoenix, along with up to $5 billion in loans, largely to secure the deal against potential changes by President-elect Donald Trump. This investment, part of the CHIPS and Science Act, aims to boost domestic semiconductor manufacturing and create thousands of jobs in Arizona. Despite Trump's previous opposition to similar deals, the finalized contract is binding, ensuring the continuation of a project critical for U.S. economic and national security.

title: FBY: One Of My Favorite Santa's Income Plays
summary: The author sees an opportunity to invest in big tech, particularly META, after a recent market sell-off, citing strong leadership and innovation. FBY, a YieldMax ETF for META, is highlighted as a favorable pick for high dividends and potential capital gains, especially in anticipation of a "Santa rally." The article suggests FBY offers a safer income play through covered call strategies compared to direct stock ownership.

title: Corsair Unveils The First True Gaming Keyboard And Mouse For Macs
summary: Corsair has launched the first true gaming keyboard and mouse designed specifically for Macs, aiming to enhance the gaming experience on Apple devices. The K65 Plus Wireless keyboard and M75 Wireless gaming mouse come in Mac-exclusive colorways and are available through Apple. These peripherals, coupled with Apple's recent focus on gaming and the powerful M4 chip in the Mac mini, suggest a growing potential for Mac gaming.

title: Wearable Devices Ltd. Unveils Mudra Band and Mudra Link: Revolutionary Neural Wristband Technology for Gesture Control
summary: Wearable Devices Ltd. has launched the Mudra Band and Mudra Link, innovative neural wristbands using proprietary Surface Nerve Conductance (SNC) technology for intuitive gesture control of digital devices. These devices aim to enhance user interaction in extended reality (XR) experiences by interpreting neural signals from wrist movements, offering a hands-free interface compatible with various operating systems. The technology allows gesture recognition even when hands are not visible, setting a new standard for user interaction in the rapidly evolving tech landscape.

title: VOOG vs. SCHG: Which Is the Better Growth Stock ETF?
summary: This article compares two popular growth ETFs, the Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (VOOG) and the Schwab U.S. Large Cap Growth ETF (SCHG). While both are strong performers with similar portfolios of top growth stocks like Apple, Microsoft, and Nvidia, SCHG consistently outperforms VOOG in 3, 5, and 10-year returns and boasts a significantly lower expense ratio. The analysis concludes that SCHG is the better choice due to its superior performance and lower costs.

title: Black Friday Arrives at T-Mobile: Unwrap 4 Lines for Only $100/mo, 4 iPhone 16 on Us and More
summary: T-Mobile and Metro by T-Mobile are launching Black Friday and Cyber Monday deals, including special offers on the latest iPhone 16 lineup and other Apple products. Customers can get four lines for $100/month with four iPhone 16s on Us, or other device discounts with eligible trade-ins and plans. The promotions also include an ultimate Apple bundle for Cyber Monday, offering an iPhone 16 Pro, Apple Watch SE 2nd Gen, and AirPods 4 all on Us.

title: America eats the world
summary: This article discusses the unprecedented dominance of US equities in the world market, with the S&P 500 now accounting for two-thirds of the world equity index. It explores how this concentration has occurred, driven by the rise of technology and the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks, and questions whether this momentum is sustainable in the long term, especially considering the shift of these tech giants towards more capital-heavy AI investments. The author uses a "second-level thinking" approach to analyze the drivers and potential challenges to this US market leadership.

title: Under the Hood of Fidelity’s Blue Chip Growth
summary: The Fidelity Blue Chip Growth ETF (FBCG) is an active U.S. equity growth strategy with a large-cap bias that has outperformed its benchmark, the Russel 1000 Growth index. The fund focuses on identifying blue chip companies with sustainable business models, high returns on equity, and strong revenue and earnings growth through bottom-up fundamental analysis. FBCG's portfolio includes both mega-cap companies and smaller emerging growers, aiming for consistent +10% annual EPS growth.

title: ICLV: Value With Some Tech Exposure (NYSEARCA:ILCV)
summary: The iShares Morningstar Value ETF (ILCV) offers a diversified portfolio of 444 large- and mid-cap U.S. stocks with a low expense ratio of 0.04%. It balances traditional value sectors like Financials and Industrials with growth areas such as Information Technology and Health Care. This mix provides pure domestic market exposure and aims for a balanced risk-return profile, despite some concentration in the Financial sector.

title: Billionaire Warren Buffett Pours $752,000,000 Into New Assets After Dumping Apple, Bank of America
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway has invested $752 million into new assets, Domino's (DPZ) and Pool Corp (POOL), while significantly reducing its holdings in Apple (AAPL) and Bank of America (BAC). The firm's 13F filing shows a continued commitment to consumer-focused businesses with competitive advantages. Despite the sales, Apple remains Berkshire's largest holding, followed by American Express and Bank of America.

title: Here's How Much You Would Need to Invest in This Unstoppable Vanguard ETF Today to Grow Your Portfolio to $1 Million or More by Retirement
summary: This article discusses using the Vanguard S&P 500 Growth Index Fund ETF Shares (VOOG) as a vehicle for long-term wealth accumulation, aiming for $1 million by retirement. It highlights the ETF's focus on growth stocks within the S&P 500, its low expense ratio, and historical outperformance. The author provides a table illustrating the investment amounts needed over 30 and 35 years, assuming an 8% annual growth rate, suggesting at least $70,000 for 35 years to reach the $1 million goal.

title: AI: Apple, Oura, and AI 'Smart Rings'. RTZ #550
summary: This article discusses the growing trend of AI-enhanced wearables, focusing on "smart rings" from companies like Oura and their potential integration with glucose monitoring. It highlights Apple's "Apple Intelligence" AI strategy, particularly its focus on healthcare within wearables, and speculates on Apple's potential entry into the smart ring market, possibly through an acquisition of Oura. The strategic partnership between Oura and Dexcom, a leading glucose monitoring company, is presented as a significant development in mainstreaming blood sugar tracking.

title: Corning offers to waive exclusive deals in EU antitrust probe, may stave off fine
summary: Corning has offered to drop all exclusive dealing clauses in its agreements with mobile phone makers and raw glass processing companies to resolve an EU antitrust investigation. This move could help the company avoid a significant fine. The European Commission will now open a six-week feedback period for third parties before deciding whether to accept Corning's concessions, which would last for nine years.

title: Google faces £7 billion UK class action lawsuit over search engine dominance
summary: Google is facing a £7 billion class action lawsuit in the UK, which has been certified for trial after Google's attempt to dismiss it was rejected. The lawsuit, filed by consumer rights advocate Nikki Stopford, accuses Google of abusing its dominant search engine market position to inflate advertising costs passed on to consumers. Google denies the allegations, while simultaneously facing increased scrutiny and legal challenges from regulators globally, including the US Department of Justice.

title: Apple takes another bite of Penn District
summary: Apple has expanded its office footprint at Vornado Realty Trust’s Penn 11 by an additional 61,000 square feet, bringing its total space to 460,000 square feet across 11 floors. This new lease, for space formerly occupied by Macy's, extends Apple's presence in the building until 2035. The deal involved complex negotiations to keep Macy's credit on the hook while establishing a direct relationship with Apple.

title: The S&P 500 (SPY) Is Ready for a Sell-Off
summary: The S&P 500 has gained 25% year-to-date, but this growth is largely driven by a small group of "Magnificent 7" stocks, pushing valuations above historical averages. While the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) is generally recommended for long-term investors due to the market's historical resilience, the current concentration of gains suggests a potential sell-off is due. Despite this, experts advise against shorting the market and suggest that consistently investing in SPY can still lead to long-term gains, as bear markets are typically short-lived and followed by stronger bull markets.

title: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Apple to Lead with TSM’s Advanced 2nm AI Chips in 2025
summary: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Apple are expected to be the first customers for TSMC's advanced 2nm AI chips, with mass production scheduled to begin in 2025. This development highlights the increasing importance of advanced semiconductor technology in the AI sector and positions AMD as a key player among trending AI stocks. The article also touches on significant investments in AI startups and the overall boom in the AI market.

title: 2 no-brainer Warren Buffett stocks to buy into 2025
summary: As 2024 concludes, investors are looking to Warren Buffett's philosophy for guidance. This article highlights two "forever stocks" from Buffett's portfolio, Pool Corporation (NASDAQ: POOL) and Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO), as compelling long-term opportunities for 2025. It examines their recent performance, financial stability, and potential for growth.

title: Should iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW) Be on Your Investing Radar?
summary: The iShares S&P 500 Growth ETF (IVW) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Growth segment of the US equity market, with significant holdings in Information Technology, and top positions in Nvidia, Apple, and Microsoft. With an expense ratio of 0.18% and a "Buy" rating from Zacks, it presents a compelling option for investors seeking growth, despite the inherent volatility of growth stocks. The fund has shown strong performance, up about 34.84% year-to-date and 40% over the last year.

title: Tech stocks hit first all-time high since July
summary: Technology stocks reached new all-time highs on Wednesday, driven by strong performances from key software players like Salesforce, Adobe, and ServiceNow, as well as megacap companies such as Apple, Nvidia, and Amazon. The Nasdaq Composite rallied 1%, and the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) advanced 1.8%, marking its first new high since July. Marvell Technology also saw significant gains, surging 23% after a strong quarter.

title: Tech giant with 30,000 employees has no return-to-office policy
summary: Globant SA, a software company with nearly 30,000 employees across 33 countries, has opted against a mandatory return-to-office policy, differentiating itself from other tech giants. CEO Martin Migoya emphasizes making offices attractive and flexible connection points rather than forcing attendance. The company is redesigning its office spaces to encourage collaboration and is planning to hire another 20,000 employees as part of a US$1-billion investment.

title: Amkor secured north Peoria land for its $2B complex. What's next?
summary: Amkor Technology Inc. has finalized the purchase of 56 acres in north Peoria for its $2 billion microchip assembly and testing facility, which will be the largest of its kind in the U.S. The project, supported by $600 million in CHIPS Act subsidies, aims to create about 2,000 jobs and will serve companies like Apple and TSMC. Construction is slated to begin by September 2025, with the first phase completed by September 2027 and full completion by September 2034.

title: E.l.f. brings shopping to the speakeasy with Virtual Luxe Lounge
summary: E.l.f. Cosmetics has introduced the "Virtual Luxe Lounge," an immersive 3D digital shopping experience accessible to members of its Beauty Squad loyalty program. Developed with Obsess, this speakeasy-themed platform allows users to shop holiday product drops and access exclusive coupons. This initiative is part of E.l.f.'s broader strategy to engage consumers digitally, following previous collaborations like a beauty shopping app for Apple Vision Pro, and aligns with their significant marketing investments that have boosted sales and attracted younger audiences.

title: The Inside Story of Apple Intelligence
summary: This article delves into Apple's strategy for generative AI, dubbed Apple Intelligence, highlighting the company's "best, not first" approach. Key executives, including John Giannandrea and Craig Federighi, explain that Apple has been preparing for this moment for years and focused on integrating AI at a systems level with a strong emphasis on privacy. The piece also contrasts Apple's approach with competitors and discusses the future trajectory of AI at Apple.

title: Report: Apple will ship custom 5G modem chip with the 2025 iPhone SE
summary: Apple Inc.'s custom 5G modem chip, codenamed Sinope, is expected to debut in the 2025 iPhone SE. Produced by TSMC, Sinope offers download speeds of up to 4 gigabits per second, which is less than half of Qualcomm's latest modems due to more limited frequency support and carrier aggregation implementation. Apple plans to integrate Sinope with its Carpo RF front end and aims to release more advanced custom modems in subsequent years, eventually hoping to outperform Qualcomm and integrate modems directly into iPhone processors, as well as extend their use to Macs and the Vision Pro.

title: Apple to bring e-commerce site, stores to Saudi Arabia
summary: Apple is expanding its retail presence in Saudi Arabia, launching an online Apple Store in summer 2025 with Arabic service and support. Following this, the tech giant plans to open its first flagship physical stores in the kingdom starting in 2026, including a notable location in Diriyah. This expansion builds on existing investments, such as the Apple Developer Academy, and aims to deepen connections with Saudi Arabian customers.

title: Taiwan Semiconductor Extends Lead As Top Chipmaker
summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSM) has solidified its position as the top global wafer foundry, increasing its market share to 64.9% in Q3 2024 due to strong demand for smartphone products and high-performance computing. The company reported $23.5 billion in revenue, a 39% increase, driven by 3nm and 5nm technologies. This growth contrasts with Samsung's market share decline and highlights the rising demand for advanced chip technologies and the impact of U.S. chip subsidies on key players like TSM and GlobalFoundries.

title: Jefferies says the Russell 2000 will reach 2,715 by 2025 year-end (NYSEARCA:IWM)
summary: Jefferies analysts predict the Russell 2000 will increase by 13.5% to 2,715 by the end of 2025, anticipating it will finally outperform large-cap stocks after eight years of underperformance. Steven DeSanctis, Jefferies' U.S. small-mid cap strategist, provided this outlook.

title: Apple honors 2024 App Store Award winners
summary: Apple announced the winners of the 2024 App Store Awards, recognizing 17 apps and games for their exceptional user experiences, design, and innovation. The awards include categories for various Apple devices, the inaugural Apple Vision Pro category, and Cultural Impact winners, highlighting developers who have enriched users' lives and communities. The winning selections showcase the diverse opportunities available across the App Store and the Apple ecosystem.

title: Report: Apple working with Broadcom to develop custom chipset for AI
summary: Apple Inc. is reportedly collaborating with Broadcom Inc. to develop a custom server processor, codenamed “Baltra,” for AI features in its iOS operating system, with a targeted launch in 2026. This move aligns with Apple's strategy of using proprietary chips for on-device AI and Broadcom's expertise in semiconductor interconnect designs. The project likely leverages Broadcom's advanced packaging technologies for faster chip-to-chip communications.

title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is favoured by institutional owners who hold 61% of the company
summary: Institutional investors hold 61% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), making them the most powerful shareholder group with significant influence on the company's share price. The Vanguard Group, Inc. is the largest shareholder with 8.9% of shares, while insiders own less than 1% and the general public holds a 37% stake.

title: 6 Best Ways to Redeem Aeroplan Points
summary: This article by NerdWallet Canada outlines six effective strategies for maximizing the value of Aeroplan points. Key recommendations include understanding the average point value, considering partner airlines for better redemption rates, opting for business-class flights, identifying "sweet spot" routes, utilizing family sharing plans, and prioritizing flight redemptions over other options like gift cards or car rentals. The article emphasizes calculating the cents-per-point value to ensure worthwhile redemptions.

title: Sonos Arc Ultra review: The best Dolby Atmos soundbar just got better
summary: The Sonos Arc Ultra soundbar aims to redeem the brand after a problematic app rollout. It significantly upgrades over its predecessor with a 9.1.4-channel configuration, Sound Motion technology for improved audio quality in a more compact design, and Bluetooth support. While a substantial upgrade for new buyers seeking premium home theater sound, current Sonos Arc owners may not find the improvements significant enough to warrant replacement.

title: TSMC says first advanced U.S. chip plant 'dang near back' on schedule. Here's an inside look at the Arizona fab
summary: TSMC's first advanced U.S. chip fabrication plant in Arizona is "dang near back on the original schedule" after delays and an increased investment from $12 billion to $20 billion. The plant will produce 4-nanometer chips and has committed to building two more fabs by the end of the decade, totaling $65 billion. Challenges include securing a skilled workforce, managing significant water and power demands, and global supply chain complexities, but the U.S. Commerce Department finalized $6.6 billion in CHIPS Act funding for the project.

title: Will the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) return another 36% in 2025?
summary: The article discusses the extraordinary 36.34% year-to-date return of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV) in 2024, largely driven by the "magnificent seven" big tech stocks. It explores whether this strong performance is sustainable for 2025, referencing an expert's bullish outlook on US equities due to anticipated earnings growth, healthy economic projections, and falling inflation. The expert suggests that the high valuations of the big tech companies are justified by strong fundamentals.

title: 11%+ Yields From AI Stocks: QQQI Vs. QYLD (NASDAQ:QQQI)
summary: This article compares QQQI and QYLD, two funds designed to generate high yields from mega-cap AI companies, to help income-focused investors choose a suitable diversification instrument. While mega-cap tech stocks typically offer low dividends, these funds aim to provide attractive income opportunities. The analysis details their characteristics and suitability for an income portfolio, recommending that interested investors can access further insights through High Yield Investor's service.

title: Victoria’s Secret Is PETA’s ‘Company of the Year’ for its Fabulously Innovative Feather-Free Fashion Show
summary: PETA has named Victoria’s Secret its 2024 Company of the Year for its innovative use of feather-free materials in its fashion show, showcasing vegan Angel wings made from 3-D printed materials, quilted satin, soda can tabs, and Swarovski crystals. This decision followed PETA's communication with the retailer about the cruelty involved in obtaining feathers from birds. The move is applauded by PETA as an example for designers and consumers to adopt animal-friendly practices in fashion.

title: Pattern Energy Announces Equity Investment from Consortium Headed by APG and ART
summary: Pattern Energy announced that a consortium led by APG Asset Management and Australian Retirement Trust will acquire Riverstone Holdings LLC’s equity stake in the company. This investment strengthens Pattern Energy's position, allowing it to advance its development pipeline of over 25-gigawatts of renewable energy and transmission projects. The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2025.

title: Apple says it has told suppliers to stop buying minerals from Congo
summary: Apple has instructed its suppliers to halt the purchase of tin, tantalum, tungsten, and gold from the Democratic Republic of Congo and Rwanda due to escalating conflict in the region. This decision comes in response to allegations from the Congolese government that Apple uses conflict minerals laundered through international supply chains. Apple strongly disputes these allegations.

title: Apple Supplier Jabil Soars On Q1 Earnings Beat Fueled By Data Center Demand: Retail Gets Bullish
summary: Jabil Inc., an Apple supplier, saw its shares jump by 10% after reporting first-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations. The strong performance was driven by robust demand in its intelligent infrastructure segment, particularly in cloud, data center, and warehouse automation markets, leading to an "extremely bullish" retail sentiment. Despite an overall revenue decline due to a business transition, the company provided an optimistic outlook for Q2 2025 and the full fiscal year.

title: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) is a favorite amongst institutional investors who own 61%
summary: Institutional investors own 61% of Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL), making it a favored stock among this group. While this signifies credibility, the concentration of ownership among a few large investors means their trading actions can significantly impact the stock price. Insiders, despite owning less than 1%, hold a substantial dollar value in shares and have been recent sellers.

title: After Earnings, Is Broadcom Stock a Buy, a Sell, or Fairly Valued?
summary: Broadcom's third-quarter earnings report, released on December 12, 2024, has led Morningstar to raise its fair value estimate for the company's stock to $190 per share, up from $155, driven by an increased AI revenue growth forecast. Despite the upward revision, Morningstar now considers Broadcom's shares to be roughly fairly valued after a significant post-earnings jump, giving it a 2-star rating. The article highlights Broadcom's strong potential in AI chips, its wide economic moat from intangible assets and switching costs, and its financial strength, while also noting risks related to market cycles and supply chain reliance on Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing.

title: JEPQ vs. QQQ: Which ETF Aligns With Your Investment Goals?
summary: This article compares two ETFs, JEPQ and QQQ, both tied to the Nasdaq 100, highlighting their distinct investment strategies. JEPQ focuses on income generation and reduced volatility through covered call strategies, offering a 9.2% annual yield, while QQQ provides direct exposure to the Nasdaq 100 with a focus on growth and higher volatility. The analysis delves into their historical performance, risk metrics, income stability, and suitability for different investment goals, concluding that JEPQ is better for income-seekers and QQQ for growth-oriented investors.

title: Jabil’s Winning Streak Begins: Market Bottom Set, Future in Focus
summary: Jabil's fiscal Q1 2025 report indicates that the company has hit its market bottom, with a strong outlook for future growth despite a top-line contraction. The company outperformed expectations for both revenue and earnings, providing optimistic guidance and benefiting from strength in cloud, data center, and digital commerce segments. Analyst and institutional investor trends are positive, supporting a potential new all-time high for the stock.

title: ‘Synaptics plans to assemble 10% chips in India’
summary: US semiconductor company Synaptics Inc. plans to assemble and package 10% of its chips in India by 2027 to diversify its supply chain and reduce reliance on China. The company will use an OSAT facility in Hyderabad and partner with embed UR to develop edge AI solutions, aiming for the process to begin in late 2025 or early 2026. This move also addresses concerns about proposed US tariffs and the need for supply chain alternatives.

title: Apple to see strong Christmas iPhone sales that launch a ‘renaissance of growth’
summary: Apple is projected to experience a renaissance of growth driven by strong iPhone sales during the holiday season, fueled by an estimated 300 million iPhones over four years old. This potential "supercycle" is expected to lead to significant upgrades to new AI-capable iPhones, especially in the key China market, and could propel Apple to a $4 trillion market cap.

title: Apple approaches $4 trillion valuation as investors bet on AI momentum
summary: Apple is nearing a $4 trillion market valuation, driven by investor enthusiasm for its AI enhancements, which are expected to boost iPhone sales. The company has outpaced Nvidia and Microsoft in this race, with a roughly 16% share jump since November adding about $500 billion to its market cap. Analysts anticipate a rebound in iPhone revenue in 2025 as Apple integrates generative AI technology across its devices.

title: Globalstar director James Monroe III buys $985k in stock By Investing.com
summary: Globalstar director James Monroe III has purchased 500,000 shares of the company's common stock for approximately $985,050. This acquisition comes as Globalstar shows strong momentum with an 87.5% price return over the past six months and reported record Q3 revenue, alongside strategic advancements in 5G technology and satellite connectivity with Apple. The company has also announced plans for a reverse stock split to optimize its capital structure.

title: Evolving the device experience at Microsoft
summary: Microsoft is transitioning its employee device management from a traditional on-premises model to a modern, cloud-based approach using Microsoft Intune, Azure Active Directory, and co-management strategies. This shift supports hybrid work, enables employees to be productive from anywhere on various devices (Windows, Android, iOS, macOS), and enhances security through Zero Trust principles and verified device health. The migration addresses challenges by using Hybrid Azure AD joined devices as a bridge and standardizing management practices.

title: Apple’s been on an uncommonly strong run. So a tough January could now be in the cards.
summary: Apple's stock has achieved a rare five-week streak of 2%-plus gains, a phenomenon that historically precedes a roughly 5% drop in the following month. Investors are advised to be cautious as this pattern suggests a potentially tough January for Apple shares. The stock's recent performance marks the first time in 14 years and very rarely in the company's 44-year public history that it has had such a strong, sustained run.

title: Western Digital Corporation (WDC): Among the Cheap Technology Stocks to Buy According to Analysts
summary: Western Digital Corporation (WDC) is highlighted as a cheap technology stock to buy, driven by analyst expectations of significant revenue growth fueled by increasing data storage demand, AI, and autonomous driving advancements. Wells Fargo reiterated a Buy rating with an $85 price target, projecting a total addressable market growth from $65 billion in 2024 to $100 billion by 2030. The company's HDD business is thriving, with record shipments and gross margins, and some hedge funds believe its earnings potential is underestimated due to AI growth and attractive valuation.

title: 2 High-Powered ETFs That Have Doubled in 5 Years. Here's Why It's Not Too Late to Invest.
summary: This article highlights two growth-focused ETFs, the iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF) and the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap Growth ETF (SCHG), both of which have doubled in value over the past five years. It explains why these ETFs, concentrating on large and mid-cap growth stocks, particularly in tech and consumer discretionary sectors, remain attractive investments. The article suggests that despite past gains, continued growth in areas like AI and strong underlying companies make them suitable for long-term investors.

title: JEPQ vs JEPI: Are these boomer candy ETFs good buys in 2025?
summary: The article compares the JPMorgan Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPI) and the JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF (JEPQ), which are known for their high dividend yields. While these ETFs offer attractive monthly income, their total returns have historically underperformed passive S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 index funds. The author suggests that for long-term investors, the vanilla S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indices remain superior due to their better historical total returns despite lower yields.

title: Forrester’s Digest: Globalstar’s plans emerge
summary: Globalstar has ordered 50 satellites from MDA Space, costing C$1.1 billion, for its new 48-satellite Low Earth orbiting C-3 System, largely funded by Apple. This system, complementing its Gen3 satellites, will serve the U.S. market with enhanced signal technology and will include about 90 new Earth station antennas globally. Separately, the French regulatory authority has filed with the ITU for an 8,834-satellite system, AST-NG-C-4, which includes Globalstar's frequencies and is linked to CCC Communication Holdings France, an entity managed by an Apple Inc. director.

title: Want to be a millionaire by 2040? Here's how $200K today could grow to $1 million tomorrow with this BlackRock Tech ETF
summary: BlackRock's iShares Expanded Tech Sector ETF (ETN) offers a potential path to turn $200,000 into $1 million by 2040, primarily by investing in major technology companies with strong AI exposure like Meta, Apple, Nvidia, and Microsoft. The ETF has shown impressive historical returns, averaging 11% yearly since 2001 and 20.3% over the last decade, far outpacing the S&P 500. However, investors are advised to diversify their portfolios due to potential risks if AI growth fails to meet expectations.

title: Between the Lines: Alabama Power invests in Black futures through its support of HBCUs
summary: Alabama Power and its parent company, Southern Company, actively support Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs) through various initiatives. This includes significant financial grants, partnerships for workforce development, and programs like the AABE (American Association of Blacks in Energy) HBCU Energy Hackathon. The company's efforts aim to enhance educational opportunities, foster economic development, and develop future Black leaders.

title: Roundhill Launches 5 Leveraged Weekly Income ETFs
summary: Roundhill Investments has launched five new WeeklyPay ETFs (NVW, TSW, AAPW, COIW, PLTW) on the Cboe Exchange, offering weekly distributions and leveraged exposure to specific single stocks like Nvidia, Tesla, and Apple. These actively managed funds aim to generate 1.2 times the weekly returns of their underlying companies, providing income-focused investors with amplified returns and potential tax advantages through return of capital distributions. This innovation expands Roundhill's portfolio of weekly income strategies, with plans for additional leveraged WeeklyPay ETFs covering other major companies.

title: Clean200 list shows sustainable companies on path to dominate global economy
summary: The 2025 Clean200 list, compiled by As You Sow and Corporate Knights, highlights 200 publicly traded companies leading the sustainable clean energy economy. Despite political attacks, these companies, including Apple, Microsoft, and Tesla, generated $2.5 trillion in sustainable revenue and outperformed fossil fuel stocks and the global benchmark MSCI ACWI since 2016. The report emphasizes that the clean energy economy is demonstrating robust growth and is on track to dominate the global economy.

title: Learning curves: The future of pilot training with AI, flight simulators and digital training
summary: The article discusses the evolution of pilot training due to the global pilot shortage, emphasizing the integration of AI, flight simulators, and digital training methods. It highlights how training providers like BAA Training, Skyborne Academy, and CAE are adopting hybrid approaches, VR, AR, and data analytics to enhance learning efficiency, maintain safety standards, and address the growing demand for new pilots while ensuring competency in both automated and manual flight skills. Regulators like the UK CAA and FAA are closely monitoring these advancements to ensure high standards are maintained.

title: Chip design software maker Cadence revenue skyrockets with AI demand
summary: Cadence Design Systems reported strong Q4 2024 revenues, up 27% to $1.36 billion, driven by surging demand for AI-driven chip design software and expanded partnerships with major chipmakers like Nvidia and Intel. Despite robust growth and a 2024 fiscal year revenue of $4.6 billion, the company's 2025 financial outlook fell below Wall Street expectations due to uncertainties in its China business amid evolving trade restrictions. Cadence expects flat growth in China for 2025 and plans to be prudent with its guidance given the market's unpredictability.

title: Apple to invest more than $500B in US manufacturing
summary: Apple Inc. announced a significant investment of over $500 billion into U.S. manufacturing over the next four years, aiming to boost domestic job creation and manufacturing capabilities. This commitment includes opening a new server facility in Houston by 2026 for its AI systems, doubling its U.S. Advanced Manufacturing Fund to $10 billion, and establishing a training academy in Detroit. The move, seen by some as a response to potential tariffs and a win for the current administration, will also expand Apple's data center capacity and support silicon engineering.

title: Apple’s $500B US expansion includes chips from TSMC Phoenix
summary: Apple pledged $500 billion for US expansion, including sourcing chips from TSMC's Phoenix factory, where mass production began recently. This investment is set to create 20,000 new US jobs and expand manufacturing and data centers across several states. Former President Trump and the Biden administration both claim credit for stimulating domestic chip production, with Trump citing tariffs and Biden highlighting the CHIPS and Science Act.

title: Apple Plans Houston Plant to Produce AI Servers
summary: Apple Inc. plans to establish a new 250,000-sq-ft manufacturing facility in Houston, Texas, to produce servers for its Apple Intelligence and Private Cloud Compute AI services, with an expected opening next year. This initiative is part of a larger $500 billion investment in the U.S. over four years, including expanding data centers and doubling its "Advanced Manufacturing Fund" to $10 billion. The move comes as companies face new tariffs on goods imported from China and indicates a shift towards more U.S.-based manufacturing, potentially with Foxconn identified as the manufacturer for the Houston plant.

title: Apple commits to 'largest ever' $500bn US investment
summary: Apple announced a multi-year plan to invest over $500 billion in the US and create 20,000 new jobs, with a focus on research, development, software, and AI. This commitment includes building a new manufacturing factory in Texas to support its AI system, Apple Intelligence, and expanding data centers across several states. The announcement comes after Apple CEO Tim Cook met with President Donald Trump, who has consistently pushed for increased domestic manufacturing.

title: 98% of John Deere & Apple Shareholders Reject Anti-Diversity Proposals
summary: Shareholders of Deere & Co (NYSE:DE) and Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) overwhelmingly rejected shareholder proposals aimed at undermining diversity and inclusion programs, with votes against reaching over 98% at both companies. This follows a similar rejection at Costco, indicating a strong shareholder belief that diversity and inclusion are material to profitable growth and financial outperformance. Andrew Behar, CEO of As You Sow, stated that these votes signal a rejection of attempts to inject political culture wars into standard business practices, reinforcing the business rationale for diverse workforces.

title: How Apple’s iconic products reshaped everyday life: A look back at decades of innovation
summary: This article chronicles how Apple's key product releases, from the Apple II to AirPods, have profoundly changed daily life over several decades. It highlights how Apple consistently redefined technology markets, even when not being the first to introduce a product. The piece emphasizes the company's strong brand loyalty and its focus on both design and functionality to revolutionize user experience.

title: Apple's new Houston facility could start a renaissance for electronics companies
summary: Apple's decision to build a new AI server manufacturing facility in Houston could signal a resurgence for the region's electronics manufacturing sector, which previously thrived with companies like Texas Instruments and Compaq. The facility, part of a $500 billion investment in U.S. technology, will assemble servers crucial for Apple Intelligence, and is expected to bring thousands of jobs. This move, facilitated by Foxconn, may also help Apple navigate potential tariffs on foreign imports and aims to bring overseas manufacturing back to the U.S.

title: The Fintiv Pendulum Swings Again: More Discretionary Denials Coming Soon
summary: Acting USPTO Director Coke Stewart has rescinded the 2022 Vidal memorandum, which had limited discretionary denials of PTAB post-grant proceedings. This policy shift is expected to lead to a significant increase in discretionary denials of IPR institution petitions, returning greater discretion to the PTAB in cases with parallel district court litigation. The move emphasizes reliance on established PTAB precedent, particularly decisions in Apple Inc. v. Fintiv, Inc. and Sotera Wireless, Inc. v. Masimo Corp., and suggests a notable change in the landscape for patent challengers.

title: Milberg Lawsuit Accuses Meta of Tracking Users Without Consent
summary: Milberg has filed a class action lawsuit against Meta, alleging that the social media giant bypasses user privacy settings by directing them to Meta's in-app browser to track their online activity. The lawsuit claims Meta uses JavaScript injection to monitor user interactions on third-party websites without consent, despite users' privacy preferences. This legal action seeks compensatory damages and injunctive relief for a national class, a California subclass, and an iOS subclass of Meta users.

title: QQQX: 8%-Plus Yield, 9% Discount, Long-Term NAV Growth (NASDAQ:QQQX)
summary: QQQX is a closed-end fund that generates an 8%+ yield by selling covered calls on NASDAQ 100 stocks, aiming for attractive returns with reduced volatility. The fund boasts a 35% NAV/share increase since inception and currently trades at a 9.11% discount to NAV, providing regular quarterly distributions with potential tax advantages. Despite risks like price volatility and call option risks, QQQX is considered a speculative Buy for investors seeking high yield.

title: Will Apple's $500 Billion U.S. Manufacturing Investment Make a Difference for American Workers?
summary: Apple announced a $500 billion investment over four years to expand U.S. facilities, aiming to create 20,000 American jobs, including a new advanced manufacturing facility in Houston for AI servers. However, critics, like the Alliance for American Manufacturing President Scott Paul, suggest this investment, while substantial, is relatively minor for a company of Apple's size and doesn't address the core issue of offshoring iPhone production. The article contends that Apple's latest announcement is consistent with past patterns of making similar U.S. investment claims without significantly altering its manufacturing footprint for its primary products.

title: Apple defeats US consumer lawsuit over iCloud storage, for now
summary: Apple has successfully dismissed a consumer lawsuit alleging monopolization of the digital storage market through its iCloud service, though the plaintiffs have been granted leave to amend and refile their complaint. U.S. District Judge Eumi Lee ruled that the plaintiffs failed to show Apple violated antitrust law or held monopoly power, and that consumers are not obligated to purchase iCloud storage. The lawsuit, filed in March 2024, sought to represent millions of consumers paying for iCloud storage, arguing Apple coerced users into using its service for core data backups.

title: Apple introduces iPad Air with powerful M3 chip and new Magic Keyboard
summary: Apple has unveiled a new iPad Air featuring the powerful M3 chip, delivering significant performance enhancements and Apple Intelligence integration. Available in 11-inch and 13-inch models, the device also introduces a redesigned Magic Keyboard and supports Apple Pencil Pro, targeting content creators, students, and travelers. Alongside the new iPad Air, Apple updated its standard iPad with the A16 chip and doubled its starting storage, while also highlighting the environmental considerations in both products.

title: Federal Circuit Affirms Partial Win for Patent Owner Against Apple
summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals (CAFC) has upheld a mixed ruling from the Patent Trial and Appeal Board (PTAB) concerning Gesture Technology's patent on camera sensing technology. While some claims of the patent were deemed unpatentable, others were not, leading to an appeal by Apple and a cross-appeal by Gesture. The CAFC rejected Apple's arguments regarding the legal standard for obviousness and Gestures's estoppel claim, ultimately affirming the PTAB's decision.

title: Apple unveils new Mac Studio, the most powerful Mac ever, featuring M4 Max and new M3 Ultra
summary: Apple has announced the new Mac Studio, powered by the M4 Max and the new M3 Ultra chips, making it the most powerful Mac ever created. This professional desktop offers groundbreaking performance with extensive connectivity, including Thunderbolt 5, and supports up to 512GB of unified memory and 16TB of SSD storage. It is designed to handle intensive AI workloads and is built for Apple Intelligence, with availability starting March 12th.

title: Apple Debuts New MacBook Air, Mac Studio With AI-Ready Chips
summary: Apple Inc. has launched updated MacBook Air laptops and Mac Studio desktops featuring new AI-focused M4 processors. The MacBook Air models come in 13-inch and 15-inch sizes, start at lower prices, and include improved cameras and multi-monitor support. These updates aim to sustain the recent sales resurgence for the company's computer line.

title: Apple And The UK Government Broke A “Trust Promise”
summary: Apple has ceased offering its Advanced Data Protection (ADP) feature in the UK, a decision prompted by the UK government's request for access to user data. This move is seen as a breach of trust by Apple, which traditionally champions user privacy, and raises concerns about global repercussions for data privacy. The article emphasizes that this decision could further erode already weak public trust in the UK government, potentially hindering its AI implementation strategies.

title: Apple’s use of SiTime in iPhone 16e fuels stock rally for chipmaker
summary: The inclusion of SiTime components in Apple's new iPhone 16e model has led to a 20% stock rally for the lesser-known chipmaker. SiTime, which produces silicon-based clock components, is seeing this as a significant market opportunity, with Apple reportedly accounting for 22% of its revenue. Apple's newest phone also features its own custom modem chip, the C1, moving away from Qualcomm components.

title: Apple defeats AliveCor bid to block US smartwatch imports in US appeal
summary: A U.S. appeals court upheld Apple's win against medical device maker AliveCor in a patent dispute, preventing a potential import ban on Apple Watches. The court affirmed the invalidity of AliveCor's heart-rate monitoring patents that Apple was accused of infringing. This decision negates a U.S. trade tribunal's earlier ruling that Apple had violated AliveCor's rights, though AliveCor plans to explore further legal options.

title: News | Tech CEO follows his customers in relocating to South Florida
summary: Varonis, a technology company, is relocating its headquarters from New York to Miami, following a trend of businesses moving to South Florida. Co-founder and CEO Yaki Faitelson cited Miami's business-friendly environment, quality of life, and ability to attract talent and clients as key factors. The move reflects Miami's growing appeal as a hub for finance, tech, and professional services, though the local office market is currently experiencing a moderation after a pandemic-era boom.

title: Coinbase to Debut Combined Equities & Crypto Index Futures
summary: Coinbase Derivatives is set to launch Mag7 + Crypto Equity Index Futures on September 22, offering the first US-listed derivative that provides combined exposure to traditional equities and cryptocurrency ETFs. This innovative product aims to provide thematic exposure to innovation and growth assets, diversification, and strategic risk management. The index will include the "Magnificent 7" stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA, META, TSLA), Coinbase Global Inc. (COIN), and cryptocurrency ETFs (IBIT, ETHA), with an even-weighting methodology and quarterly rebalancing.

title: Movano's Evie Ring Adds New Personalization and Trend Tracking to Support Women's Wellness Journey
summary: Movano Health has updated its Evie Ring with new personalization and trend tracking features to enhance women's wellness journeys. These updates include integration with Apple Health, detailed 7- and 30-day trend graphs for various health metrics, and expanded workout tracking capabilities. The Evie Ring, priced at $269 with no subscription fees, aims to provide comprehensive and personalized health insights.

title: Timur Turlov: The Future of the Market and How Entrepreneurs Can Stay One Step Ahead
summary: Timur Turlov, CEO of Freedom Holding Corp., shares his insights on navigating the future market as an entrepreneur. He emphasizes adaptability, investing in technology and AI, viewing money as a tool, building trust over marketing, self-education, and balancing family and business. Turlov's company has seen significant growth, achieving $655.2 million in revenue and a market capitalization exceeding $9.5 billion.

title: Green Dot considers sale as CEO exits
summary: Green Dot, an embedded finance company, is exploring "potential strategic alternatives," including a possible sale, after posting a $26.7 million loss last year. The company's CEO, George Gresham, has also exited his role and been replaced by interim CEO William Jacobs. This upheaval follows Green Dot grappling with shrinking profit margins and a $44 million fine from the Federal Reserve.

title: Intuit QuickBooks Enables Tap to Pay on iPhone for Small and Mid-Market Businesses to Accept Contactless Payments
summary: Intuit Inc. announced the availability of Tap to Pay on iPhone for its QuickBooks Online customers in the U.S., allowing small and mid-market businesses to accept contactless payments using just an iPhone, without additional hardware. This feature integrates directly with QuickBooks Online, streamlining financial management and accelerating cash flow for merchants. The company highlights that this solution addresses common challenges faced by small businesses, such as cash flow issues and the need for flexible payment options, while also offering lower processing fees and secure transactions.

title: Sonos has reportedly dropped one of its worst ideas
summary: Sonos has reportedly canceled its plans for a streaming video player codenamed "Pinewood," a product that many observers believed was a disastrous idea for the company. This decision comes after a challenging period for Sonos, including a botched flagship app update and the launch of the indifferently received Ace headphones. By dropping the streaming player, Sonos avoids entering a saturated market with a potentially overpriced device and can instead refocus on its core multi-room audio business.

title: Former 2U and Capital One executive launches AI startup to help companies make better sense of data
summary: Harsha Mokkarala, a former executive at 2U and Capital One, has launched E:cue, an AI startup aimed at helping companies better utilize their data. Based in Rockville, the young company has secured backing from the D.C. investment firm Ardent Venture Partners. The article highlights other related startup news in the DMV area, including investments in other local tech companies.

title: Over 3 in 4 Canadian employers continue to struggle to hire talent: Report
summary: A new report by ManpowerGroup indicates that 77% of Canadian employers face significant challenges in hiring skilled talent, a slight improvement from the previous year, but still above the global average. This talent shortage is widespread across various industries, employee numbers, and regions within Canada, with provinces like Quebec and Ontario showing high percentages. Companies are addressing this by increasing wages, upskilling current employees, and targeting new talent pools, while experts suggest further measures like promoting equal opportunities and continuous training.

title: 3 Undervalued Wide-Moat Stocks With Unbreakable Competitive Advantages
summary: This article identifies three undervalued wide-moat stocks: MSCI, CSX Corporation, and Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM). Each company benefits from strong competitive advantages and high barriers to entry in their respective industries, leading to sustainable profitability. Analysts see significant upside potential for all three, with TSM offering the highest projected growth.

title: Tariff Implications For Investors: Building A Resilient Portfolio Amid Pullbacks
summary: J.P. Morgan's Global Investment Strategy Team discusses the implications of recent tariff announcements on the markets, noting the S&P 500's first 10% correction in two years. They argue that the current market pullback is a valuation adjustment rather than a fundamental economic issue, citing resilient "hard data" and historical market behavior after growth scares. The article advises investors to build resilient portfolios through diversification, income-generating assets like infrastructure, and inflation hedges such as gold.

title: Movano Evie Ring adds new data tracking features
summary: Movano Health has introduced new data visualization features to the Evie Ring app, focusing on women's health metrics and improving user experience. These updates include Apple Health integration, menstrual cycle tracking, and comprehensive 7-day and 30-day graphs for various health indicators. The app also now allows users to log past workouts even if the ring was not worn, enhancing the device's practicality and utility.

title: Is Wesco International Inc. (WCC) the Best Stock to Buy According to Seth Klarman?
summary: The article discusses Wesco International Inc. (WCC) as a stock held by Seth Klarman's Baupost Group, which has refocused its strategy after a period of underperformance. Klarman's firm generated a 10% return in 2024 and increased its credit investments. Wesco International (WCC) is highlighted as an industrial company benefiting from growth trends, especially in data centers, and delivered strong Q4 2024 results.

title: When Facebook Ads Won’t Help Performance Advertisers
summary: This article discusses when Facebook ads are beneficial for performance advertisers and when they may not be. While Facebook offers extensive targeting and can drive traffic, marketers should be aware of diminishing returns due to factors like inappropriate budgets, ad frequency fatigue, rising CPA, and audience demographics. The piece emphasizes the importance of a diversified media mix and exploring alternative advertising channels for sustainable growth.

title: Deadline Alert: Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (SWKS) Investors Who Lost Money Urged To Contact Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP About Securities Fraud Lawsuit
summary: Glancy Prongay & Murray LLP is reminding investors of Skyworks Solutions, Inc. (NASDAQ: SWKS) who suffered losses to contact them regarding a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Skyworks made misleading statements about its business operations and prospects, particularly concerning its relationship with Apple and its ability to capitalize on AI. Investors who purchased Skyworks securities between July 30, 2024, and February 5, 2025, have until May 5, 2025, to file a lead plaintiff motion.

title: Cruise Operators Navigate Customers’ Insatiable Demand for Bandwidth
summary: Cruise ship operators are facing an insatiable demand for bandwidth from passengers, driven by high-resolution smartphone cameras and a cloud-based "always on" culture. While network management tools prioritize premium users, providers like Starlink are seen as crucial for meeting the immense connectivity needs, despite some market competition and a lack of conventional enterprise-grade agreements. The evolving expectations of passengers necessitate continuous retrofitting of vessels and complex network management to ensure a satisfactory user experience, which is paramount for the industry.

title: Apple loses German antitrust fight, faces greater scrutiny
summary: Apple lost its challenge in Germany's top civil court against its classification as a significant market power, a designation that grants antitrust regulators more authority to scrutinize its business practices. The Federal Court of Justice upheld the German cartel office's 2023 ruling, placing Apple alongside Google (Alphabet) and Meta as tech giants subject to measures curbing dominance. This decision strengthens the German antitrust watchdog's position, especially concerning Apple's App Store and tracking regulations, though the EU's Digital Markets Act remains the primary standard for Big Tech.

title: Denny’s debuts Nvidia Breakfast Bytes
summary: Denny's has launched a new limited-time menu item called "Nvidia Breakfast Bytes" to honor Nvidia founder Jensen Huang, who reportedly conceived the idea for Nvidia at a Denny's diner booth. The dish features sausages wrapped in pancakes with maple syrup, reflecting Huang's preferred way of eating it. This launch coincides with Yum Brands Inc. also announcing a partnership with Nvidia to integrate AI technologies across its restaurants.

title: S&P 500 Sell-Off: Here's Why This ETF May Be the Smartest Way to Invest in the S&P 500 Right Now
summary: The S&P 500 has experienced a sell-off, prompting investors to consider alternative ways to gain exposure to the index. This article suggests the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF (RSP) as a smart investment option, highlighting its diversified sector allocation compared to the traditional market-cap weighted S&P 500, which is heavily concentrated in tech. While the standard S&P 500 has outperformed in the last decade, the equal-weight ETF has shown stronger long-term performance since its inception and during market recoveries not driven by specific sectors.

title: Here Is Why Yield Hungry Investors Should Buy Schwab's SCHD Over The Safe Vanguard VIG
summary: This article compares two popular dividend ETFs, Schwab's SCHD and Vanguard's VIG, for yield-hungry investors. While both offer multi-sector exposure and low expense ratios, SCHD is highlighted as the better choice due to its higher trading volume, more balanced sector diversification, and significantly higher dividend yield. Although VIG focuses on dividend growers, SCHD provides a better overall package for substantial dividend distributions.

title: 4 tech giants look cheap ahead of earnings
summary: Ahead of their earnings reports, four major tech companies—Apple, Amazon, Facebook, and Microsoft—are considered undervalued. Analysts see Apple as undervalued despite recent revenue guidance adjustments, Amazon as continuing its impressive growth, Microsoft showing broad-based strength, and Facebook maintaining its competitive advantages in digital advertising despite recent controversies.

title: Trump tally: J&J plans $55 billion investment to expand U.S. manufacturing
summary: Johnson & Johnson announced a $55 billion investment over the next four years to expand U.S. manufacturing, research, and technology. This move aligns with President Trump's goal of boosting domestic manufacturing and will include new facilities and expansions to existing sites. J&J's investment is part of a larger trend of major U.S. companies pledging significant spending to expand U.S. operations.

title: SCHG Vs. VUG: Both Are An Equally Great Way To Capture The Tech Rebound
summary: The article discusses how SCHG and VUG are strong ETF options for gaining exposure to the tech sector, especially given the expected growth in AI spending. While both are considered excellent for long-term investors bullish on technology, SCHG has historically shown a slight edge over VUG due to its top holdings. Both ETFs are characterized by low expense ratios and strong dividend growth, making them suitable for aligning portfolios with future tech advancements and higher valuations.

title: Worried About the Markets? This Tech ETF Is Up Over 25% This Year and Could Still Go Higher.
summary: The Invesco China Technology ETF (CQQQ) has surged over 25% this year, outperforming the S&P 500 and the broader tech sector, driven by advancements in Chinese AI. Despite recent market downturns and concerns over tech valuations, this ETF offers exposure to undervalued Chinese tech companies with significant growth potential, especially in the AI sector. With a forward price-to-earnings multiple of just 19, it presents a compelling investment opportunity.

title: Digging into Berkshire Hathaway’s portfolio
summary: This article analyzes Berkshire Hathaway's equity portfolio using Morningstar's Global Risk Model, uncovering its concentrated and evolving nature. Despite Buffett's historical aversion to tech, the portfolio is now notably technology-focused, primarily due to its significant Apple holding. The analysis highlights how Berkshire's investment strategy, though idiosyncratic, shows unique characteristics in liquidity, momentum, moat, value-growth, and volatility compared to the S&P 500.

title: HIDIVE Expands Service to Amazon Prime Video in U.K., Canada, Australia, New Zealand
summary: AMC Networks announced that its HIDIVE anime streaming service is now available as an ad-free add-on subscription on Amazon Prime Video Channels in the U.K., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. The direct-to-consumer app is also available in these territories. Additionally, HIDIVE has expanded its availability in the United States through partnerships with Samsung, LG, and VIZIO, and updated its website and apps in March 2024 with new features.

title: BCX: Examining Inflation Protection Benefits And Recession Risks
summary: The BlackRock Resources & Commodities Strategy Trust (BCX) is a closed-end fund offering commodities exposure and a high income yield, making it attractive for inflation protection. The fund has historically performed well during inflationary periods, outperforming the S&P 500 year-to-date, and trades at a compelling discount to NAV with a significant yield and monthly distributions. While distributions have been fully covered year-to-date, they were not fully covered for the full year 2024.

title: Why Nano Dimension Stock Crashed on Tuesday
summary: Nano Dimension stock dropped 16.5% after a Delaware court ruled in favor of Desktop Metal, forcing Nano Dimension to go through with its acquisition of Desktop Metal. Nano Dimension had attempted to back out of the $183 million deal announced nine months prior, leading to legal action from Desktop Metal. The ruling mandates Nano Dimension to execute a national security agreement with CFIUS and close the purchase by March 31, or extend the deadline.

title: Shopify Takes Steps To Pull In Billions From Passive Funds
summary: Shopify is moving its US-listed shares from the New York Stock Exchange to the Nasdaq Global Select Market, starting March 31. This strategic move could allow the e-commerce company to be included in major stock indexes like the Nasdaq 100, attracting significant investment from passively managed funds. While inclusion can increase buying power, it also ties stock performance more closely to the broader market, making it a "two-way street" according to analysts.

title: ADX: Tech Tilt Exposure With Telling Income And Limited Price Volatility (NYSE:ADX)
summary: The Adams Diversified Equity Fund (ADX) offers investors exposure to large-cap and tech stocks with an 8% of NAV annual distribution yield, blending capital appreciation with consistent income. Its diversified portfolio includes major tech holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and NVIDIA, alongside other sectors. Despite consistently trading at a discount-to-NAV, ADX demonstrates a superior 10-year Sharpe Ratio and lower volatility compared to SPY, making it an attractive option for tech and income-focused investors.

title: The Smartest Trillion-Dollar Stock to Buy With $1,000 and Hold Forever
summary: The article identifies Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRK.A, NYSE: BRK.B) as the smartest trillion-dollar stock to buy and hold forever, comparing its diversification and leadership advantage under Warren Buffett to an index fund. It highlights Buffett's track record of outperforming the S&P 500 and stresses that the company is well-positioned for continued success even after his tenure due to its strong culture and chosen successor.

title: Gravity Officially Launches Mobile Version of Roguelike Deck-Building Adventure Game 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse' Globally
summary: Gravity has officially launched the mobile version of its roguelike deck-building adventure game, 'Shambles: Sons of the Apocalypse,' globally. The game, which combines text adventure, card deck-building, and roguelike genres, is set in a post-apocalyptic world where players' choices significantly impact the storyline and gameplay. Available for USD 6.99 on Google Play and Apple App Store, it also offers seven DLC packs for expanded content.

title: What is the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV)?
summary: The article discusses the dividend yield of the iShares S&P 500 ETF (IVV), noting its excellent capital returns over the past five years driven by US tech giants, despite a relatively low dividend yield of 1.01% as of February 2025. It emphasizes that while IVV isn't ideal for large investment income, its overall net return and low management fee make it attractive for long-term growth due to its exposure to strong US businesses benefiting from trends like digitalization and AI.

title: Rivian creates new company aimed at small, lightweight vehicles
summary: Rivian has launched a new company called Also, Inc., which will focus on small, lightweight electric vehicles, including a possible e-bike, to address global mobility challenges. Rivian will maintain a minority ownership, and the new venture has secured $105 million in investment from Eclipse Ventures. Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe will chair Also's Board of Directors, which is staffed by a team of experienced professionals from various tech and automotive companies.

title: The Annoying Bug Making Apple Users Very Angry
summary: Apple users are frustrated with a persistent and unclosable notification bug related to the "Reactions" feature in macOS. The notification informs users that Reactions have been turned off by their browser, but its "x" button is non-functional, leaving the annoying message on screen. This issue highlights Apple's oversight in implementing a minor feature notification without a proper dismiss function, causing significant irritation among its user base.

title: Better Comeback Stock: BlackBerry vs. Unity Software
summary: This article compares BlackBerry (BB) and Unity Software (U), two former high-growth tech stocks that have experienced significant declines. It analyzes their past performances, current business strategies, and future outlooks, concluding that Unity Software, despite its challenges, has a better chance of a comeback due to its stabilizing growth and AI-driven advertising platform, whereas BlackBerry's divestments and declining revenue make its future less certain.

title: A few minutes with Jeff Bush, GM executive director of digital vehicle experience
summary: Jeff Bush, GM's executive director of digital vehicle experience, shares insights from his career journey from Silicon Valley startups like Apple and Google to the automotive world. He discusses GM's commitment to electrification, his vision for over-the-air updates improving user experience, and the company's efforts to modernize its software and hardware stack. Bush emphasizes that GM offers engineers exciting challenges in shaping the future of mobility through safe, reliable, and enjoyable products.

title: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) Announces the Appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering
summary: CXApp Inc. (Nasdaq:CXAI) has announced the appointment of Terry Blanchard as Senior Vice President of Engineering. Blanchard, with over 30 years of engineering leadership experience from companies like Apple, WhatsApp, and NVIDIA, will lead CXAI's global engineering organization and drive the development of next-generation AI technologies for workplace experiences. This strategic hire aims to accelerate CXAI's product roadmap and technical strategy, focusing on creating advanced Agentic AI solutions.

title: QQQI ETF Holdings List — NASDAQ:QQQI
summary: This article provides the holdings list for the NEOS Nasdaq 100 High Income ETF (QQQI) as of February 26, 2026. Key holdings include NVIDIA, Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. The list details the weight percentage, number of shares, and market value for each company held by the ETF.

title: Berkshire Hathaway Gains Defy Stock Market Slump
summary: Berkshire Hathaway's stock has shown notable resilience and gains in early 2025 while the broader equity market faced a slump. This performance is attributed to its diversified business structure, strong operational earnings from its various segments, and Warren Buffett's disciplined investment philosophy. The company's stability and value-oriented approach are attracting investors seeking a safe harbor amidst market volatility.

title: Apple to Invest Billions in U.S. Manufacturing
summary: Apple Inc. plans to invest over $500 billion in the U.S. within the next four years, focusing on boosting American manufacturing, research and development, and job creation. This initiative includes building a new factory in Houston for server manufacturing, doubling its U.S. Advanced Manufacturing Fund to $10 billion, and establishing a training academy in Detroit. These efforts aim to support American innovation, high-skilled jobs, and advanced technology fields like AI and silicon engineering.

title: Stocks Have Worst Day Since 2020 as Trump’s Tariffs Raise Risk of Recession
summary: US and global stock markets experienced their worst day since 2020 due to new tariffs unveiled by President Trump, which are expected to harm economic growth and increase inflation. Technology and energy sectors were hit hardest, while bonds rallied as investors sought safety in government debt amidst rising recession fears.
